title: High-Speed CMOS ADCs for 5G New Radio and AI-Edge Systems: Design Trends and Trade-Offs
authors: Norhamizah Idros, Jagadheswaran Rajendran, Selvakumar Mariappan, Asrulnizam Abd Manaf, Narendra Kumar, Arokia Nathan, Binboga Siddik Yarman
abstract: The rapid growth of wideband wireless communication and edge intelligence applications has driven stringent requirements for analog to digital converters (ADCs) that simultaneously achieve high sampling rates, robust dynamic performance, energy efficiency, and scalability across advanced CMOS technology nodes. This paper presents a comprehensive review of high-speed CMOS ADC architectures, including flash, advanced flash, successive approximation-register (SAR), pipelined, pipelined-SAR, and time-interleaved (TI) designs, with emphasis on their suitability for emerging 5G New Radio (NR) and artificial intelligence (AI)-edge systems. Recent state of art CMOS implementations are critically examined and benchmarked, and the fundamental trade-offs among sampling speed, resolution, power consumption, and silicon area are systematically analyzed across different architectures and technology nodes. Key architectural techniques and scaling trends enabling performance improvement are discussed, together with a comparative evaluation using Schreier Figure of Merit (FoMS). The analysis indicates that continued CMOS scaling and architectural innovation have enabled FoMS values exceeding 160 dB, meeting the demands of low-power, high speed, and high resolution data conversion in next-generation wireless and artificial intelligence (AI)-edge platforms.

title: S-Edge: A Multi-Region Edge Computing Framework with Adaptive Data Compression and Dynamic Load Balancing
authors: Emir Husain, Dev Patel, Vedang Kore, Manik Gupta, S Sharmiladevi
abstract: The growth of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has posed serious problems in terms of bandwidth usage, latency, and data integrity in centralised cloud systems. Edge computing can help resolve these problems by processing data closer to the source, but existing frameworks frequently lack built-in mechanisms for dynamic load distribution and adaptive data optimisation. This paper presents S-Edge, a multi-region edge computing framework that combines real-time anomaly detection, smart load balancing, and dynamic compression to maximise resource utilisation. A smart-switching compression layer is introduced that selects dynamically among ZLIB, BZ2, and LZMA based on data priority and compression efficiency threshold τ = 0.5, achieving significant storage reduction. The system employs a capacity-aware load balancing algorithm that redistributes traffic across regions when utilisation exceeds Lthresh = 1000 bytes (80% capacity), maintaining system stability. Anomaly detection using Isolation Forest achieves a precision, recall, and F1-score of 0.99 under controlled simulation conditions, representing an upper bound specific to the deliberately separated synthetic distributions used; robustness analysis under overlapping distributions yields F1 of 0.39–0.63 depending on overlap severity. Security is provided through AES-256-GCM encryption and SHA-256 integrity checks. The system achieves a primary storage recovery latency of under 1 ms, with asynchronous multi-region replication completing within approximately 153 ms. Experimental results confirm the applicability of the framework to scalable, resource-constrained IoT deployments.

title: Enhanced Traction Control on Irregular Roads through Preview-based Nonlinear Model Predictive Control
authors: Raffaele Manca, Pietro Stano, Mario Mihalkov, Luis M. Castellanos Molina, Umberto Montanaro, Andrea Tonoli, Aldo Sorniotti
abstract: Upcoming V2X technologies and on-board perception systems will provide real-time information from multiple sources, such as other vehicles and the surrounding environment, and thus enable the implementation of innovative active safety control functions. This paper investigates the benefit of the preview on the road irregularities on the tire slip control performance of a traction control system for electric vehicles. To this purpose, a nonlinear model predictive controller (NMPC) for traction control is presented, which concurrently uses the information on the tire-road friction factor and upcoming road profile. The simulation results across multiple road scenarios – including comparisons with state-of-the-art benchmarking controllers – highlight the significant improvements brought by the proposed preview-based NMPC, including its robustness with respect to model uncertainties and inaccuracies in the preview information. Preliminary proof-of-concept experiments on an electric vehicle prototype with an on-board powertrain demonstrate the real-time implementability of the algorithm, and confirm the potential of the proposed control solution.

title: Quantum Key Distribution For Optical Satellite Communications: An Engineering-Oriented Review
authors: A. Popescu, A.-M. Badescu
abstract: Satellite quantum key distribution (SatQKD) is widely regarded as a promising approach for extending quantum-secure key establishment beyond the distance limitations of terrestrial optical fiber links. However, practical assessment of SatQKD remains difficult because published results rely on heterogeneous assumptions regarding channel loss, orbital visibility, atmospheric conditions, detector behavior, finite-key analysis, and classical post-processing efficiency. As a result, comparisons across protocols, architectures, and mission concepts are often difficult to interpret from an operational engineering perspective. This paper presents an engineering-oriented review of SatQKD in the context of hybrid optical satellite communication systems. The review synthesizes prior results reported across protocol-level, link-budget, orbital, finite-key, and network-level studies. Within this scope, service-level notions such as pass-integrated and availability-limited key delivery are used as an interpretive lens for discussing practical relevance under orbital intermittency, atmospheric attenuation, receiver non-idealities, finite-key constraints, and classical post-processing limitations. The contribution of this review is a cross-layer engineering synthesis of prior SatQKD results reported under heterogeneous assumptions, aimed at supporting more consistent operational interpretation across protocols, architectures, and mission concepts.

title: Cognitive GenAI-as-a-Service Orchestration in Edge Networks: A Value-Adaptive Multi-Agent Learning Framework
authors: Yan Gao, Shaoyuan Huang, Minglai Shao, Huaming Wu, Yansha Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Deploying Generative AI-as-a-Service (GenAIaaS) at the network edge enables ubiquitous intelligence but faces severe stability challenges, primarily stemming from the lack of cognitive awareness regarding stochastic inference latency spikes and the coordination complexity of interdependent microservice chains. To bridge these gaps under strict Quality of Service (QoS) constraints, we propose value-adaptive multi-agent proximal policy optimization (VAMAPPO) as a novel cognitive orchestration framework. Unlike centralized approaches, VAMAPPO treats orchestration as a decentralized cognitive decision process. It integrates a variational inference-based uncertainty quantification mechanism, empowering agents to perceive environmental volatility and dynamically optimize learning steps to prevent performance degradation. To further solve the ambiguity in distinguishing individual contributions within sequential service chains, we design a coordinated advantage function (CAF). By utilizing counterfactual baselines to estimate marginal impacts, CAF effectively disentangles complex inter-agent dependencies. Extensive experiments on real-world network topologies with trace-driven workloads demonstrate that our cognitive framework significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. Specifically, VAMAPPO achieves up to a 97.0% service success rate and reduces response latency by 24.2% while satisfying stringent service level agreement (SLA) constraints, exhibiting superior robustness in zero-shot generalization across unseen edge environments. Code and data are available at https://github.com/gymorsiback/Value-Adaptive-Multi-Agent-PPO.

title: Enhanced Sensing in Large-Scale Array PMCW Systems by Range-Angle Decoupling
authors: Hwanhee Park, Shinyoung Park, Jingon Joung, Seongwook Lee, Jeong-Hoon Park
abstract: Recently, phase-modulated continuous-wave (PMCW) systems have been gaining attention as a next-generation waveform for autonomous driving systems due to their capability of integrated sensing and communication with high-resolution sensing ability. Because high-resolution PMCW radar systems require a wide array aperture and wide bandwidth, the severe range-angle coupling effect can degrade the sensing performance. To address this issue, we first formulate a signal model for PMCW radar system that accounts for range-angle coupling, also referred to as the spatial-wideband effect, and classify this effect into range migration and beam squint. Based on the formulation, our analysis shows that PMCW radar systems are more vulnerable to range-angle coupling than frequency-modulated continuous-wave radar systems, which highlights the importance of the proposed mitigation technique. To mitigate the issue, we propose a novel range-angle decoupling method for PMCW radar systems that uses a modified correlation process. The simulation results show that the proposed method improves both the peak power and the half-power beam width of the target angle response, even in scenarios with multiple targets. Furthermore, the computational complexity of the proposed method is relaxed by Fourier transform-based interpolation, which enables significantly faster computation with negligible performance degradation.

title: AC Power Flow-Based Demand Response for Multi-Energy Three-Phase Distribution Systems
authors: Shahin Shaikh, Md Asif Shaharear
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: This paper presents an unbalance-aware demand response (DR) framework for three-phase unbalanced distribution systems with integrated multi-energy flexibility, including electricity, heat, and gas loads. Voltage unbalance is explicitly incorporated into the optimization objective to achieve a trade-off between economic performance and network operation. A nonlinear optimization problem is formulated over a 24-hour horizon and solved using Sequential Quadratic Programming (SQP), with embedded three-phase AC power flow ensuring accurate network validation and constraint satisfaction. Simulation results on a modified IEEE 37-bus system demonstrate that cost-only DR achieves lower cost but results in higher voltage imbalance, whereas the proposed method reduces average voltage unbalance and improves voltage profiles with a moderate increase in cost. Additional analyses, including voltage constraint verification, power loss comparison, and sensitivity studies, further confirm the effectiveness and robustness of the approach.

title: FedGDD: Defending Federated Learning Against Targeted Model Poisoning Sybil Attacks Based on Gradient Drift Detection
authors: Qi Xiong, Hai Dong, Nasrin Sohrabi, Zahir Tari
abstract: Sybil attacks pose a significant threat to federated learning, as malicious nodes can collaborate to form a majority and overwhelm the system. Therefore, developing effective countermeasures is essential to ensure the security of federated learning systems. To address this challenge, we introduce a novel targeted model poisoning defence method for federated learning, named Gradient Drift Detection (FedGDD). Unlike existing approaches, such as clustering, statistical analysis, and re-training, which struggle in scenarios where malicious nodes constitute the majority, FedGDD reframes malicious detection as a gradient drift detection problem. This approach identifies potential attacks by detecting deviations in gradients, operating on the premise that the loss functions for benign and malicious nodes are inherently different. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate the efficacy of FedGDD compared to six well-established methods: Flame, FLTrust, FedCPA, Median, Krum, and FL-WBC. Using tasks from both image classification and natural language processing, the experiments confirm that FedGDD is robust and independent of specific application settings. Results show that FedGDD effectively safeguards federated learning systems across a wide range of malicious node ratios. Specifically, FedGDD maintains a low attack success rate for malicious nodes when their ratio ranges from 0.2 to 0.8. Additionally, it preserves high model accuracy when the malicious node ratio is between 0.2 and 0.5. These findings highlight FedGDD's potential to enhance the reliability and performance of Federated Learning systems.

title: A Bandwidth-Enhanced Metasurface-Based RF Energy Harvester With Wide-Angle Reception
authors: Xiangyan Liu, Ning Liu, Xianjun Sheng, Tianxi Feng, Hui Li, Lei Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents an efficiency enhancement strategy for the bandwidth-enhanced metasurface-based harvester operating at wide incidence angles. A current-constrained strategy, combined with a bandwidth-enhancement scheme, is first employed to achieve a high harvesting efficiency over a wide frequency range. Subsequently, by adjusting the positions of through-vias in adjacent metasurface units, the effective coupling at large incidence angles is enhanced, and the collected energy is driven toward the output ports. As a result, the harvesting efficiency at large angles is significantly improved, thus enabling wide incidence angle reception. To validate the proposed strategy, a bandwidth-enhanced metasurface with different through-via positions and a DC combining network consisting of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times 2$</tex-math> </inline-formula> rectifiers has been designed, fabricated, and measured. At normal incidence and an incident power density of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$16.4~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, the measured total harvesting efficiency (RF–DC) remains above 27.2% (TE) and 26.6% (TM) at 3.425–3.7 GHz, corresponding to a relative bandwidth (RBW) of 7.72%. At the maximum incidence angle of 75°, the peak total efficiency reaches 19.9% under TE polarization and 24.5% under TM polarization. The proposed harvester has high efficiency, bandwidth enhancement, and wide incidence angle reception characteristics, which are expected to provide supplementary power to wireless sensing nodes in the complex electromagnetic (EM) environment.

title: Point-RMAE: Reinforcement Masked Autoencoder for 3D Representation Learning_supp1-3694193.pdf
authors: Jihua Zhu
abstract: The Mainstream 3D masked point modeling representation learning community typically employs predefined, fixed-ratio random or block masking strategies, aiming to obtain optimal representations and achieve high downstream performance. However, these empirical designs overlook the significant geometric information and structural importance differences that are inherent among different 3D points, leading to a suboptimal trade-off between the representation capture capabilities and reconstruction difficulty of such masking strategies. To address this issue, we are the first to present this decision-making problem to a reinforcement learning agent and propose a Reinforcement Masked Autoencoder for 3D representation learning, named Point-RMAE. Guided by geometric features as state factor, this method leverages the Masking Strategy Analyzer and the Dynamic Masking Generator to adaptively decide and apply the masking strategy during pretraining. The Masking Ratio Scheduling module dynamically adjusts the masking ratio based on the optimal strategy. Subsequently, the analyzer is updated by multiscale rewards derived from reconstruction quality level, distribution-aware feedback, and policy exploration. Notably, to enrich the Reward Function with distribution-aware signals and avoid decision collapse issue, we propose a Flow Matching Point Cloud Fast Generator that guides the selected masking decisions. Our method achieves outstanding performance across downstream tasks such as shape classification, medical diagnosis, object detection, action recognition, denoising and multiscale scene segmentation on ten popular 3D and 4D datasets. More importantly, Point-RMAE pioneers the application of reinforcement learning in 3D self-supervised representation learning.

title: Global Localization of the Perseverance Rover via Orbiter–UAV–Rover Collaborative Matching
authors: Zilong Cao, Xiong Xu, Qipeng Chen, Changjiang Xiao, Chao Wang, Yongjiu Feng, Huan Xie, Xiaohua Tong
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: High-precision global localization of the rover is crucial to the success of in-situ exploration on Mars. However, the automatic global localization of Mars rover has long been a challenge in this field. During the Mars 2020 mission, the Ingenuity helicopter was successfully deployed on the surface of Mars. By providing high-resolution aerial images, it provides an innovative solution for the positioning of Mars rovers. This paper presents an orbiter–UAV–rover collaborative matching framework for the high-precision global localization of Mars rovers. By taking the UAV image as a bridge, this method effectively eliminates the scale difference and perspective deviation between the orbiter and the rover image. The experimental verification on several sites surveyed by the Perseverance Rover shows that the proposed framework can achieve an average localization accuracy of 0.3845 meters (less than 2 pixels on the reference map), showing its application potential in engineering practice.

title: Implicit Continuous Full-spectrum Response Network for Visible-to-Infrared Image Translation
authors: Qiaoying Qu, Bin Pan, Ruiying Li, Zhenwei Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The acquisition of infrared remote sensing imagery is often constrained by high sensor costs and complex atmospheric conditions, leading to a scarcity of infrared data. While data-driven visible-to-infrared image translation has demonstrated potential, existing methods typically neglect to merge sensor imaging mechanisms. To bridge this gap, a joint data-mechanism-driven visible-to-infrared translation framework, named ImconfuseNet, is proposed, which consists of an implicit full-spectrum reconstruction module and two spectral response imaging modules. The sensor imaging process intended for modelling comprises two fundamental steps: the continuous radiation enegry incident on the sensor, and the sensor responds to the enegry via a specific spectral response function. In order to model the radiation enegry, the implicit full-spectrum reconstruction module learns a spatial-spectral-wise continuous representation, which projects a visible image into a continuous full-spectrum image spanning visible and infrared wavelengths. Afterward, two spectral response imaging modules simulate the sensor-specific response process to generate the infrared (and visible) images. Moreover, we construct a similar imaging mechanism-driven infrared-to-visible reverse generator and adopt a cycle-consistent training scheme to enforce domain consistency. The major contribution of ImconfuseNet is that we realize visible-to-infrared image translation by incorporating the spectral response imaging process and integrating it into a data-driven neural network. Experimental results indicate that the proposed network outperforms several state-of-the-art methods. Our code will be available at https://github.com/qffang/ImconfuseNet.

title: Integrated UAV and Satellite Observations for Antarctic Melt Pond Monitoring: Assessing the Melt-induced Risk for Transportation on Landfast Ice_supp1-3695914.docx
authors: Lei Zheng
abstract: In recent years, the extent of Antarctic sea ice has frequently reached historically low levels. Melt ponds significantly affect the sea ice heat balance and mass balance while posing challenges to ice surface transportation. Melt ponds in the Arctic have been extensively monitored using satellite remote sensing. However, the Antarctic has received very little attention, and small-scale melt pond distribution patterns remain virtually unexplored. This study combines high-resolution Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) imagery with satellite remote sensing to investigate the characteristics of melt ponds on landfast ice near Zhongshan Station, East Antarctica. UAV-derived data reveal that the melt ponds in this region are small (median area = 0.34 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) and irregular in shape (mean roundness = 4.97). Validation against the UAV data suggests poor performance of the traditional melt pond fraction (MPF) retrieval model in the Antarctic region. Therefore, we develop an MPF retrieval algorithm by integrating UAV and Landsat 8 images. The correlation coefficient is as high as 0.82, substantially outperforming the traditional method. Dynamic MPF monitoring indicates that melt ponds concentrate near the coastline, especially to the west of bare rock areas, as the persistent summer easterly winds transport low-albedo dust westward. The integrated melt-induced risk assessment, combining MPF and Normalized Difference Water Index for ice, shows that high-risk areas are concentrated in Nella Fjord, west of the Mirror Peninsula. Ice surface transport should avoid this area and be completed before mid-November.

title: An Accelerated Vectorized Discrete Ordinate Method With Forward and Jacobian Modules Developed Toward Advanced Satellite Data Assimilation
authors: Yi-Ning Shi, Jun Yang, Fuzhong Weng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The application of vectorized radiative transfer (RT) models and their associated Jacobian computations is essential for satellite variational data assimilation, yet remains substantially more expensive than scalar RT. To address this limitation, this study extends the Accelerated Discrete Ordinate Method to a fully vectorized formulation, ADOM_V, together with its tangent-linear and adjoint modules, for efficient and accurate simulations of polarized RT and its corresponding Jacobians. In ADOM_V, the full vectorized discrete ordinate solver is applied only to scattering-dominant layers, while adjacent effectively non-scattering layers are analytically merged using absorption–emission theory. This hybrid treatment preserves the key vertical RT structure while reducing computational cost, and is applicable from visible to microwave wavelengths. Idealized experiments show that the Stokes vectors simulated by ADOM_V agree closely with reference solutions from the conventional vectorized discrete ordinate method, while tangent-linear and adjoint tests confirm the numerical consistency of the Jacobian module. ADOM_V achieves speedup factors of about 4–9 relative to the conventional vectorized solver, depending on the numbers of layers, streams, and Stokes components. Incorporated into ARMS version 1.2, ADOM_V is further applied to all-sky visible radiance simulations for AGRI/FY-4B. Compared with scalar ADOM simulations, the inclusion of polarization yields physically meaningful improvements, especially for water-cloud-sensitive channels, and reveals a pronounced dependence on scattering geometry. These results demonstrate that ADOM_V is a physically consistent, numerically accurate, and computationally efficient polarized RT solver, providing an important step toward the use of vectorized RT solvers in all-sky variational data assimilation, although further acceleration is still needed for operational implementation.

title: A High-Power Ku -Band PA Using Multi-Combiner Architecture
authors: Yiting Zhang, Nengxu Zhu, Fanyi Meng
abstract: This paper presents a Ku-band multi-combined power amplifier (PA) implemented in 130-nm CMOS SOI technology for LEO satellite communication phased arrays. To overcome CMOS integration challenges while attaining watt-level output, the design incorporates two key techniques: a triple-cascode topology and a low-loss, multi-stage power combiner. The triple-cascode structure utilizes RF-grounded symmetrical stacking to eliminate external gate capacitors, thereby enhancing voltage gain, output power, and robustness against input imbalance. The multi-stage combiner efficiently aggregates power from 32 unit cells by integrating four-way transformers with strip-line and transmission-line networks, achieving low insertion loss within a compact footprint. Experimental results show a 3-dB bandwidth of 10.5–14.5 GHz, a saturated output power (Psat) of 30.8 dBm (1.2 W), and a peak power-added efficiency (PAE) of 19.9%. The proposed PA achieves superior output power among state-of-the-art 8–18 GHz CMOS PAs, demonstrating its strong potential for next-generation space-borne transmitters.

title: Stochastic Computing Circuits for Nonlinear Functions Based on Multiplexers
authors: Yongqiang Zhang, Defang Meng, Jie Han, Shaowei Wang, Guangjun Xie, Xin Cheng
abstract: Stochastic computing (SC) is a renewing computing paradigm that differs from weighted binary ones. It operates on binary stochastic bitstreams, or called stochastic numbers (SNs), instead of binary numbers (BNs). This work proposes circuits based on multiplexers (MUXs) to address the issue of SC in computing nonlinear functions. These circuits significantly reduce the number of required stochastic number generators (SNGs) and shorten critical path delay (CPD), thereby lowering hardware costs. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed designs outperform existing designs in both computing accuracy and hardware overhead. For example, the proposed design for sin(x) using a 3rd-order Maclaurin series achieves averaged reductions of 66.84% in mean square error (MSE) and 24.62% in power-delay product (PDP), respectively, compared with previous designs.

title: Disk-Integrated Lunar Phase Curves from FY-4B/AGRI: Hapke Photometric Modeling from 470 nm to 2230 nm
authors: Wei Cai, Tianyi Xu, Yunzhao Wu, Jun Wang, Min Shu, Yong Zhang, Ling Sun, Zhiwei Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The photometric behavior of the lunar surface provides important constraints on regolith scattering processes and serves as a key tool for interpreting remote sensing observations and understanding the physical mechanisms responsible for the opposition effect. In this study, lunar observations acquired by the Advanced Geostationary Radiation Imager (AGRI) onboard the Fengyun-4B (FY-4B) geostationary meteorological satellite are processed to construct a photometric dataset spanning wavelengths from 470 nm to 2230 nm and phase angles from −133.7° to 122.7°. From this dataset, disk-integrated reflectance is derived, and phase curves in five spectral bands are constructed and modeled using the Hapke photometric formulation. The opposition surge amplitude exhibits weak wavelength dependence within the sampled range, suggesting that shadow-hiding effects dominate over coherent backscatter at disk-integrated scale. While most band ratios increase monotonically with phase angle, ratios involving the 2230 nm band exhibit a distinct arch-shaped dependence whose peak phase angle increases systematically with wavelength separation, indicating a transition in scattering regimes toward the shortwave infrared. Photometric correction tests demonstrate that the retrieved Hapke parameters provide effective reflectance normalization, with cross-phase reflectance stability better than ∼8%. These results demonstrate that geostationary disk-integrated observations provide robust constraints on lunar photometric properties and contribute to improved characterization of regolith scattering at global scales.

title: A Bad Data Identification Method Based on Optimal Weight-Tuning for Power System State Estimation
authors: Bowen Hu, Yadong Zhou, Sizhe He, Yujie Yang, Yang Liu, Ting Liu, Xiaohong Guan
abstract: The weighted least squares (WLS) method has been widely used in power systems for state estimation (SE), but in the presence of gross errors, the results of WLS SE can be significantly biased. While various bad data identification (BDI) methods exist, identifying multiple bad data for SE is still challenging, especially when bad data located at leverage points can disproportionately corrupt SE accuracy. This paper proposes an Optimal-Weighted Least Squares State Estimation (OWLS-SE) method with an optimal weight-tuning strategy to address these challenges. The impact of bad data at leverage points on estimation errors is first mathematically demonstrated through a theoretical upper bound of the SE relative error. Then, the optimal dynamic weights are developed to minimize this error bound by quantifying leverage point impacts and integrating them into a multi-objective optimization framework. The proposed OWLS-SE achieves simultaneous SE and BDI within a single optimization model, eliminating conventional two-stage processing. Numerical experiments on the 6- bus system, IEEE 30-, 118- bus systems, and the 2869- bus PEGASE system demonstrate that OWLS-SE achieves superior BDI and SE accuracy with robust performance even when multiple gross errors occur at leverage points, while maintaining computational efficiency comparable to the simplest WLS method.

title: Consistency-based Semi-supervised Evidential Active Learning Framework for Robust Classification of Radiology Images
authors: Shafa Balaram, Manh Cuong Nguyen, Yang Yu, Ivan Ho Mien, Huijuan Yang, Navya Sindhu Vanjavaka, Nurul Hafidzah Rahim, Alistair Giap Chuan Koh, Shaheen Low, Ashish Jith Sreejith Kumar, Feri Guretno, Yih Yian Sitoh, Ashraf Kassim, Pavitra Krishnaswamy
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Deep learning offers high performance for radiology image classification, but relies on large, expert annotated datasets. Semi-supervised learning and active learning approaches can leverage unlabelled samples and mitigate the annotation burden. Combining these techniques via semi-supervised active learning (SSAL) can compound their benefits, yet the effectiveness of this approach may be hindered by unreliable uncertainty estimation and consistency enforcement. To address these challenges, we propose Consistency-based Semi-supervised Evidential Active Learning (CSEAL). CSEAL is a principled SSAL framework leveraging evidential learning for reliable estimation of predictive uncertainty for consistency enforcement and prioritised sampling. First, we develop evidential counterparts of leading semi-supervised methods with different consistency enforcement mechanisms: Pseudo-labelling, Virtual Adversarial Training, Mean Teacher, and NoTeacher, and demonstrate customisability of CSEAL. Second, we introduce Noise Robust-evidential NoTeacher, an enhancement over evidential NoTeacher, that uses consensus principles and a small-loss inclusion mechanism to learn with noisyannotations. In extensive experiments on many X-ray, CT, and MRI datasets, we demon strate that CSEAL offers substantial performance gains over competitive SSAL baselines and enhances robustness in noisy annotation scenarios. Third, we translate CSEAL into an annotation platform and demonstrate its value as an aid for real-world radiology image annotation. Specifically, our CSEAL-assisted platform performs close to fully supervised learning with very small labelled datasets, en ables accurate auto-labelling, and maintains performance even when only noisy labels from junior annotators are available for training. Our work offers new opportunities to enhance the efficiency of clinical image annotation and model development workflows.

title: Physics-Informed Prostate MR–US Registration by Biomechanical Fields Prediction Network
authors: Shixing Ma, Zhaoxi Lin, Xinzhe Du, Yipeng Hu, Zhe Min
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Embedding biomechanical priors into medical image registration is essential to ensure physiologically credible soft-tissue motion. However, the optimal strategy for enforcing these priors within learning-based models re mains under-explored. In this work, we propose the Biome chanical Fields Prediction Physics-Informed Neural Net work (BFP-PINN) as a unified framework for prostate MR TRUS point-set registration. To systematically investigate the impact of different physics-embedding mechanisms, we instantiate and compare three distinct strategies within this backbone: (i) Deformation, which predicts displacement constrained by strong-form Navier–Cauchy residuals; (ii) Strain, which predicts both displacement and strain; and (iii) StressStrain, which jointly predicts displacement, stress, and strain. This controlled study isolates the effects of predicting intermediate physical fields versus direct reg ularization. Extensive experiments on simulated and clinical datasets show that the Deformation strategy provides the best overall balance between geometric accuracy and biomechanical plausibility in the evaluated setting. These findings suggest that while coupled-field predictions are theoretically rigorous, the direct residual constraint offers superior optimization stability for clinical registration tasks. Source codes are available at https://github.com/ Msx00/PINNs-for-Point-Set-Registration.git.

title: Two-Way Co-Design: Learnings from the Solid Lines project
authors: Nicola St John
publish: Proceedings of the 19th Participatory Design Conference 2026, Vol. 1: Full Papers

title: Optimization Model of Investment Benefit for Power Grid Enterprises Based on Improved Genetic Algorithm
authors: Weiyang You, Xiuna Wang, Gang Yu, Ling Wei, Lei Zhu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of single objective setting, low optimization accuracy and premature convergence of standard genetic algorithm (GA) in the investment decisionmaking of power grid enterprises, this paper proposes an investment benefit optimization model based on improved genetic algorithm. Firstly, combined with the operational characteristics and regulatory requirements of power grid enterprises, a multiobjective optimization framework is constructed, which takes economic benefit maximization, power grid reliability improvement and social service benefit enhancement as core objectives, and integrates investment quota limit, grid structure constraints and policy compliance as constraint conditions. Secondly, aiming at the defects of standard GA such as poor local search ability and easy to fall into local optimal solution, an improved genetic algorithm is designed by introducing adaptive crossover-mutation operator, elite retention strategy and adaptive weight adjustment mechanism of fitness function. Finally, the simulation verification is carried out based on the actual investment data of a regional power grid enterprise.

title: A Multi-Objective Operational Management Approach for Photovoltaic Charging Stations Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Xingyu Shen, Kai Liao, Jianwei Yang, Zhengyou He
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Coupling photovoltaic (PV) generation with energy storage significantly enhances the economic and environmental performance of service area charging stations. Nevertheless, the volatility of PV output and the uncertainty of electric vehicles (EV) charging demands lead to conflicts among multiple objectives, including operating costs, user satisfaction, and PV consumption. Consequently, this paper presents a Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) based multi-objective energy management method for PV-storage charging stations. First, a charging event generation model based on adversarial networks is established. Second, a deep reinforcement learning approach utilizing TD3 is developed, enabling real-time optimal energy dispatching. Finally, the comparison results show that the proposed method outperforms the benchmark algorithms, successfully achieving a real-time optimal balance among multiple objectives.

title: Prototype Development of an Integrated Distributed Energy-Dissipating Modular Multi-Level Converter Based on 6.5kV IGBT
authors: Yiliang Xu, Yuebin Zhou, Wanyu Cao, Liu Yang, Yue Wu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: To address the AC fault ride-through of voltage source converter-based high voltage direct current (VSC-HVDC) systems, a submodule was developed using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.5 \text{kV} / 3 \text{kA}$</tex> IGBT power devices. The topology and control methods of the submodule are introduced, and its control principles are verified through system simulation. The insulation, thermal field, magnetic field strength, and busbar electrodynamic force of the submodule are confirmed through multi-physics simulation, ensuring the design's reasonableness. Finally, experimental tests verify the submodule's current-carrying capacity and power dissipation capacity. The results show that the submodule with the power dissipation function can regulate the submodule capacitor voltage within set parameters, ensuring the converter's normal operation during fault ride-through.

title: Current Stress Optimization Control of Dual Active Bridge DC-DC Converter Based on Dual Integral Sliding Mode Control
authors: Xinrui Pei, Fei Yu, Jiaming Qi, Xiaoqian Yue, Mingren Wu, Jie Li, Jinjin Zan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: As clean energy gradually replaces traditional energy, the dual active bridge DC-DC converter, as an important component of DC microgrids, is prone to issues such as slow dynamic response and reduced efficiency when subjected to disturbances. In this paper, by optimizing the combination relationship of current stress under extended phase shift modulation, a reduced-order model of the DAB converter is established, and an optimal control strategy integrating dualintegral sliding mode control and minimum current stress is proposed. Simulation results show that, compared with traditional PI control, the proposed control strategy can effectively reduce current stress and effectively improve the system dynamic performance.

title: Secure Optimization with Asynchronous Structured Skyline Predicates under Vertical Data Federation
authors: Yu Chen, Rongmao Chen, Shaojing Fu, Mingwu Zhang, Yuexiang Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Skyline optimization is a powerful tool for filtering prominent data to support analysis and decision-making. However, traditional centralized skyline predicates are inadequate for contemporary data islands, and shallow data federation poses a threat to privacy with sensitive data. In existing distributed environments, achieving both efficiency and security in skyline computation remains a critical challenge. This paper addresses the challenge of performing secure skyline predicates on encrypted data federation while safeguarding both the dataset and skyline from unauthorized access. We propose a novel asynchronous structured skyline predicate based on vertical dominance and truth-value conversion, taking full advantage of distributed computing. Furthermore, we introduce a secure optimization that balances security and efficiency, thereby facilitating a distributed skyline predicate. We evaluate the efficiency and scalability across various parameters, demonstrating improvements in traversal overhead and expensive ciphertext operations.

title: Joint Beam Scheduling and Passive Beamforming for IRS-Assisted Hybrid-Field SWIPT
authors: Chaoying Huang, Wen Chen, Qiong Wu, Jinhong Yuan
abstract: This paper investigates an intelligent reflecting surface (IRS)-assisted simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) system under the hybrid-field model, where the IRS operates in the near-field region of the base station, while one information user (IU) and multiple energy users (EUs) are located in the far-field of the IRS. Specifically, we establish a hybrid-field model and derive a closed-form expression for the element-wise combined channel gain. Based on this, we jointly optimize beam scheduling, power allocation, and IRS phase shift with the quality-of-service (QoS) constraint, enabling dynamic beam steering to maximize the total harvested energy of EUs while maintaining a balanced trade-off between information transfer and energy harvesting. An alternating optimization algorithm is developed to solve the non-convex problem by applying the Lagrange duality method, semidefinite relaxation (SDR), and successive convex approximation (SCA). Our results reveal that the beam is always directed toward the IU to guarantee its QoS and is further adaptively steered toward the EU with the highest energy-harvesting priority in hybrid-field SWIPT. Numerical results validate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme.

title: An Ultrawideband and Low-Profile Phased Array Design for Satellite-assisted Internet of Things Vehicular Communication
authors: Liangliang Zhao, Hailiang Zhu, Aidong Li, Lehu Wen, Shi-Tong Wang, Kai-Cheng Wang, Huiling Zhao, Chufeng Hu
abstract: This article presents a low-profile dual-polarized phased array antenna featuring an improved feed structure and a multiresonant wide-angle impedance matching (WAIM) layer for satellite-assisted Internet-of-Things (SATA-IoT) vehicular communications across the L-, S-, C-, and X-bands. First, an improved feed structure based on a double-sided multi-notch balun (DMB) is developed to provide stable impedance transformation and tailored field distribution, thereby enhancing impedance matching in the low- and mid-frequency bands. Second, a planar nonuniform metasurface (PNM)-based multiresonant WAIM layer is introduced. By properly engineering its effective permittivity and permeability, two desired resonances together with inductive coupling are achieved, enabling wide-angle scanning performance in the high-frequency band. These two key techniques jointly allow the proposed array to realize ultrawide bandwidth, wide-angle scanning capability, and a low-profile configuration. To validate the design, a 5×5 prototype was fabricated and measured. The measured results show a 9:1 impedance bandwidth from 1.4 to 12.6 GHz. In particular, both array configurations exhibit an active VSWR below 3 at broadside, and maintain active VSWR below 3.3 for scan angles up to ±70° across the entire operating band. The overall profile is only 0.075 λlow. These results demonstrate that the proposed array is a promising candidate for SATA-IoT and related vehicular IoT communication systems.

title: Decoupled Seg Tokens Make Stronger Reasoning Video Segmenter and Grounder
authors: Jisheng Dang, Xudong Wu, Haowen Yan, Huicheng Zheng, Wei-Shi Zheng, Jianhuang Lai, Bin Hu, Tat-Seng Chua
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Existing video segmenter and grounder approaches, exemplified by Sa2VA, directly fuse features within segmentation models. This often results in an undesirable entanglement of dynamic visual information and static semantics, thereby degrading segmentation accuracy. To systematically mitigate these issues, we propose DeSa2VA, a decoupling-enhanced prompting scheme integrating text pre-training and a linear decoupling module to address the information processing limitations inherent in SAM-2. Specifically, first, we devise a pre-training paradigm that converts textual ground-truth labels into point-level prompts while generating corresponding text masks. These masks are refined through a hybrid loss function to strengthen the model's semantic grounding capabilities. Next, we employ linear projection to disentangle hidden states generated by a large language model into distinct textual and visual feature subspaces. Finally, a dynamic mask fusion strategy synergistically combines these decoupled features through triple supervision from predicted text/visual masks and ground-truth annotations. Extensive experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art performance across diverse tasks, including image segmentation, image question answering, video segmentation, and video question answering. Our codes are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/longmalongma/DeSa2VA</uri>.
