abstract: None

title: Stress-Aware Modeling in UX: Combining Synthetic Physiological Data Features with Personality-Based Labeling
authors: Alexandros Liapis, Konstantinos Panitsidis, Georgia Zournatzidou, Anna Triantafyllou, Panagiotis Sarigiannidis, Nikolaos Ntampakis
abstract: Stress modeling from physiological signals has become an established approach in user experience (UX) evaluation, offering objective insights into user discomfort during interaction. A persistent challenge, however, lies in the limited availability of high-quality datasets, which can constrain the robustness of machine learning models. This study addresses this challenge within a baseline-independent stress modeling framework, by generating synthetic physiological feature data from two independent UX interaction scenarios and evaluating their utility under personality-based labeling, with Emotional Stability used as an indicator of stress proneness. Distributional comparisons between real and synthetic features showed that the two datasets are difficult to distinguish at the multivariate level, with a two-sample discrimination test yielding near-chance performance, with an Area Under Curve (AUC) of approximately 0.47. Across nine classification models, synthetic features led to performance levels comparable to those obtained using real data alone, while their combination with real samples resulted in modest gains for some classifiers, such as Random Forest (94.06% to 95.87%) and Multi-Layer Perceptron (92.70% to 95.43%). When models trained under one UX interaction scenario were evaluated on a different scenario, performance remained stable, indicating limited sensitivity to changes in interaction context. Overall, the findings highlight synthetic feature augmentation as a reliable strategy for supporting robust and data-efficient baseline-independent stress-aware modeling in UX evaluation.

title: The Latens Patronus: Seamless Model Watermarking for Latent Diffusion Model in IoT Environments
authors: Qin Zhao, Tong Liu, Wei Ke, Guoheng Huang, Xueyuan Gong, Xiaochen Yuan
abstract: With the rapid development of the Internet of Things (IoT), generative artificial intelligence has been widely applied in various IoT applications. However, the wide adoption of latent diffusion model (LDM) in such IoT scenarios raises severe risks of copyright infringement and model theft due to the lack of effective protection mechanisms. To address this challenge, we propose Latens Patronus, a seamless model watermarking technique for copyright protection of LDM in IoT environments. Unlike existing model watermarking methods, our method does not require additional watermark input and additional parameters for a specialized embedding network, making it more suitable for deployment in real-world IoT applications. Specifically, we design a Watermark Encoder to integrate image watermark into latent features during the generation process and aWatermark Decoder to accordingly extract the watermark from suspicious images accurately. We further introduce a diminishing training strategy that gradually fades out auxiliary supervision signals, eliminating the need for persistent watermark guidance, and adding no extra overhead to the original model. Extensive experiments on multiple LDM variants demonstrate that Latens Patronus outperforms existing watermarking methods in both invisibility and robustness against image-level and model-level attacks.

title: Branching-Ratio-Based Detection Efficiency Calibration of Transition-Edge Sensors at C-Band Wavelengths
authors: Tetsuya Tsuruta, Takeshi Jodoi, Mauro Rajteri, Daiji Fukuda
abstract: We characterize a detection efficiency calibration system with a wavelength-tunable pulsed photon source for single photon detectors or photon number resolving detectors covering the telecommunication range from 1510 nm to 1570 nm, with measurements performed at the representative wavelength of 1550 nm. The source employs an external cavity diode laser and acousto-optic modulator for pulse generation with controlled mean photon number. By implementing a branching-ratio monitoring scheme instead of direct power monitoring, we achieved a relative Allan deviation below 0.02% at an averaging time of 10000 seconds, representing a tenfold improvement in long-term stability. This stability enables extended calibration measurements while maintaining consistent photon flux determination. Using photon-number-resolving detection with transition edge sensors, we measured second- and third-order intensity correlation functions from single datasets. The light source maintained Poisson distribution at repetition rates below 1 MHz, with weak bunching (g⁽²⁾ ≈ 1.05, g⁽³⁾ ≈ 1.16) observed at higher frequencies. The measured correlations satisfied the theoretical relation for weak bunching, indicating a single dominant mechanism, likely background light from the finite AOM extinction ratio. While we cannot entirely exclude potential contributions from the detector's response characteristics, the systematic frequency dependence and consistency with the weak bunching model strongly suggest the light source as the primary factor. The demonstrated stability and characterized photon statistics confirm the suitability of this source for detector calibration at repetition rates up to 1 MHz. These performance metrics meet the requirements for calibrating photon-number resolving detectors used in quantum state characterization and quantum information applications.

title: Quickest Change Detection for UAV-Based Sensing
authors: Saqib Abbas, Anurag Kumar, Arpan Chattopadhyay
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of quickest change detection (QCD) at two spatially separated locations monitored by a single unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) equipped with a sensor. At any location, the UAV observes i.i.d. data sequentially in discrete time instants. The distribution of the observation data changes at some unknown, arbitrary time and the UAV has to detect this change in the shortest possible time. Change can occur at most at one location over the entire infinite time horizon. The UAV switches between these two locations in order to quickly detect the change. To this end, we propose Location Switching and Change Detection (LS-CD) algorithm which uses a repeated one-sided sequential probability ratio test (SPRT) based mechanism for observation-driven location switching and change detection. The primary goal is to minimize the worst-case average detection delay (WADD) while meeting constraints on the average run length to false alarm (ARL2FA) and the UAV’s time-averaged energy consumption. We provide a rigorous theoretical analysis of the algorithm’s performance by using theory of random walk. Specifically, we derive tight upper and lower bounds to its ARL2FA and a tight upper bound to its WADD. In the special case of a symmetrical setting, our analysis leads to a new asymptotic upper bound to the ARL2FA of the standard CUSUM algorithm, a novel contribution not available in the literature, to our knowledge. Numerical simulations demonstrate the efficacy of LS-CD.

title: An Efficient Junction Temperature Prediction Model for Diamond-on-Chip-on-Glass (DoCoG) Packaging Based on Thermal Resistance Network
authors: Chenghao Bei, Tian Yu, Minghao Zhou, Shuchao Bao, Yuqi Su, Yi Zhong, Xiaofeng Gu, Daquan Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence (AI) and high performance computing, 2.5D packaging employing glass interposer has demonstrated significant advantages in improving system performance and integration density. However, the continuous increase in power density and the strengthened thermal coupling effects in multi-chip packaging lead to a rapid rise in chip junction temperature, posing serious challenges to system reliability and thermal design. Bonding diamond onto the chip and integrating it with glass interposer to form diamond-on-chip-on-glass (DoCoG) packaging structure can effectively reduce chip junction temperature. To enable fast prediction of junction temperature during the packaging design, this study proposes an efficient thermal resistance network model. The proposed model considers two heat dissipation paths in DoCoG packaging and incorporates thermal coupling resistance between chips, allowing accurate characterization of internal heat conduction and convection mechanisms. For the periodic microstructures in DoCoG packaging, equivalent modeling methods are employed to simplify their thermal characteristics, thereby significantly reducing model complexity. Finite element simulations are conducted under different chip power levels and convection boundary conditions to validate the proposed model. The results demonstrate that the analytical model maintains good prediction accuracy and high computational efficiency across various operating conditions.

title: Are Deep Learning Based Hybrid PDE Solvers Reliable? Why Training Paradigms and Update Strategies Matter
authors: Yuhan Wu, Jan Willem van Beek, Victorita Dolean, Alexander Heinlein
abstract: Deep learning-based hybrid iterative methods (DL-HIMs) integrate stationary iterative solvers with neural operators, utilizing their complementary spectral biases to accelerate convergence. However, many DL-HIMs stagnate at false fixed points where updates vanish while significant physical residuals persist. We demonstrate that reliability depends on training paradigms and update strategies rather than neural architectures alone. Through a detailed study of a nonlinear DeepONet-based and a linear FFT-based DL-HIM, we show that convergence degrades when training objectives are misaligned with solver dynamics and problem physics. We examine Anderson acceleration and find its classical form ill-suited for nonlinear neural operators. We introduce physics-aware Anderson acceleration (PA-AA), which minimizes the physical residual rather than the fixed-point update. Numerical experiments confirm that PA-AA restores reliable convergence in substantially fewer iterations. These findings suggest that addressing controversies surrounding AI-based PDE solvers requires attention not only to architectures but to physically informed training and iteration design.

title: Multi-Agent Opportunistic Routing for UAV Swarms: A Deployment-Aligned Robust Learning Framework
authors: Jianrui Fan, Lei Lei, Shengsuo Cai, Gaoqing Shen, Pan Cao
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarms are essential for mission-critical aerial Internet of Things (IoT) applications. However, reliable multi-hop routing in these swarms is severely challenged by highly dynamic topologies and partial, asynchronous observations. Furthermore, conventional training data poorly represent rare but decisive disruptions, such as terrain occlusion, no-fly-zone detours, and congestion bursts. To address these issues, this paper proposes a deployment-aligned robust learning framework organized into three functional layers. At the policy layer, we propose the Multi-Agent Opportunistic (MAO) routing method, learned via a Belief Graph-based MAPPO (BG-MAPPO) algorithm. To overcome partial and stale observations, BG-MAPPO constructs a local belief graph encoding the dynamic neighborhood state, which is then used to jointly determine the optimal forwarder-set size and a diversity-aware routing distribution. At the training layer, the policy is optimized inside a terrain-aware, physics-calibrated Digital Twin Network (DTN). To prevent overfitting to limited training distributions, a diffusion-based generator (FD3M) enriches the training data with trajectory-and-flow samples covering critical corner cases. At the validation layer, large-scale simulations show that MAO reduces end-to-end delay by 27–34% compared to representative baselines. The policy also incurs modest operational overhead, maintaining millisecond-level inference and bounded signaling. Finally, hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) experiments confirm that the DTN accurately predicts physical execution, with relative gaps of only 4% in packet delivery ratio and 7% in delay, effectively bridging the sim-to-real gap for robust UAV-swarm networking.

title: Elastic Cloud Scaling for Small Models: Dynamic Resource Allocation as an Alternative to Training Large LLMs
authors: Mitali Thapa, Kamal Kant Hiran, Simran Ajit Kumar Mohanty, Deepak Parashar, Ankur Goyal
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have driven significant advances in natural language processing, but their static deployment for high-throughput inference often leads to high computational cost and latency. For moderate-complexity, high-volume workloads, smaller language models (SLMs) can provide sufficient performance when combined with effective resource management. This work presents a system-level analytical framework to evaluate elastic inference serving for SLMs under cost and latency constraints, without positioning them as replacements for LLMs. In this research, a hybrid methodology comprising a threshold-driven autoscaling simulation, akin to Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA), and the actual latency values (p50 and p95) of a CPU-based DistilBERT deployment are used. It makes comparisons between elastic scaling and a fixed resource allocation baseline based on synthetic daily workload patterns with peak-to-low demand ratios greater than 10:1. The findings indicate that elastic scaling is a good response to the demand variations, sustains the target utilization, lowers the cost of computation, and ensures that the performance remains constant. In general, the paper reveals significant trade-offs in fixed and dynamic resource allocation and shows the advantages of using elastic inference as a way of cost-efficient implementation in resource-constrained environments.

title: MLDCSS: Energy-Aware Multi-Level Cooperative Spectrum Sensing with Hierarchical Fusion and Non-Coherent MIMO in CR-IoT Networks
authors: Kaushik Das, Chetna Singhal, Amit Kumar Dutta, Raja Datta
abstract: This research article presents a unique multi-evel decision cooperative spectrum sensing (MLDCSS) framework for multiple-input-multiple-output (MIMO)-enabled cognitive radio internet of things (CR-IoT) networks. The architecture uses a modified k-means algorithm to account for residual energy and spatial proximity, resulting in up to 35% longer cluster-head lifetimes. Secondary users (SUs) use non-coherent square law selection (SLS) with multiple antennas for pilot-free sensing. Cluster heads (CHs) use equal gain combining (EGC) to fuse intra-cluster observations, reducing per-node computational load by approximately 40% compared to maximal ratio combining (MRC). The fusion center (FC) uses a hierarchical two-stage fusion approach to make final spectrum occupancy judgments, resulting in a 20% increase in detection probability at 0.1 false alarm rate. Analytical performance constraints for Nakagami-m fading are derived and validated using large Monte Carlo simulations, revealing close agreement within 2 dB over a wide signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) range. Complexity study demonstrates overall linear scaling with the number of SUs, delivering a 50% reduction in signaling overhead and 15% higher area-under-curve (AUC) in Pd-Pf tradeoffs compared to centralized and flat OR fusion baselines. These findings show that MLDCSS is a scalable, energy-efficient, and useful solution for distributed spectrum sensing in next-generation CR-IoT networks.

title: PAMA: Provable-Secure Anonymous Multi-Factor Authentication with Post-Quantum Security for IoT-enabled E-Healthcare Systems
authors: Yuqian Ma, Yongliu Ma, Zhiquan Liu, Qi Jiang, Qingfeng Cheng
abstract: The booming technologies of the Internet of Things (IoT) enable various applications and develop well. E-healthcare system is one of the successful examples, where people manage their healthcare information, diagnosis data, physical examination results, and so on. These types of sensitive information cause crucial security risks, posing threats to the security of the lives and property of the people. Given this concern, safeguard measures, such as authentication and encryption, are necessary. However, due to the openness of the wireless networks, authentication protocols for IoT-enabled e-healthcare systems are usually vulnerable to serious attacks. Furthermore, the quantum era is around the corner, urging authentication protocols to possess post-quantum security properties. In this paper, we propose a multi-factor password authentication scheme, named PAMA, with post-quantum security for the e-healthcare system. The lattice cryptography is adopted for post-quantum security. We formally prove the proposed PAMA scheme to be secure, and also informally verify the security against common attacks. Furthermore, we compare the performance of the communication efficiency of the PAMA scheme with other related works from both theoretical and experimental aspects. The efficiency comparison results demonstrate that our PAMA scheme approximately reduces the computation cost by 37.59% and the energy consumption by 37.5%, compared to the related post-quantum schemes. In summary, the PAMA scheme has a great advantage in secure authentication and agreement on a session key in the e-healthcare system.

title: An Improved Beta Burst Extraction for Chip-Based Deep Brain Stimulation with Real-Time Model Updating
authors: Yi-Huan Ou-Yang, Hsiao-Chun Lin, Chi-Wei Huang, Chung-Yu Wu, Ming-Dou Ker, Chen-Yi Lee
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Engineering in Medicine and Biology
abstract: Goal: Existing beta burst detection algorithms for closed-loop deep brain stimulation (DBS) are computationally complex, limiting their use in implantable devices. We aimed to develop an improved beta burst extraction algorithm for chip-based DBS devices with real-time model updating. Methods: Building on an established beta burst detection method, we proposed a sliced mechanism for peak frequency finding and modified burst extraction for information sharing with real-time model updating. Results: Testing on rat electrocorticographic (ECoG) recordings showed that the proposed algorithm maintains a strong correlation (ρ = 0.89 ± 0.06) with the conventional method, with a 53.3% reduction in computational complexity for peak frequency finding. Conclusions: Integrating this improved beta burst detection into chip-based DBS devices represents a key algorithmic advancement toward adaptive neuromodulation therapies. The strong correlation and reduced complexity validate our proposal for real-time neural biomarker tracking, facilitating hardware and chip implementation, and advancing the development of implantable systems.

title: A Deep Bidirectional LSTM Model Enhanced by Transfer-Learning for the Classification of Peripheral Arterial Blood Pressure Waveforms
authors: D. Guberti, M. Carrara, M. Ferrario
abstract: Objective: Arterial blood pressure waveform (BPW) morphology provides critical insight into cardiovascular status and can serve as an early marker of pathological changes, particularly in critically ill patients where waveform alterations may occur over hours. This study aims to develop a deep learning (DL) framework to classify BPWs into type A and type B/C morphologies, reflecting distinct cardiovascular conditions. Methods: A bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) model was trained on central (aortic) and peripheral (femoral) invasive BPWs from a large animal cohort. The framework extends the applicability of waveform separation analysis (WSA), as waveform type determines the choice and tuning of arterial blood flow estimation methods. To explore clinical relevance, deep transfer learning (DTL) was applied to femoral BPWs from the MIMIC-III database, retraining only the last 8 of 15 layers to balance accuracy and computational cost. Results: The BiLSTM achieved 73% accuracy on aortic and 77% on femoral BPWs in independent animal test sets. Using DTL, classification on human femoral BPWs reached 77% accuracy, comparable to animal model performance. Conclusion: DTL effectively adapts animal trained models to human data, supporting potential clinical translation. Results also highlight challenges due to limited large, labeled clinical datasets, essential for robust validation. Significance: DL-based BPW classification may improve cardiovascular monitoring in critically ill patients by enabling early detection of vascular alterations.

title: Feature Matching-Based Extraction of Independent Ice Floe Motion in the Arctic Marginal Ice Zone
authors: Rui Zhang, Adan Wu, Jinlei Chen, Qingchao Xu, Liuhao Wang, Kaihui Zhang, Yilei Xiao, Lei Fu, Fuhong Deng, Rui Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The extraction of independent ice floe motion in the Arctic Marginal Ice Zone (MIZ) plays a crucial role in motion prediction, polar climate research, and ensuring navigation safety. In this study, we propose an integrated algorithm framework that combines ice floe contour masking with the A-KAZE feature tracking method to extract high-precision motion data from time-series MODIS imagery. The algorithm's performance was evaluated across five sub-regions. Comparisons with the National Snow and Ice Data Center sea-ice drift product indicated high accuracy and stability. Additionally, results aligned closely with the classical linear free-drift model, demonstrating that the algorithm successfully captures the actual motion trends of ice floes driven by wind and ocean currents. Further validation against manually identified data revealed strong consistency in both ice floe centroid positions and motion vectors. These findings underscore the method's robustness and reliability, making it a valuable tool for understanding and predicting ice floe dynamics in the MIZ.

title: Voltage Risk Assessment of Power Systems Based on an Electrical Coupling-Aware Temporal Fusion Network Model
authors: Boqing Yan, Zhe Lv, Zengping Wang, Chenhui Lin
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable generation units into power systems, renewable-dominated power systems are facing new challenges. In particular, the integration of renewable generation may cause bus voltage fluctuations and even voltage violations. To address this issue, this paper proposes an Electrical Coupling-aware Temporal Fusion Network specifically designed for voltage violation risk assessment in power systems. The model consists of three core modules: an electrical couplingaware graph embedding layer, a spatiotemporal feature extraction module, and a risk assessment output layer. The proposed model does not rely on explicit calculation of power flow equations. Instead, it learns the electrical coupling relationships among nodes through graph structure modeling. At the same time, it captures both the spatial topological characteristics and the temporal dynamic evolution of the power grid, enabling multi-scale risk perception for subsequent risk assessment. The model is trained and tested using real historical data from a regional power grid and an improved IEEE 39-bus test system with high renewable penetration. The results demonstrate that, compared with conventional methods, the proposed voltage violation risk assessment approach achieves higher warning rate and lower false alarm rate. It also exhibits strong generalization capability and robustness, making it suitable for practical application in realworld power systems.

title: A Photovoltaic Resource Survey Method Guided by UAV Precision Survey Samples and Trained Across Scale Image Mapping
authors: Zhiyong Liu, Yongbiao Liang, Lieliang Hu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: The large-scale integration of distributed photovoltaic (PV) power has created an urgent need for the coordinated optimization of assessment accuracy and resource allocation efficiency in distribution network area planning. Addressing the inherent contradiction between accuracy and cost in the traditional “full-area satellite assessment + sampling UAV verification” model, this paper proposes a UAV-driven PV survey and assessment method based on a “priority-guidedlocal precision measurement-full-area extrapolation” analytical framework. First, high-value areas are located by integrating remote sensing preliminary screening results with area operation data, constructing a priority evaluation model that couples resource potential and access urgency, ensuring accurate resource allocation by UAV inspections under the area-based hierarchical mechanism. Second, considering the characteristics of satellite imagery (wide coverage but limited accuracy) and UAV imagery (high accuracy but cost constraints), a cross-scale modeling method based on local feature fitting and full-area collaborative extrapolation is proposed. This method uses a deep residual network to learn the mapping relationship between highresolution UAV imagery observations and satellite imagery features, achieving the value extension from local precision understanding to full-area assessment. Experiments in 32 areas in Shaoguan, Guangdong Province, show that the full-area assessment accuracy improved from 0.811 to 0.851 after applying this method. This method essentially guarantees low-cost sampling learning, full-area applicability, and long-term effectiveness-a truly economical and practical approach.

title: Harmonic Cancellation-Based Virtual DQ Decoupling Control for Totem-Pole Bridgeless PFC with Enhanced Dynamic and Steady-State Performance
authors: Shiping Huang, Song Xiong, Jian Zhou, Xin Ni
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: The totem-pole bridgeless power factor correction (TBPFC) converter based on traditional dual-closed-loop control is subject to the inherent double-line-frequency ripple of single-phase AC/DC conversion systems, which makes it difficult to simultaneously achieve low input current harmonics and high dynamic response performance. To solve this problem, a quasi-resonant-based virtual dq decoupling control method is proposed in this paper. A second-order generalized integrator (SOGI) is introduced to construct virtual orthogonal components, thus realizing dq transformation and current inner-loop decoupling for the single-phase system. On this basis, a feedforward harmonic cancellation scheme is designed by extracting the d-axis double-line-frequency component, which decouples the input current quality of TBPFC from the bandwidth of the voltage outer loop. In addition, a proportional-integral-resonant (PIR) controller is integrated into the current inner loop to achieve deep suppression of high-order harmonics. The effectiveness of the proposed control method is verified by simulation. The THD of the input current is reduced from 4.48% to 3.44%, improving the current quality by 13.5%. During a load step-up transient, the settling time of the output voltage is reduced from 80 ms to 48 ms, and during a load step-down transient, it decreases from 82 ms to 56 ms, achieving an overall improvement of 50%.

title: A 0.2–12-GHz Fast-Settling Wideband Frequency Synthesizer With Stage-Switching Frequency Extender and Continuous Compensation PFDCP
authors: Xinyi Zhang, Wanqing Wu, Jiangnan Li, Ruiyong Xiang, Ke Cao, Guoxun Dai, Haigang Feng, Zhihua Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: A fast-settling wideband frequency synthesizer (FS) for multiband communication applications is presented. Through the integration of a wideband low-noise VCO with the proposed stage-switching frequency extender (SS-FEX), the FS achieves continuous frequency coverage from 0.2 GHz to 12 GHz with high area efficiency. Equipped with inter-stage switches and wake-up transistors, the SS-FEX supports a division ratio range of (2 or 3)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">N</sup> (where N = 1-4) with low power consumption, without degrading the phase noise performance of PLL. The optimized E-TSPC divider adopted in the SS-FEX enables robust divide-by-2/3 operation over a wide frequency range. To accelerate locking, a continuous compensation phase-frequency detector and charge pump (CC-PFDCP) is proposed, which fully utilizes the idle time after the PFD reset. Fabricated in a 65nm CMOS process, the proposed synthesizer occupies a compact wideband technique (W.T.) area of 0.12 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and achieves an rms jitter of 139.6 fs. The measured locking time is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$13~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s. Consuming 19.3 mW overall, the FS achieves a figure of merit (FOM<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {J}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of 244.2 dB, a FOM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">J,T</sub> of 269.9 dB, and a W.T. area FOM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">J,T</sub> (FOM<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {J,T,A}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of 279.1 dB.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Impact of Acceleration and Imperfect CSI on Multidimensional Waveforms in Doubly Dispersive Channel
authors: Lavanya Mallampalli, Ch Santosh Reddy, Debarati Sen
abstract: The rapid growth of modern mobile electronic platforms requires reliable and seamless wireless communication links for uninterrupted data exchange for which Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation has emerged as a robust waveform due to its resilience in the delay–Doppler domain and N-dimensional (N-D) mapper improves performance by increasing the minimum Euclidean distance between constellation points, thereby reducing bit error rate (BER). To enhance the spectral efficiency in doubly dispersive channel, Orbital Angular Momentum (OAM) multiplexing is employed. This paper analyzes the joint effects of platform acceleration and imperfect Channel State Information on ND-OTFS–OAM systems using simulation-based BER evaluation.

title: IMS Society Awards
authors: N/A

title: Regrown-Ohmic-Contacts Enabled GaN HEMTs with Ultra-Low Noise Figures and Superior Power Merits for Wide-Band LNA Applications
authors: Haochen Zhang, Mingshuo Zhang, Xinchuan Zhang, Hu Wang, Junzhuo Qiu, Hongtu Qian, Hui Zhang, Yansong Yang, Chengjie Zuo, Yi Pei, Haiding Sun
abstract: Herein, based on MOCVD-regrown ohmic contacts (ROCs), a GaN HEMT with ultra-low noise figure and superior power merit is reported. Thanks to the ROCs, a low contact resistance of 0.13 Ω·mm is achieved, benefiting 26% reduced parasitic source resistance compared to the devices with alloyed ohmic contacts. As such, the ROC-HEMT shows reduced thermal noise and ultra-low minimum noise figures within a wide bandwidth, featuring 0.38/0.71/1.19 dB at 10/18/30 GHz with associated gain of 12.5/9.8/6.6 dB. In addition, remarkable output power and efficiency at Ku-band can be delivered by the ROC-HEMT, verifying the feasibility of MOCVD-ROC techniques to advance noise & power performance of GaN HEMTs towards compact all-GaN RF front-end modules.

title: Tri-Hybrid Combining Design in Segmented Pinching-Antenna MU-MISO Uplinks
authors: Jung-Chieh Chen, Hung-Hsun Chen
abstract: Reconfigurable antenna technologies, such as fluid and movable antennas, exploit spatial diversity but have limited ability to mitigate large-scale path loss due to their confined adjustment range. Pinching-antenna (PA) systems address this limitation by enabling flexible antenna deployment along dielectric waveguides. However, conventional single-waveguide PA architectures suffer from inter-antenna radiation, increased inwaveguide attenuation, and practical maintenance challenges. The segmented waveguide-enabled pinching-antenna (SWAN) architecture alleviates these issues by dividing the waveguide into independent segments, yet its combining flexibility remains limited in multiuser scenarios with a constrained radio frequency (RF) chain budget. To overcome this limitation, this paper investigates a practical SWAN-assisted multiuser multiple-input single-output uplink system employing tri-hybrid combining, where low-resolution phase shifters, discrete PA positions, and both fully connected (FC) and partially connected (PC) architectures are considered. We formulate a sum-rate maximization problem that jointly optimizes the digital combiner, analog phase shifters, and PA activations. To solve this highly coupled non-convex problem, we develop a unified algorithmic framework based on cross-entropy optimization (CEO) that learns high-quality configurations via parameterized probability distributions. In addition, a low-complexity coordinate update algorithm (CUA) is proposed to achieve a favorable trade-off between computational overhead and system performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed CEO-based design outperforms benchmark methods under both FC and PC architectures, while the CUA-based design achieves an attractive complexity-performance trade-off.

title: Convergence of Large Language Models, Internet of Things, and Process Mining: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Iman El Kodssi, Hanae Sbai, Nassima Ait Mansour
abstract: The convergence of Large Language Models (LLMs), Internet of Things (IoT), and Process Mining represents a transformative opportunity for intelligent process analysis, yet remains critically underexplored. While IoT generates massive sensor streams and Process Mining provides analytical methodologies, integrating these with LLM capabilities for semantic understanding and automated reasoning faces substantial technical barriers. This systematic review analyzes approximately 19,000 publications across four major databases to quantify and characterize this convergence landscape. Our analysis reveals a striking 32-fold research gap: while pairwise technology combinations dominate the literature (98.3%), full three-way convergence accounts for only 1.7% (n ≈ 330 articles), a figure validated across all four independent databases after rigorous deduplication, despite comparable development windows. We propose a three-dimensional taxonomy organizing convergence research by LLM functional role, IoT application domain, and process mining task type, revealing pronounced concentration in conservative use cases and critical gaps in predictive monitoring and system integration. Synthesis of reported implementations identifies super-linear integration barriers, with 76% of systems remaining at proof-of-concept stage. The findings establish convergence as an emerging high-impact research frontier requiring coordinated advances in transformation reliability, real-time architectures, and holistic evaluation frameworks to bridge the gap from prototype to production deployment.

title: Theoretical Study and Validation for the High-Gain High-Mode-Purity Double Spiral Vortex Electromagnetic Wave Antenna Array
authors: Ke Wang, Binyun Yan, Renli Zhang, Weixing Sheng
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: The gain and mode purity of the double spiral vortex electromagnetic (EM) wave antenna array are studied theoretically and validated experimentally for high-gain, high-mode-purity double spiral array designs. A double arm Archimedean spiral array structure is firstly proposed, and the gain and mode purity are analyzed theoretically using numerical methods as its azimuth-dependent radius exhibiting additional azimuthal terms in the field expansion. Theoretically, with the same aperture, the DSA can achieve 12% higher mode purity and a 33.9% smaller physical area compared to a UCA, while maintaining comparable gain. Based on theoretical analysis, a DSA and a UCA with the same aperture are constructed and compared. Measured results of the prototypes are in good agreement with the simulations, further verifying the theoretical analysis. The DSA is a strong candidate for a high-gain, high-mode-purity vortex EM wave antenna array.

title: General Revenue Adequacy Conditions for Energy Transport Networks
authors: Sidhant Misra, Marc Vuffray, Anatoly Zlotnik, Aleksandr M. Rudkevich
abstract: Optimization is widely used to determine the physical and financial exchange of wholesale electricity in organized markets. Guarantees of solution optimality and feasibility rest largely on convexity, which is not in general a characteristic of the governing equations for power grid and gas pipeline networks. Policy decisions that base the scheduling and locational pricing of electricity transactions on optimization rely on the guarantee of revenue adequacy, which ensures that the market administrator will collect enough payments in congestion rents to settle financial transmission rights. Developing a similar mechanism for locational trade valuation of natural gas also requires assurance that pricing outcomes are revenue adequate, and also cover the costs of gas compressor operation. However, it has been shown that the AC power flow equations are in general non-convex and hence conditions for guaranteeing revenue adequacy in optimal power flow solutions are challenging to generalize. In this study, we develop a general formal mathematical setting for nonlinear physical network flows and examine the conditions for revenue adequacy. The result is verified for DC and AC power flow as well as steady-state gas flow in a pipeline network.

title: Beam-Switchable Antenna Array Based on Parasitic Unit Boundary for Customer Premises Equipment
authors: Sipei Peng, Zuxing Lu, Hui Pang, Shu-Lin Chen, Hai Lin
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: To address the demand for wide beam coverage in 5 G and Internet of Things (IoT) devices, this paper proposes a low-profile cross-dipole antenna designed for customer-premises equipment (CPE). The antenna has high isolation, stable gain, compact size, and ease of fabrication. The 6×7 antenna array constructed using the proposed antenna elements, combined with the input configuration of the feed network, enables switching between three radiation beams. Within the 2.515–2.675 GHz frequency range, a slotted octagonal parasitic element is introduced to regulate the surface current distribution on the radiating patch, thereby achieving beamwidth broadening and mitigating pattern distortion. The antenna system achieves three reconfigurable radiation states through simulation and measurement. The half-power beamwidths (HPBW) in the horizontal and vertical directions are 60×60, 60×26, and 25×60, respectively. This flexibility enables adaptive coverage forr diverse scenarios.

title: Diagnosis and Treatment of a Batch-Related Abnormal Gas Generation Fault in Converter Transformers
authors: Jin Wang, Peijie Luo, Xin Wang, Pengxiang Zhou, Sheng Feng
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: This paper elaborates in detail on the diagnosis and treatment process of a batch-related abnormal gas generation fault in converter transformers. Long-term tracking and analysis of oil chromatography data revealed that overheating faults caused by design or manufacturing defects existed internally in this batch of converter transformers. Further disassembly inspection identified overheating traces on the surface of the valve-side Y-connected grading tube and its leads. The lead joints utilized a pit crimping process, resulting in poor contact between the leads and connectors, increased contact resistance, and severe overheating under prolonged high-load currents, which triggered the decomposition of insulating oil and gas generation. Based on these findings, targeted factory repair measures and operational management strategies were proposed, including adopting cold crimping processes for joints and reducing operational loads. Subsequent operation of the converter transformers demonstrated the effectiveness of these measures, providing valuable references for the design, manufacturing, and on-site fault diagnosis and treatment of converter transformers.

title: High-Precision Modeling of Key Components in Fuel Cell Hybrid Systems and Research on Multi-Physics Coupling Characteristics
authors: Mengjie Li
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: The optimization of energy management strategies (EMS) for Fuel Cell Hybrid Electric Vehicles (FCHEVs) relies heavily on high-precision dynamic models of core components. Addressing the challenge of balancing computational complexity with real-time performance in existing models, this paper proposes a systematic modeling methodology encompassing fuel cell stacks, supercapacitors, and lithium-ion batteries. First, based on the Butler-Volmer and Nernst equations, an electrochemical model for the fuel cell is established, incorporating activation, ohmic, and concentration polarizations. A multi-physics coupling mechanism is introduced to correct for the effects of temperature, pressure, and humidity. Furthermore, a semi-empirical gray-box model combining physics-driven principles with data fitting is constructed, significantly improving computational efficiency while maintaining high accuracy. Second, to address system degradation, a dynamic lifetime assessment model is developed, accounting for reduced electrochemical activity, material aging, and operational conditions. Finally, a refined equivalent circuit model for supercapacitors based on double-layer theory and a lithium-ion battery model considering multi-RC networks are constructed. Simulation and experimental validation results demonstrate that the mean relative error (MRE) of the fuel cell gray-box model is less than 3 %, with computational efficiency improved by over 50 % compared to pure electrochemical models. The MRE for both supercapacitor and lithium-ion battery models is controlled within 2.5 %, exhibiting robust performance under various operating conditions. This study provides a reliable theoretical model foundation for the development of real-time EMS and lifetime prediction in FCHEVs.

title: Intertemporal Strategies Amid Technological Interaction during Integration of the IoT and Blockchain_supp1-3695080.pdf
authors: zhenglong zhou
abstract: The integration of the Internet of Things (IoT) and blockchain technology represents transformative synergy that optimizes blockchain consensus algorithms (including edge computing). Considering the technological interaction, this article presents two intertemporal strategies - passive purchase and active purchase-that are distinguished by whether the retailers bear the technological incompatibility cost. This research uncovers the underlying logic driving retailers' adoption of crossphase strategies and offers managerial insights for value creation in supply chains fortified by the “ IoT+blockchain ” interaction. Key discoveries are that if technological compatibility is comparatively high, the active purchase strategy entices retailers to be initially wary of the blockchain technology, stimulating fresh demand for integrating the IoT and blockchain and showcasing technological interaction expansion. Intriguingly, circumstances with low technological compatibility favor launching the passive purchase strategy, where increased technology output in Period 1 compensates for deficiencies in Period 2, spurring retailers to actively shoulder variable costs stemming from technological incompatibility. Conversely, a moderate level of technological development motivates retailers to create proprietary technologies so that the technology output in Period 2 compensates for deficiencies in Period 1, thereby fostering positive product demand and achieving equilibrium profits amid the technological interaction. In essence, this article provides retailers with managerial insights to tailor intertemporal strategies to market conditions, underscoring the pivotal role of technological compatibility and interaction.

title: Net-Zero Carbon Planning for Port Industrial Parks with High-Grade Heat Demand Based on Multi-Objective Optimization
authors: Nan Li, Bohan Meng, Hailin Mu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Taking an economic development zone in Hainan, China, as a case study, this study develops a long-term (2024–2050) multi-objective planning model integrating macroeconomic structural evolution with cutting-edge energy supply technologies. The model aims to minimize total discounted costs, cumulative net carbon emissions, and technology risk indices, while innovatively incorporating an endogenous demand-side response mechanism and dynamic carbon quota constraints. By solving the model via the Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II), the optimal compromise pathway was identified using the Ideal Point Method. Results indicate that the optimal pathway limits the total discounted cost to approximately 178 billion RMB and cumulative net emissions to 240 million tons, achieving a synergistic balance between environmental and economic goals. Endogenous industrial upgrading (with the secondary industry's share decreasing from 57.6% to 33.6%) significantly reduces the marginal cost of decarbonization. Driven by dynamic carbon pricing, the system triggers the premature retirement of stranded fossil fuel assets and effectively hedges against long-term carbon tax burdens through the front-loaded deployment of offshore wind coupled with molten salt thermal storage and high-temperature gas-cooled reactors (HTGR).
