                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    /rGO Extended-Gate Field-Effect Transistors for pH and Theophylline Detection
authors: Wei-Hsiang Chiang, Jun-Wei Chen, Po-An Yang, Yi-Hsuan Lee, Pei-An Wu, Anoop Kumar Singh, Po-Liang Liu, Hsin-Yu Chou, Dong-Sing Wuu
abstract: We report an electrolyte-gated sensing platform based on a β-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/reduced graphene oxide (rGO) -based extended-gate field-effect transistor (EGFET) developed as a dual-functional sensing platform capable of detecting pH and theophylline (TP) using the same device architecture. By systematically optimizing the incorporation of rGO at the sensing interface, the device achieves a pH sensitivity of 0.0406 V/pH, representing a 69% increase compared to the pristine β-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> EGFET, accompanied by a highly linear response with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> reaching 0.9853 and well-balanced reference-voltage excursions of approximately ± 0.21 V. Long-term operational stability is further confirmed by a significantly suppressed voltage drift of only 4–6 mV over a continuous measurement duration of 600 min, demonstrating robust performance under prolonged liquid-phase operation. For TP sensing, the optimized EGFET exhibits a high drain-current sensitivity of 0.248 μA/(μg·L<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>), with a linear response characterized by an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value of 0.9422, indicating effective transduction of molecular adsorption events into gate bias modulation. The overall improvement in sensing performance is attributed to enhanced interfacial charge buffering capability and stabilized field-effect coupling resulting from rGO integration. These findings underscore the potential of β-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/rGO-based EGFETs as a scalable, stable, and versatile sensing architecture for real-time nutritional biomarker monitoring in precision healthcare applications.

title: Adaptive Subspace Signal Detection and Performance Analysis in Nonzero-Mean Clutter
authors: Weijian Liu, Zhenyu Xu, Jun Liu, Hui Chen, Yongxiang Liu
abstract: To solve the problem of detecting subspace signals in nonzero-mean clutter, we propose adaptive detectors, based on the strategies of generalized likelihood ratio test (GLRT), Rao test, Wald test, gradient test, and Durbin test. The results show that the detectors based on GLRT, Rao and Wald are structurally consistent with the subspace detectors in zero-means clutter. The analytic expressions for the probability of detection (PD) and probability of false alarm (PFA) of each detector are derived, and two major performance differences in the nonzero-mean clutter scenario are revealed. One is the loss of degree of freedom (DOF), which is reduced by 1 compared with the zero-mean clutter scenario. The second is the loss of signal-to-clutter (SCR) ratio. Simulation and measured data verify the effectiveness of the proposed detectors and demonstrate their practical value in real-world radar systems.

title: Unrepeated White Rabbit Time Synchronisation Over a 300 km Optical Fibre Link
authors: Ben Amies-King, Marco Lucamarini
abstract: White Rabbit (WR) technology provides a commercially-available off-the-shelf solution for time synchronisation, with sub-nanosecond accuracy and picosecond-level precision, over optical fibre links typically spanning tens of kilometres. Such high-performance time dissemination can support a variety of applications, including position, navigation and timing (PNT), financial transactions, metrology, as well as entanglement and quantum key distribution (QKD). Demonstrations of WR over significantly longer distances remain few and far between, particularly in scenarios where intermediate amplification is unavailable, such as stretches of long-haul underwater fibre. In this work, we report the longest unrepeated deployment of WR to date, achieving time synchronisation over a 300km (51.34 dB) single-span optical fibre link, even in highly asymmetric configurations, with 99.86% uptime, whilst maintaining picosecond-level precision. This was achieved through careful selection and optimisation of the components deployed at the link's end points. By leveraging standard telecom fibre and off-the-shelf hardware, our results pave the way for a scalable and standardised timing backbone for large-scale quantum networks, offering a practical route towards time distribution in future heterogeneous quantum communication systems.

title: Fusion of Multi-Source Data for Comprehensive Assessment of Phytoplankton Composition
authors: Ehsan Mehdipour, Hongyan Xi, Alexander Barth, Adalbert Wilhelm, Astrid Bracher
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Understanding phytoplankton composition and distribution is essential for assessing marine ecosystem processes, yet satellite and in situ datasets each have substantial limitations. This study develops a fusion framework that combines gap-filled satellite-derived total chlorophyll a (TChla) and phytoplankton functional type (PFT) chlorophyll a (Chla) concentrations with in situ data points extracted from a hyperspectral flow-through spectrophotometric system (ACS). The objective is to generate spatially continuous and accurate Chla fields. The in situ hyperspectral absorption data obtained during the RV Polarstern PS113 expedition were used to derive TChla and PFT Chla concentrations with associated uncertainties, using an empirical orthogonal function based regression model (EOF-GLM). The ACS-derived estimates were then merged with the gap-filled satellite products through optimal interpolation to produce a consistent spatiotemporal dataset. The ACS-derived TChla and PFT Chla products showed strong agreement with the ground truth Chla concentrations (HPLC-Chla), with the coefficient of determination (R²) values above 0.66 for all groups and median percentage-deviation (MedPD) values between 27% and 46%. The fusion dataset further improved performance, reaching R² values higher than 0.69 and MedPD values between 21% and 44%. The combined product provides spatially and temporally continuous Chla fields and resolves regions with extensive satellite data gaps more effectively than either dataset alone. This fusion approach supports more reliable analyses of phytoplankton dynamics and can guide sampling strategies in areas with limited satellite coverage. Future work should focus on refining uncertainty estimates and testing the framework across different oceanic regions to improve its stability and broader applicability.

title: On Improved Modeling for LEO Doppler Spread
authors: Kuan-Po Chiu, Sumit Roy
abstract: Wide-sense stationary, Uncorrelated Scattering (WSSUS) channel models with corresponding Doppler shift/spread and coherence time profiles have been fundamental staples underlying stochastic models in terrestrial networking with end-point mobility. In this letter that focusses on low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite links, we highlight need for greater accuracy based on the distinct transmitter–scatterer–receiver geometry in such scenarios. This contribution re-derives the Doppler spread for LEO downlink based on typical geometry-driven local scattering scenarios and highlights gaps with prior art and alignment with recent observations.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Fault Identification, Protection, and Localization Strategies in HVDC Transmission Systems
authors: B Mahalakshmi, K Iyswarya Annapoorani
abstract: High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) transmission systems are highly significant in transporting high power at great distances, interconnecting asynchronous grids, and linking large-scale sources of renewable energy. Although HVDC systems have certain technical benefits, they are highly challenged in locating and guarding faults. This is due to the fact that the fault current increases rapidly, the system is low inertia, the converters dominate the behavior, and the coordination of the protection is complex, particularly in those networks where Voltage Source Converters (VSCs) and Modular Multilevel Converters (MMCs) are used. This paper includes a comprehensive discussion of fault classification, protection concerns, and the current advancement of fault detection and localization techniques of HVDC transmission systems. The literature survey is conducted as a structured narrative review, based on systematic search and qualitative analysis of relevant studies. A detailed explanation of fault classification, protection issues, and the latest development of fault detection and localization methods of HVDC transmission systems are discussed in this paper. The classical methods of protection, like protection by overcurrent, protection by differential, and protection by impedance, as well as protection by harmonic analysis, are strictly assessed regarding their limitations with reference to high-resistance faults, noise sensitivity, and dependence on communication. Besides this, more sophisticated protection techniques based on signal processing, traveling waves, transient analysis, and directing schemes are discussed exhaustively. The article further discusses the fact that an increasing number of individuals are applying artificial intelligence and machine learning methods, such as ANN, SVM, CNN, LSTM, and hybrid signal-processing-based models, to make HVDC protection quicker, more precise, and more dependable. The key research gaps are issues such as the problems with real-time implementation, data dependency, scaling, and coordination during multiterminal HVDC grids. Lastly, the paper presents future research directions that will enable the next-generation HVDC systems to become safer, faster, and more flexible.

title: Reinforcement learn-based dialogue policy with LLM-assisted decision distillation
authors: Guozhen Gao, Kai Xu, Zhenyu Wang
abstract: The dialogue policy is a fundamental component of pipeline dialogue systems, responsible for steering dialogue generation and ensuring compliance with established output constraints. Traditional approaches to dialogue policy learning typically involve sequential decision-making models based on deep reinforcement learning (RL). However, these deep RL-based dialogue policies often encounter challenges related to low sample efficiency, which can hinder their performance. Recent advancements in large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated exceptional capabilities across various domains, prompting an exploration of their potential to enhance dialogue policies. In this paper, we propose a novel framework called P3L, which synergistically integrates the fine-tuning of LLMs with on-policy RL methods to optimize dialogue policy learning. Our approach employs a robust feedback mechanism to collect and analyze interaction data, facilitating the fine-tuning of LLMs. Allowing the insights gained from LLMs to be incorporated as regularization terms within the RL-based agent’s learning process. This integration of LLM decision-making aims to address the limitations of traditional RL methods by leveraging the rich contextual understanding and generative capabilities of LLMs. The experimental results demonstrate that P3L significantly enhances the efficiency of dialogue policy learning, outperforming agents that rely solely on RL techniques.

title: Cert-ADD: Certified Adaptive Directional Defense Against Backdoor Attacks in Smart Homes Vision_supp1-3696003.pdf
authors: Jianbin Li
abstract: With the rapid development of the Internet of Things (IoT), smart home vision systems are widely deployed on smart cameras and home hubs to support security monitoring and automation decisions. Continual (re)training enabled by on-device collection and cloud feedback, together with third-party data processing, makes training-time backdoor poisoning a stealthy and high-impact threat. Existing empirical defenses lack formal guarantees and can be bypassed by adaptive attacks. Although randomized smoothing-based certified defenses provide theoretical guarantees, they typically assume isotropic noise and implicitly treat all directions as equally sensitive. This fails to capture directional sensitivity variations in real data and leads to overly conservative certification. To address this limitation, we propose Cert-ADD, a direction adaptive certified backdoor defense that learns a direction dependent smoothing noise structure for each sample and injects perturbations with different strengths along different feature directions, better matching local geometry and improving certified robustness. Cert-ADD further trains multiple direction smoothed models and aggregates their predictions. To handle sample specific ellipsoidal certified regions induced by direction dependent noise, we design an ellipsoid oriented conflict handling and region update mechanism, termed direction adaptive certification, which maintains prediction consistency within each region while preserving geometric separability as much as possible. Experiments on multiple benchmarks under diverse backdoor settings demonstrate that Cert-ADD consistently improves certified robustness over isotropic defenses.

title: Jointly optimizing global generalization and local personalization via prompt fusion for Heterogeneous Consumer Electronics
authors: Binbin Huang, Lingbin Wang, Ming Hu, Jin Fan, Yishan Chen, Guanjie Cheng, Shangguang Wang, Shuiguang Deng
abstract: The proliferation of intelligent consumer electronics necessitates a service architecture capable of delivering personalized services while protecting privacy. Federated Learning (FL), as a privacy-preserving paradigm, enables multiple consumer electronic devices to collaboratively train a model without exposing sensitive user data. However, FL performance (i.e., convergence performance, global generalization, and local personalization) is severely compromised by heterogeneous data distribution across consumer electronic devices, which is prevalent in most real-world scenarios. Prompt learning, as an efficient approach, is introduced to improve either global generalization or local personalization. They typically struggle to balance both directions, as optimizing one often leads to failure in another. To address this problem, we design a novel FL framework named PGA-FL, which fuses local and global prompts by Genetic Algorithm (GA) to update the local prompts on the clients and aggregates the uploaded local prompts based on their semantic similarity to update the global prompts on the server, to achieve a superior trade-off between the global generalization and the local personalization. To demonstrate the effectiveness of our PGA-FL framework, we theoretically prove its convergence performance and perform extensive experiments. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed PGA-FL framework can achieve superior performance in terms of convergence performance, global generalization, and local personalization with respect to other state-of-the-art baseline algorithms.

title: Synchronous High-Bandwidth Composite Control of Fast–Slow Coordinated Tool Servos for Diamond Turning of Functional Optical Surfaces
authors: Wei-Wei Huang, Zhiwei Zhu, Peng Huang, Tianxiao Chang, Dingkun Meng, Chilin Chen, Xuekai Shi, Jingtian Ye, Hao Wu, Xinquan Zhang, Mingjun Ren, Li-Min Zhu
abstract: Owing to its ability to overcome the inherent tradeoff between stroke and bandwidth, the fast–slow coordinated tool servo (FSCTS) has emerged for diamond turning of complex functional optical surfaces with large depth. However, in addition to the intrinsic hysteresis and resonance associated with fast tool servo (FTS) subsystems, the FSCTS architecture introduces additional challenges, such as the phase deviation and trajectory assignment, between the fast and slow tool servo (STS) subsystems. To address all these limitations, this article proposes a synchronous high-bandwidth composite control (SHCC) scheme for the FSCTS. A novel output-feedback-based damping control strategy is developed through rigorous analysis of the system dynamics model, which can circumvent the inherent order limitation in conventional output-feedback damping controllers and thus enable effective resonance suppression of high-order FTS subsystems. On this basis, a zero-phase feedforward controller is developed to compensate the phase deviations between the FTS and STS subsystems for ensuring synchronous motion. Furthermore, an integrated optimization framework for the SHCC parameters is formulated under practical design constraints, including the closed-loop stability, stability margins, and actuator saturation, to achieve optimal tracking performance. Comparative diamond turning experiments demonstrate that the FSCTS equipped with the proposed SHCC scheme achieves superior machining accuracy and efficiency.

title: Enhancing Brain Signal Generation Through A Hybrid Approach Integrating Reinforcement Learning And Diffusion Models
authors: Yang An, Yuhao Tong, Weikai Wang, Steven W. Su
abstract: Developing a reliable EEG-based Brain Computer Interface (BCI) system typically requires large and diverse training datasets, but collecting sufficient data remains challenging due to subject fatigue and interindividual variability. To address these limitations, this study proposes a reinforcement learning-enhanced EEG diffusion (RLED) framework for adaptive data augmentation in endogenous EEG tasks, with a focus on motor imagery and emotion recognition. The framework integrates a reinforcement learning mechanism to dynamically regulate the diffusion training process and achieve a flexible balance among temporal, spectral, and class-related features. Experiments on four datasets demonstrate that the proposed method generates high-quality synthetic EEG signals and consistently improves classification performance. These findings show that the proposed RLED framework may serve as a promising tool for EEG data augmentation and generalization in practical BCI applications.

title: A Stiffness-Adaptive Visuo-Tactile Robotic Palpation System for Surface Texture Profiling and Deep Lesion Localization
authors: Dongliang Fan, Chunyu Liu, Yiding Nie, Canrui Yang, Kai Qiao, Li Shao, Guanhua Liu, Xianming Feng, Hanyu Yao, Weimin Zhu, Xin Ma, Jian S. Dai
abstract: Accurate intraoperative tissue characterization relies on the ability to resolve both surface microtextures and deep-seated structures for comprehensive diagnosis. However, achieving surface profiling and deep-tissue inspection remains a critical challenge due to the difficulty of combining variable stiffness and high-fidelity perception within a compact device. Here, we propose a robotic palpation system consisting of a novel visuo-tactile sensing probe (diameter: 10 mm) integrated into a robotic platform for accurate tissue characterization. This probe exhibits adaptive strain-stiffening behavior (achieving a 470-fold stiffness variation), applicable for various surgical conditions. With our 3-D reconstruction and scanning framework, the palpation system achieves high-precision 3-D reconstruction (MAE: 28 μm in the z-axis, 3.1 μm in the xy-plane), profiling surface textures (feature size: 150 μm), and delineating the boundaries of complex deep-seated inclusions (sensitivity $ > $ 0.92). Finally, an ex vivo porcine liver model was prepared to demonstrate our system’s capability to analyze and decouple microscale superficial irregularities from macroscale lobulated deep-seated lesions under dynamic disturbances. These results confirm the system’s potential as a precise, quantitative platform for robotic surgical palpation in complex pathological scenarios.

title: AI’s 10 to Watch
authors: N/A

title: Kinematically-Constrained Marching for Optimal Reeds-Shepp Nonholonomic Path Planning on 2D Cartesian Grids_supp1-3695992.mp4
authors: Ibrahim Ibrahim
abstract: We present an effective solution for computing locally optimal Reeds-Shepp distances and paths for kinematically-constrained vehicles in environments represented as obstacle-rich 2D Cartesian occupancy grids, addressing the reliance of current methods on discretization and approximation techniques. Our solution leverages a visibility-based marching architecture with continuous analytical expressions for propagating the Reeds-Shepp distance function. We introduce a model to identify reachable and unreachable regions for Reeds-Shepp vehicles, accompanied by a comprehensive representation of the Reeds-Shepp distance function in both cases. Unlike existing approaches, our method computes locally optimal distances and smooth paths globally without discretizing vehicle orientations, motion primitives, or the PDE, and without gradient descent (GD) backtracking, ensuring both accuracy and computational efficiency. Extensive simulations in various environments demonstrate effective improvements over state-of-the-art methods, particularly in complex obstacle-rich scenarios. To facilitate adoption, we provide an open-source solver implemented in C++.

title: Iterative Moveout Correction for Seismic Gather Based on a Random Stacking Model Trace
authors: Ning Wang, Zhiwei Li, Siyuan Chen, Shan Guan, Lei Wang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: In response to the traditional full-stack method for obtaining model traces, which has poor noise and moveout tolerance, a novel approach is established to obtain model traces suitable for low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), and large moveout gathers is also proposed to enhance the quality of the gather. The algorithm assumes that individual traces in a seismic gather contain most of the waveform information, but their SNR is poor, and the waveform of the model trace obtained by full stacking is distorted due to the moveout among the traces. Research indicates that some traces exhibit small moveouts and high waveform correlation, and stacking them could lead to obtaining more accurate model traces. We perform cross-correlation calculations between each trace to obtain a correlation matrix and select the most correlated partial traces for stacking. This approach possesses good noise resistance and is applicable to large moveout data. Then, through an iterative strategy, we alternately perform moveout correction and seek the model trace, gradually increasing the number of traces involved in the stacking and random stacking iterative moveout correction. The performed tests with various models and field data validate that the proposed random stacking strategy is more robust for model trace estimation in low-SNR and large-moveout scenarios compared to conventional algorithms.

title: STH-CL: Spatial and Temporal Hypergraph Contrastive Learning for Radar Signal Sorting
authors: Xinglin Liu, Yanling Shi
abstract: Radar signal sorting is a critical step in electronic reconnaissance. To address the lack of correlation between temporal and spatial information in radar signal sorting, we propose the spatial and temporal hypergraph contrastive learning architecture (STH-CL). This architecture utilizes a Gaussian mixture model (GMM) to construct a spatial hypergraph based solely on pulse positions in feature space. It integrates the GMM prior into the attention mechanism of a lightweight Transformer to extract temporal features, thereby establishing a temporal hypergraph using K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN). The complementary contrastive learning improves hyperedge quality and enables accurate sorting. Experiments on simulated radar parameter data demonstrate that STH-CL achieves over 90% sorting accuracy.

title: Mixed Fault Diagnosis of Photovoltaic Array Based on Random Forest-Self-Training Bayesian Optimization Support Vector Machine
authors: Shaochi Wang, Lan Cao
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Various fault conditions may occur during the actual operation of photovoltaic arrays. In response to potential short circuits, abnormal aging, shadow blocking, and various mixed faults, this paper proposes a photovoltaic component fault diagnosis method based on random forest-self-training Bayesian optimization support vector machine (RF-BOSVMST). This method has the following advantages: it uses a self-training learning method to complete complex semi-supervised learning tasks with a small amount of labeled data; and it uses the random forest algorithm to predict open-circuit voltage data, which is difficult to collect. This reduces the difficulty of obtaining photovoltaic array feature data and accurately diagnosing photovoltaic array faults. The model algorithm is based on support vector machines (SVMs) and uses Bayesian optimization algorithms to optimize the hyperparameters of SVMs. Then, it uses self-training learning methods to improve the optimized model. Finally, it uses random forest algorithms to predict open-circuit voltage data, which is difficult to collect, and combines it with other fault characteristics to create a test dataset for evaluating the diagnostic model's classification performance.After being verified using simulation and experimental datasets, the method proposed in this paper was found to effectively perform photovoltaic array fault diagnosis tasks with high accuracy rates for both single-fault and mixed-fault scenarios.

title: Key Factors by which Active Distribution Networks Influence Transmission System Transient Stability
authors: Shanhua Hu, Pengfei Tian, Qing Mu, Xing Zhan, Yalou Li, Yuan Miao
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Identifying the key factors through which Active Distribution Networks (ADNs) affect transmission system (TS) transient stability is essential for accurate ADN equivalencing and for analyzing the impacts of high-penetration distributed generation within ADNs on bulk power system stability. This paper analyzes the interaction mechanisms between ADNs and the TS at different stages of disturbance evolution, and systematically identifies the dominant factors affecting the main grid at each stage. The effectiveness of the identified key factors is validated through integrated transmission-distribution co-simulation.

title: Optimization of Coal Mill Output Combination for Multi-Load Operation of the Coal-Fired Unit
authors: Zheng Jing, Yichao Zou, Yixuan Liu, Teng Wang, Bin Chen, Wei Han, Jinshi Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: To optimize the operational parameters and enhance the performance of coal-fired units, this paper constructed an optimization model for mill group output that considers the unit's combustion stability, flame stabilization capability, and energy consumption level. A one-dimensional combustion model of the coal-fired unit's boiler furnace was established to quantitatively evaluate the system's operating and energy consumption characteristics under different mill group output combinations. Meanwhile, the optimal mill group output combination across various unit loads was investigated. The research results indicate that, compared to the equalized mill group output combination, the optimized mill group output combination achieves improved combustion stability at low loads, enhanced thermal load uniformity in the main combustion zone, and reduced mill group operational energy consumption. Taking the 100 % THA operating condition as an example, the optimized output combination led to a 0.9 % increase in the average output of the lower-layer mills, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 9 \%}$</tex> reduction in the temperature nonuniformity of the main combustion zone, and a 1.6 % decrease in the total mill group energy consumption. This fully taps into the intrinsic potential of the coal mill output combination, optimizing the operational performance of both the pulverizing system and the boiler combustion system.

title: Impedance Modelling and Stability Analysis of Grid-Connected Inverters with Power Loop and Feedforward Control
authors: Guihua Liu, Kun Liu, Yousu Yao, Chaohou Liu, Lei Wang, Xinyu Wang, Ye Tao
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: With the large-scale grid connection of new energy equipment, impedance modelling has become a key method for system stability analysis. Existing research has failed to fully meet the demands of practical engineering applications, with limited analysis of multi-link joint modelling incorporating power loops and feedforward control, as well as investigations into their impedance characteristics and inter-stage coupling effects. Taking the LCL inverter as the subject of study, this paper proposes an impedance modelling method incorporating the power loop, active damping, and voltage feedforward. Models are established in both the dq-domain and sequence-domain, analysing the influence of each control module on impedance characteristics and inter-sequence coupling. Frequency mapping-based disturbance injection is employed for full-band frequency sweeping, with theoretical and simulation results demonstrating high consistency, thereby validating the model's accuracy. Stability analysis demonstrates that time-domain simulations align with model predictions, confirming the model's reliability for stability assessment.

title: Reliability Evaluation for the 110 kV Station-Level Protection System Based on Markov Model Considering Self-Checking Mechanisms
authors: Wenkai Kang, Chenggang Li, Yuxiang Yan, Jindong Guo, Huinan Shen, Xinyang Yu, Ancheng Xue
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: The reliability of relay protection systems is crucial to the safe and stable operation of substations and power grids. To overcome the limitation of conventional reliability block diagram (RBD) methods in describing dynamic fault detection and repair processes, this paper proposes a multi-state Markov reliability evaluation method for a 110 kV station-level protection system considering self-checking mechanisms. A three-state model is first established for a single protection device by distinguishing selfdetected faults from undetected faults. Then, line, bus-section, and transformer protection subsystems are modeled in a unified framework, and a state aggregation strategy is adopted to control state-space growth. Based on this approach, a 28 -state Markov model is constructed for the studied station-level protection system, and steady-state unavailability is calculated. Results show that the self-checking rate has little effect on steady-state unavailability when different fault-detection modes share the same repair efficiency. However, when self-detected faults can be repaired faster, increasing the self-checking rate significantly reduces system unavailability. The proposed method provides a practical tool for reliability evaluation and maintenance optimization of modern substation protection systems.

title: From Control to Co-Evolution: Rethinking Participation in Bio-Inclusive Design
authors: Francesca Zeccara, Elio Romanazzi, Patrizia Bolzan

title: IaC Generation with LLMs: An Error Taxonomy and A Study on Configuration Knowledge Injection
authors: Roman Nekrasov, Stefano Fossati, Indika Kumara, Damian Andrew Tamburri, Willem-Jan van den Heuvel

title: A Data-Driven Safety Risk Identification Method for Grid-Connected Energy Storage Systems
authors: Xinming Fan, Wei Jiang, Qian Liu, Bo Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: With the rapid integration of distributed energy resources, such as photovoltaic systems, energy storage units, and electric vehicle charging stations, ensuring operational safety has become increasingly important. To address this issue, a unified framework is developed to process multi-source electrical data and identify abnormal operating conditions. First, electrical measurements, including voltage, current, and temperature, are collected and preprocessed through data cleaning and time synchronization. Then, statistical feature extraction is applied to capture both steady-state and dynamic characteristics of the system. Based on the extracted features, a lightweight multi-layer perceptron (MLP) model is employed for risk identification. Experimental results on an energy storage dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an overall accuracy of 97.7%, effectively distinguishing between normal operation, overcharge, and heating states. In addition, the proposed model maintains low computational complexity, making it suitable for small-scale datasets and practical deployment scenarios.

title: Energy Consumption Analysis and Energy Saving Optimization of Air Conditioning System in Constant Temperature Laboratory of Scientific Research
authors: Di Pan, Xinying Zheng, Kaile Sun, Zhun Li
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Under the background of the promotion of the “ Dual Carbon strategy” and the energy-saving renovation of public buildings, the constant temperature laboratory of scientific research institutes has strict requirements on temperature, humidity and cleanliness, the long-term stable operation of the air conditioning system has led to high energy consumption, especially in old research institutes. Based on the real power consumption statistics, cooling load index and equipment operation parameters, this paper analyzes the energy consumption characteristics and existing problems of the constant temperature air conditioning system in a scientific research institute. The research found that the constant temperature air-conditioning power consumption of the Institute accounted for more than 50 % of the total power consumption, and the main reasons for high energy consumption were system dispersion, equipment aging and lack of regulation measures. In view of the above problems, optimization strategies such as the establishment of centralized air conditioning monitoring system, the replacement of efficient and energy-saving equipment, and the promotion of unit frequency conversion transformation are proposed, and practical cases are given. The research results can provide data support and practical reference for the energy-saving operation, refined management and energysaving transformation of constant temperature laboratories in similar scientific research institutions.

title: An Improved Transient Voltage Stability Assessment Method for the Renewable Energy Plants
authors: Ruiyu Hu, Zhonggui Gan, Xiayao Li, Zhe Zhang, Zhaowei Li, Qinglong Liu, Ancheng Xue
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: With the rapid increase in renewable energy penetration, renewable energy power plants, characterized by low inertia and weak grid support capability, have a growing impact on transient voltage stability in power systems. However, existing transient voltage stability margin indices based on multi-binary tables are not well suited for renewable energy power plants. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes an improved transient voltage stability margin index tailored for renewable energy power plants. The deficiencies of the conventional index in transient stability assessment are first analyzed. Subsequently, incorporating the low-voltage ride-through (LVRT) characteristics of renewable energy plants, an enhanced assessment index is developed. Simulation studies are carried out on a modified IEEE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex>-machine <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 9}$</tex>-bus system, and the performance of the proposed index is evaluated under different LVRT control strategies. The results indicate that the proposed index is computationally robust and physically interpretable, enabling effective quantification of transient voltage stability margin for renewable energy power plants, and demonstrating its validity and engineering applicability.

title: Memristor PID Algorithm Based on PSO-Optimized BP Neural Network
authors: Guannan Song, Ziqiao Wang, Chang Su, Wenjing Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Due to significant drawbacks of the traditional PID algorithm, this paper proposes a novel memristor PID algorithm based on PSO-optimized BP neural network. The method optimizes the BP neural network using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and combines memristor control to adjust PID parameters. A simulation model is built in Simulink. The results show that compared with traditional PID and BP-PID control, the proposed method has shorter settling time, smaller overshoot, lower voltage fluctuation, smaller steady-state error, and stronger robustness.
