title: Learning Policy-Dependent Traversability for Terrain-Aware Quadruped Navigation
authors: Chengzhen Yan, Yiming Jiang, Qingchen Liu, Jiahu Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Quadruped robots have exhibited highly adaptive locomotion capabilities, yet reliable navigation on complex mixed unstructured terrain remains a major challenge. A key difficulty is that existing navigation frameworks typically rely on geometric costmaps, which fail to capture the fact that terrain traversability is inherently policy-dependent, as different locomotion controllers exhibit distinct contact strategies, dynamic limits, and behavioral capabilities. To address this issue, we present a unified framework that learns and exploits policy-dependent traversability for large-scale autonomous navigation with quadrupedal robots. We first develop a data-driven traversability estimator that predicts the traversability of each terrain region under a given locomotion policy, trained from a kernel-based traversability sample map aggregated from simulated policy rollouts. This estimator is used to construct a terrain-aware costmap with policy-dependent feasibility constraints. Then, a two-stage planning pipeline with grid-based search and trajectory optimization is proposed to generate smooth, policy-feasible global trajectories for execution by the corresponding locomotion policy. Finally, we extend the framework to multiple locomotion policies, enabling automatic policy switching based on predicted traversability and enhancing adaptability to diverse terrain conditions. Experiments across large and challenging environments demonstrate that our approach significantly improves navigation reliability, terrain adaptability, and long-horizon autonomy for quadrupedal robots.

title: A Low-Cost MEMS-Based Vertical Inertial Navigation and Condition Monitoring System for Mineshafts
authors: A. Mölle, M. Sobczyk
abstract: Mine shafts constitute the principal bottleneck of an underground mining operation and play a critical role in ensuring safe, efficient, and continuous production. They serve as the essential interface between surface-level supply and hoisting processes and the underground extraction areas. Consequently, shafts are vital for both operations and safety. Globally, shaft hoisting installations are subject to strict safety regulations and periodic inspections. Under demanding operational conditions—such as high hoisting capacities, elevated conveyance speeds, and adverse environmental influences including high humidity, water ingress, and the transport of abrasive materials—the components of the shaft hoisting system are exposed to significant mechanical and tribological stresses. Shaft guiding structures, in particular, are continuously subjected to wear. In addition to operators’ efforts to maintain high system availability, national mining and occupational safety regulations often require regular certification and inspection by qualified or accredited experts. To assess the operational condition of rigid shaft guidance systems, a compact measurement device has been developed at the Institute of Mechanical Engineering (IMB), TU Bergakademie Freiberg. The system can be deployed without interrupting ongoing operations or requiring structural modifications to the existing infrastructure. It is primarily based on low-cost MEMS-based inertial measurement units (IMUs), including a barometric pressure sensor. A customized strapdown algorithm, incorporating additional constraint and boundary conditions, has been implemented to realize an inertial navigation system (INS) that operates entirely independent of global navigation satellite systems (GNSS).

title: Textless, Tiny, and Private: Hashed n-Grams With LLM-Teacher Distillation and Local Differential Privacy for Mental-Health Screening on Social Media
authors: Dandan Liu, Guangrui Fan, Qian Guo, Rui Zhang, Lihu Pan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: We present a textless, compute-light pipeline for user-level mental-health screening on social media that combines salted hashed character <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-grams, local differential privacy (LDP), and large language model (LLM)-teacher concept distillation into a tiny, calibrated logistic classifier. The encoder stores no vocabulary or raw text; instead, all posts for a user are aggregated on device into a salted hashed presence vector, and a subsampled randomized-response mechanism is applied once per user to provide an explicit user-level LDP guarantee for this representation. To recover interpretability without online LLMs, we invoke frozen LLMs (one for Chinese and one for English) on a small, stratified subset to label clinically grounded concepts (e.g., hopelessness, self-blame, sleep disturbance, and help-seeking); compact students are then trained on hashed features to predict these concepts across the full corpus. On two large Weibo user-level corpora, our Hashed + differential privacy (DP ) + Concepts model achieves calibrated utility competitive with a dense-embedding baseline while retaining a CPU-only footprint. Relative to a hashed baseline without concepts, we observe consistent gains in macro-AUPRC and substantially lower calibration error after isotonic mapping. Privacy audits show that, at our default user-level privacy budget, LDP plus salt rotation reduces user-level membership-inference attack success, resists reconstruction compared to embedding inversion, and substantially lowers cross-deployment linkage area under the curve (AUC) toward chance in our empirical setting. We release code and audit scripts to facilitate responsible reuse.

title: End-to-End Architecture for LLM-Based Business Process Automation: A Low-Code Integration Framework with Performance Benchmarking
authors: Rajesh Soma
abstract: Intelligent decision-making remains a major gap in traditional Business Process Management (BPM) systems, which are typically limited to rule-based workflows and require human intervention for complex decisions. This paper introduces LLMFlow, a novel low-code integration framework that connects large language models (LLMs), such as GPT-4, with the Microsoft Power Platform to deliver intelligent, end-to-end business process automation. LLMFlow was evaluated across three enterprise deployments: invoice processing, customer service automation, and supply chain optimization, achieving 92% processing accuracy, 62% faster response times, and 53% lower operational costs per transaction compared to traditional BPM solutions. The system sustained 132 requests per second, with a consistent average response time of 456 ms. To our knowledge, this work presents one of the early production-validated implementations of a multi-LLM orchestration framework that integrates predictive response-time routing with low-code workflow compilation and enterprise benchmarking.

title: Overcoming RF Hurdles in 5G/6G Satellite-Terrestrial Integration
authors: Fabrizio De Paolis, Gabriele Gradoni
abstract: The integration of satellite networks and terrestrial networks (TNs) in 5G and 6G is a significant step toward achieving ubiquitous connectivity and enabling a wide range of applications. This integration presents unique challenges for RF design, particularly in mitigating interference, accommodating components in compact antenna arrays, and maintaining the spectrum and energy efficiency of the overall network. Interference from terrestrial 5G base stations can affect satellite ground terminals, requiring innovative filtering solutions to avoid performance degradation. The compact size and integration requirements of 5G antenna arrays pose a challenge for filter design and diplexer integration. The high peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) of 5G signals necessitates efficient power amplifier (PA) designs and PAPR reduction techniques to maintain high efficiency and linearity. Wideband Doherty PAs (DPAs) have emerged as a promising solution for 5G applications, offering high efficiency over a wide bandwidth. These DPAs can be implemented in both sub-6GHz and millimeter-wave (mm-wave) bands, using single-stage or multistage architectures depending on the frequency and gain requirements. Looking toward 6G, reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) are an emerging technology that can potentially improve satellite coverage, mitigate interference, and reduce RF transceiver complexity in integrated satellite–terrestrial networks. Addressing these RF design challenges is crucial for enabling seamless integration of satellite networks and TNs in 5G and unlocking the full potential of 6G technologies.

title: Knowledge-Guided Deep Reinforcement Learning for Timely State Estimation in Wireless Embodied Networks
authors: Jiazheng Chen, Changyang She, Ping Zheng, Bohai Li, Jun Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Remote state estimation of numerous functional components is essential for the reliable and real-time operation of modern embodied intelligent agents. In this paper, we focus on solving the optimal resource allocation problem in such a large-scale embodied network with massive connected sensing devices and limited wireless spectrum resources for achieving high-quality remote state estimation. First, we derive the structural properties of the optimal resource allocation policy, including the no-waste channel structure of the optimal action and the monotonicity of the optimal value function. Next, we propose the Knowledge-Guided deep reinforcement learning (KG-DRL) algorithm by leveraging the derived structural knowledge to guide the training of the actor and critic neural networks (NNs). Specifically, a structure-guided trajectory for facilitating the actor NN learning is generated by selecting the action based on the no-waste channel structure, and the critic NN is guided to be monotonic by introducing the monotonicity violation penalty term. Numerical results illustrate that the proposed KG-DRL algorithm outperforms the benchmark DRL algorithms by about 20%-35% in terms of the estimation mean-square error (MSE) and the total power consumption, while saving the required convergence episodes by approximately 35%.

title: A Flexible Electrochemical Sensor with Sensitive and Selective Performance for Real-Sample Cholesterol Detection Enabled by PANI/Cu&#x2082;O Nanohybrids_supp1-3694112.docx
authors: Muthusankar Eswaran
abstract: Cholesterol is a crucial biomarker for cardiovascular risk assessment; however, routine measurement using enzyme-based assays can be limited by cost, complexity of immobilization, and instability under practical conditions. In this work, we fabricated a flexible, non-enzymatic electrochemical cholesterol sensor by forming a PANI/Cu₂O nanohybrid on a gold-plated polyimide substrate. The electrode was evaluated using cyclic voltammetry (CV), chronoamperometry (CA), linear sweep voltammetry (LSV), and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) in 0.1 M NaOH solution. The PANI/Cu₂O electrode exhibited enhanced interfacial charge-transfer properties, as evidenced by a lower charge-transfer resistance (Rct = 16.8 Ω) compared to pure PANI (35.3 Ω), indicating faster electron transport and improved ionic diffusion. Calibration based on electrochemical measurements at varying cholesterol concentrations yielded a sensitivity of 0.011 mA μM⁻¹ cm⁻² and limit of detection limit (LOD) 0.77 μM. The sensor demonstrated strong selectivity against common interfering species, including urea, glucose, sodium chloride, and ascorbic acid. Mechanical robustness was confirmed through bending tests at 0°, 30°, and > 45°, which showed negligible variation in current response. Furthermore, real-sample analysis in cow milk using the standard addition method resulted in satisfactory recoveries of 102.73% and 97% with low relative standard deviations (1.56% and 4.01%), confirming reliable performance in a complex biological matrix. Owing to its superior analytical performance, flexibility, cost-effectiveness, and proven reliability in real-sample analysis, the proposed PANI/Cu₂O-based electrode shows great promise for practical applications in cholesterol detection and clinical diagnostics, offering a robust alternative to traditional enzymatic assays.

title: RC-DOA: DOA Estimation Based on Residual-ConvNeXt
authors: Yuhao Chen, Lang Zhou, Jie Qi, Shaohua Hong
abstract: Direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation is a crucial task in array signal processing for applications spanning radar, wireless communications, and acoustic sensing. Despite its significance, current methodologies face persistent challenges. Traditional model-based approaches exhibit severe performance degradation under low signal-to-noise ratios (SNRs), limited snapshots, and challenging array conditions, while existing deep learning methods struggle with an inherent dilemma between model complexity and estimation accuracy. To overcome these limitations, we propose Residual-ConvNeXt DOA (RC-DOA) estimation, a novel and parameter-efficient approach designed for high-precision DOA estimation. RC-DOA innovatively leverages the hierarchical feature extraction capabilities of ConvNeXt, critically enhanced by designing cross-stage residual pathways to fuse features and stabilize gradients, enabling a lightweight yet powerful design. This architecture efficiently processes dual-channel In-phase/Quadrature (I/Q) signals without complex conventional pre-processing. Our comprehensive simulations demonstrate that RC-DOA not only significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art deep learning methods across diverse challenging scenarios, including those with low SNRs, limited snapshots, and constrained array sizes, but also achieves this while maintaining a substantially lower parameter count. This work establishes RC-DOA as a robust, parameter efficient, and highly accurate solution for practical DOA estimation, particularly in challenging operational conditions.

title: Design and Analysis of Phase Conjugation-Based Self-Alignment Beamforming for RIS-Assisted Terahertz SWIPT
authors: Jiayuan Wei, Qingwei Jiang, Wen Fang, Mingqing Liu, Qingwen Liu, Wen Chen, Qingqing Wu
abstract: Terahertz (THz) simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) is a promising technology for enabling ultra-high-rate and low-latency communications in massive battery-free Internet of Things (IoT) deployments for 6G networks. However, conventional THz systems rely on narrow directional beams that necessitate precise alignment, typically achieved through high-overhead beam scanning procedures, which fundamentally at odds with the energy constraints of battery-free IoT devices. In this paper, we propose a novel self-alignment architecture for THz SWIPT leveraging a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) to eliminate complex beam scanning. By integrating phase conjugate circuits at both the base station and user equipment, the RIS facilitates a resonance-based bidirectional retroreflection mechanism, enabling the system to autonomously converge to an aligned state without manual intervention. We develop an analytical channel transfer model and a power cycle model to characterize the resonance-assisted beam alignment process and power transfer efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate that the RIS-enabled system achieves effective spatial power concentration with significant sidelobe suppression, leading to a communication capacity of 127.84 Gbit/s and a received power of 13.62 mW over a 2.2-meter link.

title: Distributed Online Convex Optimization with Nonseparable Costs and Constraints
authors: Zhaoye Pan, Haozhe Lei, Fan Zuo, Zilin Bian, Tao Li
abstract: This paper studies distributed online convex optimization with time-varying coupled constraints, motivated by distributed online control in network systems. Most prior work assumes a separability condition: the global objective and coupled constraint functions are sums of local costs and individual constraints. In contrast, we study a group of agents, networked via a communication graph, that collectively select actions to minimize a sequence of nonseparable global cost functions and satisfy nonseparable long-term constraints, using full-information feedback and intra-agent communication. We propose a distributed online primal-dual belief consensus (DOPBC) algorithm, where each agent maintains and updates beliefs about the joint decisions and joint Lagrangian multipliers, which are repeatedly exchanged with neighboring agents. Under this belief-sharing protocol, DOPBC yields sublinear regret and cumulative constraint violation (CCV) bounds, both in O(Tmax{1–c, c}), where T denotes the time horizon, and c is a hyperparameter controlling step sizes. Such a result matches the lower bound of online constrained convex optimization when setting c = 1/2, indicating the online learning efficiency under nonseparability at the cost of communication overhead.

title: Stealth in Motion: A Doppler Shift Induced Secret Key for Securing Air-Ground Communications
authors: Qubeijian Wang, Shaojie Bai, Wen Sun, Wei Hu, Yalin Liu, Hong-Ning Dai, Zheng Yan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: The rapid evolution of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) has positioned air-ground networks as vital infrastructures for diverse applications. However, the open channels of air-ground networks remain inherently vulnerable to persistent eavesdropping threats. While physical-layer key generation (PLKG) offers a lightweight security mechanism by leveraging channel reciprocity to extract shared secrets, the inherent mobility of UAVs introduces a paradoxical tradeoff. Increased channel randomness from dynamic flight patterns enhances security through entropy amplification but simultaneously disrupts channel reciprocity, leading to key mismatch between legitimate parties. Existing PLKG schemes struggle to maintain reliability in key generation due to static channel characteristics and synchronization overhead, limiting their practical deployment in air-ground networks. To resolve this conflict, we propose a Doppler shift key generation (DSKG) scheme that systematically regulates Doppler shifts through UAV trajectory design to derive secure keys. By formulating the problem as a Markov decision process, we develop a proximal policy optimization (PPO)-clip-based reinforcement learning algorithm to dynamically control UAV speed and steering angle, ensuring robust Doppler shift reciprocity while maximizing both key entropy and generation rate. Experimental results quantify the improvements of our scheme over benchmarks in maintaining high key unpredictability and generation efficiency. Furthermore, the analysis provides valuable insights into parameter impacts, confirming the practical viability of the DSKG scheme for securing air-ground communications.

title: Maximize Quantum Network Throughput via EPS Placement and Lightweight Entanglement Routing
authors: Yangming Zhao, Qiucheng Zhu, Bingyi Liu, Nai Xia, Chen Tian, Hongli Xu, Liusheng Huang, Kun Yang, Chunming Qiao
abstract: Entanglement routing plays a vital role in supporting various applications in quantum networks. Existing works on entanglement routing either ignored the Entangled Photon Source (EPS) placement issue or simply assumed a pool of EPSes at a centralized location that can provision entanglement over arbitrary quantum links. In this paper, we propose LIGHTER and fidelity-aware LIGHTER (named F-LIGHTER) to solve the joint EPS placement and entanglement routing problem based on the assumption that EPSes are co-located with quantum nodes and each EPS can send one entangled photon at a time to one of its adjacent nodes only. The salient features of LIGHTER and F-LIGHTER include (i) LIGHTER and F-LIGHTER use a demand-agnostic EPS placement scheme to maximize network throughput and fairness for all feasible Entanglement Connection EC) establishment demands, and (ii) most requested ECs can be established over Entanglement Paths (EPs) determined offline, and only a small percentage of them will be established over online calculated EPs, resulting in fast and efficient entanglement routing. Extensive simulations show that compared with schemes without proper EPS placement or entanglement routing, LIGHTER can improve the network throughput by up to 175.6% and 37.0%, respectively. When the fidelity is considered, the network throughput improvement achieved by F-LIGHTER will be up to 135.0% and 21.5%, respectively.

title: FGAIM: Identifying Drug-Target Activation and Inhibition Mechanisms via Inductive Graph Neural Networks Based on Fine-Grained Interaction Strategies_supp1-3695928.pdf
authors: Yongxian Fan
abstract: Distinguishing the activation and inhibition mechanisms between drugs and targets can reveal the potential regulatory pathways of target functions, which is crucial in drug discovery and development. Although numerous deep learning based computational methods have been proposed, most of them extract drug and target features independently while neglecting interactions between drug molecules and protein residues. In addition, existing methods approach drugs in a relatively simple way and predominantly rely on protein sequence information. Deep learning, particularly graph neural networks (GNNs), has demonstrated unique advantages in processing data with complex graph-structured relationships. Motivated by these limitations, this study proposes a novel computational computational method named FGAIM, which designed to effectively identify activation and inhibition mechanisms between drugs and targets. First, a multi-scale GNN module in FGAIM is employed to learn expressive drug molecular embeddings. At the same time, protein representations are constructed by integrating pre-trained language model (PLM) embeddings and structural information derived from 3D conformations. Subsequently, FGAIM leverages a GraphSAGE module to extract features from both the primary drug graph and the fine-grained drug-protein interaction graph. Finally, the resulting drug and target embeddings are fused and fed into a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) for classification prediction. Comparative experiments on two public datasets demon strate that FGAIM significantly outperforms existing computa tional approaches and exhibits strong generalization capabilities. Further case studies reveal the advantages of FGAIM in min ing previously unrecognized activation/inhibition relationships. Building upon the fine-grained interactions, we further explored the model's interpretability at the molecular structural level through analysis of attention weights. Additionally, visualization analyses confirm that FGAIM effectively captures underlying structural patterns in the data and identifies discriminative features across different categories.

title: Improved Nondestructive Ultrasound Molecular Imaging with Lightweight Convolutional Neural Network_supp1-3687142.mp4
authors: Jihye Baek
abstract: Ultrasound molecular imaging (USMI) is an imaging approach that utilizes targeted microbubbles (MBs) to highlight biomarkers of disease. While differential targeted enhancement (DTE) is the current state-of-the-art for USMI, its reliance on destructive pulses hinders real-time clinical application. We have developed a neural network-based nondestructive USMI, validated in vivo using a transgenic mouse model of spontaneous breast cancer. To enhance training efficacy despite a limited animal number (N=14), we utilized several augmentation strategies including the use of several targeted MB types for each animal to generate independent image and texture patterns, alternative DTE approaches (sham and injection DTE), and random patch selection, overall resulting in a total of 15,350 patches to train the network. The resulting nondestructive USMI produces an image of the pixelwise MB classification score of the presence of targeted MBs. Our nondestructive USMI achieved a correlation coefficient of 0.954 with DTE, a continuous dice coefficient of 0.863 for a molecular signal coverage of the lesion over 20%, and a higher AUC than DTE ( 0.954 vs. 0.845 ) compared to the reference image developed from the contrast enhanced ultrasound (CEUS) image and manual lesion contour. Nondestructive imaging during continuous motion of the transducer under elevation sweeps yielded fewer artifacts and higher AUC than DTE ( 0.953 vs. 0.892 ), compared to the reference image. This demonstrates the potential of free-hand and real-time nonde-structive imaging. Overall, nondestructive imaging showed comparable performance to DTE under stationary conditions and superior performance to DTE under transducer motion, indicating its clinical imaging potential.

title: Aging Characteristics of XLPE Insulation Materials for 500 kV HVDC Cables
authors: Luming Chen, Sheng Jin
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: High-voltage direct current (HVDC) cables serve as critical components in modern power transmission systems. However, their insulation materials face aging challenges under coupled electrical-thermal-mechanical stresses during long-term operation, significantly compromising system reliability and service life. This study systematically investigates the aging characteristics of 500 kV cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) cable insulation under thermo-mechanical stress coupling. Through accelerated aging experiments under four representative conditions combined with multi-scale characterization methods, the research comprehensively reveals the aging mechanisms from microstructural evolution to macroscopic performance degradation. Electrical performance tests confirm that thermomechanical coupling markedly accelerates insulation degradation: breakdown strength decreases substantially while conductivity increases significantly, both exhibiting temperature dependence. Pulsed electro-acoustic (PEA) measurements reveal considerably enhanced charge injection and prolonged charge dissipation after aging, with particularly prominent positive charge accumulation at the anode under high temperatures.

title: Research on Anomaly Detection Scheme for Terminal Behaviors in the Power Internet of Things
authors: Shanyi Xie, Kai Zhang, Gang Zhou, Yutao Jiang, Yaogang Liu, Zhiwei Xu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Abnormal behavior detection of Power Internet of Things (PIoT) terminals is a crucial technical field for ensuring the safe and stable operation of power systems. However, one of the major current challenges is the limitations of existing models in terms of representation and generalization capabilities, which make it difficult to effectively distinguish the feature spaces corresponding to normal and abnormal behaviors. Therefore, this paper proposes an abnormal behavior detection method for PIoT terminals based on Swapped Assignments for Contrastive Learning (SwAV). This method learns the feature representation of normal behavior data through SwAV, thus capturing the feature patterns of normal behavior more effectively. In the detection phase, the data to be detected is compared with the learned normal behavior features to identify abnormal behavior information. Compared with traditional models, the proposed method has stronger discrimination and generalization capabilities, can effectively address the complex and variable problems of terminal behavior anomalies, and provides a more robust guarantee for the safe and stable operation of power systems.

title: Constant Voltage Output Control Technique for LCCS Topology Wireless Power Transfer Systems Based on Generalized Predictive Control
authors: Tingqiang Fu, Penghui Qian, Ke Li
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: This paper investigates the modeling and control of LCC-S wireless power transfer (WPT) systems under load and mutual inductance variations. WPT systems may experience output voltage instability due to external or internal disturbances. To effectively address this scenario, this study proposes employing generalized predictive control (GPC) to regulate system dynamics. This control method establishes a predictive model to optimize future control actions, thereby achieving precise control over system output. Based on the LCC-S topology, this paper constructs a transfer function model for the wireless power transfer system and verifies the feasibility of the primary-side phase-shift control strategy within this system. Simulation results fully demonstrate the robustness and precision of the developed modeling and control strategy.

title: Rebuilding Place, Reframing Identity: Participatory Visual and Environmental Tools for Intercultural and Marginalised Communities
authors: Ágnes Jekli, Erzsébet Hosszu
publish: Proceedings of the 19th Participatory Design Conference 2026, Vol. 1: Full Papers
abstract: None

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Adversarial Scatterer Generation for Attacking SAR Image Target Detectors
authors: Peng Zhou, Shunping Xiao, Siwei Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Adversarial attacks have been extensively studied in the context of synthetic aperture radar (SAR) target recognition. Recent studies has further revealed that SAR target detection models are also vulnerable to adversarial examples. However, most existing attack methods targeting detection models rely on gradient-based optimization strategies, which introduce global pixel-level perturbations without considering physical realizability. To address this issue, this work proposes a reinforcement learning-based adversarial scatterer generation method (RL-ASGM) that incorporates SAR imaging domain knowledge. The core idea is to deceive detectors by strategically superimposing carefully designed adversarial scatterers within target regions. The RL-ASGM framework consists of two key components: a reinforcement learning-based adversarial scatterer parameter optimizer (RL-ASPO) and an agent-cascaded black-box querying framework (ACQF). For RL-ASPO, the attributable scattering center model (ASCM) is adopted to characterize the scattering behavior of typical geometric structures, and reinforcement learning algorithms are utilized to train agents searching for effective scatterer parameters. To address multi-target scenario, ACQF employs a cascaded attack strategy that assigns a dedicated agent to each target. These agents iteratively query the detector and sequentially generate and deploy adversarial scatterers. Experimental results on the SAR-AIRCraft-1.0 and SIVED datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an average attack success rate exceeding 80% across various mainstream detectors, while occluding no more than 65 pixels per image, validating its effectiveness and superiority. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/bobp26/RL-ASGM</uri>.

title: A Heterogeneous Crowd Agents Collaborative Scheduling Method for Complex Spatiotemporal Environments
authors: Yimeng Liu, Zhi Chen, Zhiwen Yu, Jiangyan Xu, Bin Guo
abstract: The emergence of the concept of heterogeneous crowd sensing and the development of multi-agent reinforcement learning technology have brought new ideas for achieving low-cost, high-coverage, and real-time target tracking in complex spatiotemporal environments. In order to deal with the challenges faced by collaborative scheduling of heterogeneous crowd agents in complex environments, such as the lack of correlation between the crowd agents and the environment, and the complexity of the crowd agent collaboration modes caused by heterogeneous functional attributes, we propose a collaborative scheduling framework for cooperative competitive games based on hybrid Markov games. Firstly, we construct a correlation modeling framework for complex spatiotemporal regional environments and heterogeneous crowd agents. Secondly, we design a multi-level Markov collaborative scheduling algorithm assisted by environmental information enhancement. Crowd sensing information enhances the information transparency in incomplete information games, and the alliance formation and dissolution mechanism helps to give full play to the functional characteristics of heterogeneous crowd agents. In addition, we design a reward function containing multi-dimensional reward information. After comprehensive evaluation, the proposed method achieves higher cumulative reward, faster loss convergence, and shorter tracking time compared with baseline methods. The correctness and effectiveness of the model are verified through heterogeneous proportion analysis of crowd agents and comparative analysis of core module ablation. Experimental results show that the model can reduce tracking time and improve resource utilization efficiency in complex environments.

title: Discriminative Knowledge Fuzzy Transfer Learning Guided by Resting-state EEG for Cross-subject Emotion Recognition
authors: Gang Luo, Na Chu, Lixian Zhu, Chengcheng Zheng, Dixin Wang, Kun Qian, Xiaowei Li, Jingxin Liu, Shuting Sun, Bin Hu
abstract: Cross-subject emotion recognition remains a challenge due to inter-subject variability, which limits the generalized ability of models to unseen subjects. Existing studies commonly rely on tasking-state EEG data from the target subject for adaptation, which requires additional emotion-elicitation experiments and limits practical deployment. Motivated by findings that resting-state EEG can reflect individual-specific neural characteristics, this study proposes a Discriminative Knowledge Fuzzy Transfer Learning Guided by Resting-state EEG (DKFTL-R) for cross-subject emotion recognition without requiring tasking-state EEG data from the target subject. First, resting-state EEG is leveraged to characterize subject-specific neural signatures, by which source-domain selection is informed. Second, an emotion-style projection alignment module is introduced, in which discriminative emotional knowledge and domain-specific style are integrated via adaptive weighting so that a more transferable representation is obtained. Finally, a Takagi-Sugeno-Kang fuzzy classifier is employed to perform fuzzy inference on the transferable representation. Experiments are conducted on DEAP and DENS datasets, where accuracies of 58.79%, 55.89%, 62.91%, and 60.42% are achieved, respectively, demonstrating competitive performance compared with popular and recent baseline methods. To evaluate practical applicability and deployability, the proposed method is conducted on a self-constructed emotion EEG dataset (BHE-EMO), and it achieves 67.00% accuracy for two-class classification and 44.92% for three-class classification tasks, further demonstrating its effectiveness and engineering potential in real-world settings. In conclusion, we propose a new perspective on cross-subject emotion recognition by integrating resting-state EEG information with fuzzy modeling. This study also introduces a new calibration paradigm for affective brain-computer interface systems.

title: TACOS: A Topology-Aware Cooperative Multiagent Framework for Power-Computing Coscheduling in Data Center Microgrids
authors: Yiling Zhang, Yujian Ye, Jianxiong Hu, Chenye Wu, Tianxiang Cui, Goran Strbac
abstract: The rapid expansion of data center (DC) clusters has led to a significant increase in electricity consumption and carbon emissions, making power-computing coscheduling a critical enabler for sustainable DC operation. Large-scale AI services introduce massive and bursty workloads with complex task dependencies, which pose substantial challenges to minute-level scheduling in DC microgrids integrated with renewable energy and energy storage. These challenges are further aggravated by multidimensional uncertainties, including stochastic job arrivals, volatile electricity prices, and photovoltaic generation, as well as by stringent quality-of-service requirements and time-coupled computation-energy constraints. Existing analytical optimization and single-agent learning methods are often limited by poor scalability, weak topology awareness of job structures, and insufficient safety guarantees under temporally coupled constraints. To address these issues, this article proposes a topology-aware heterogeneous multiagent deep reinforcement learning framework for dynamic power-computing co-optimization in DC microgrids. Specifically, the problem is formulated as a constrained Markov Game to characterize interactions among scheduling and energy management entities. A Graphormer-based representation module is developed to encode directed acyclic graph job topologies and capture task dependencies. Moreover, constrained heterogeneous-agent proximal policy optimization with hybrid advantage decomposition and sequential updates is designed to handle temporal coupling constraints and improve the stability and safety of policy learning. Experimental results under realistic AI workload and energy scenarios demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively reduce energy costs while satisfying task deadline constraints, and exhibits strong scalability and robustness in large-scale DC microgrids.

title: Stacked Intelligent Metasurface–Assisted Fluid Antenna Systems: Outage Probability
authors: Anastasios Papazafeiropoulos
abstract: Stacked intelligent metasurfaces (SIMs) and fluid antenna systems (FAS) are emerging technologies for wave-domain and spatial signal manipulation, respectively. This letter proposes a novel joint SIM–FAS communication model in which transmission and reception are performed by a SIM and an FAS, respectively. Using the block-diagonal matrix approximation (BDMA), a closed-form expression for the outage probability is derived, and the SIM phase shifts are optimized to minimize outage. Numerical results validate the analytical accuracy and demonstrate substantial performance gains over conventional benchmark schemes.

title: Hybrid multiplexing demodulation system based on AWG theory for high-capacity FBG sensing network
authors: Yuexin Li, Zheng Lv, Jingjing Liao, Xiaoping Lou, Lianqing Zhu
abstract: This letter presents a high-capacity demodulation system for large-scale fiber Bragg grating (FBG) sensor networks, overcoming the limitations of conventional arrayed waveguide grating (AWG)-based architectures through a hybrid wavelength-division/time-division multiplexing scheme. By expanding the AWG to 32 output channels across the C-band and incorporating a 1×8 MEMS optical switch for time‑division multiplexing, the system achieves pseudo-synchronous demodulation of 128 FBG sensors across eight channels at a rate of 250 Hz, with integration enhanced via gold-wire bonding and direct coupling. A convolutional neural network (CNN)-based demodulation algorithm was used to accurately map the 32-channel optical intensity inputs to FBG center wavelengths, achieving high-precision regression. Experimental results demonstrate a wavelength demodulation stability of approximately 0.4 pm and an accuracy of 1.2 pm. The system exhibits a balanced performance in capacity, speed, and accuracy, making it suitable for demanding large-scale sensing applications in structural health monitoring and aerospace engineering.

title: Signal-Injection-Free Online Identification of Temperature-Dependent Electrical Parameters for SPMSM Drives: An Alternating Dual-Adaline Approach
authors: Chengbo Yang, Wei Liu, Bao Song, K. T. Chau
abstract: Temperature-dependent electrical parameters, i.e., stator resistance and rotor flux linkage, are crucial for the control performance optimization and thermal safety management of surface-mounted permanent magnet synchronous motor (SPMSM) drives. In this paper, an alternating dual-Adaline observer network (DAON)-based method is proposed to enable the signal-injection-free online identification of rotor flux linkage and stator resistance. It employs a cyclically operating structure consisting of two DAONs, which are designed for alternating activation and mutual updates, thereby circumventing the rank-deficient issue under insufficient excitation conditions and counteracting the inverter nonlinearity. Each DAON incorporates two Adaline-type sub-observers, both of which feature a tailored self-regulating learning rate designed via Lyapunov stability theory. Such a learning rate design not only guarantees the asymptotic convergence of the identification error but also provides an explicit selection range for the to-be-adjusted parameter, greatly enhancing engineering reliability and practicality. Simulations, as well as real-time experiments conducted on a 1.2-kW SPMSM drive system, demonstrate that the presented method is capable of delivering high-accuracy identification for the two temperature-dependent parameters, irrespective of the excitation level.

title: Principled, Model-Free State Estimation: From Constrained Smoothness to Hierarchical Energy
authors: Yiyong Sun, Yanbo Li, Feng Yin, Pooi-Yuen Kam
abstract: This work considers state estimation under challenging epistemic constraints: limited training data and no model information. To address these challenges, a hierarchy of energy functions inspired by first-order smoothness is introduced to quantify state variations along the sequence, serving as multi scale surrogates for unknown model information. We prove that each nth-order energy asymptotically converges to a linear combination of autocorrelations up to lag n, establishing a fundamental energy-model correspondence. This theoretical, Life Fellow, IEEE examples include dynamic sensor states in sensor networks [5] and time-varying wireless channels [6]. insight motivates us to define theoretical Ecaln estimators by simultaneously enforcing exact energy constraints up to order n, which asymptotically recover the global performance bound achieved by the classical model-based method with full model information. Next, we develop practical ParetoHn algorithms that activate these energy constraints progressively under a hierarchical Pareto-optimal framework, with provable conver gence and near-Ecaln accuracy at reduced complexity. Extensive experimental results in both wireless communication and general time series settings demonstrate that ParetoHn closely matches the Ecaln benchmark at low computational cost and adapts seamlessly to varying SNR, volatility, and sequence length. We also observe that Ecaln outperforms the classical model-based method on shorter sequences by exploiting refined local structure, while the model-based method regains superiority on longer sequences as global model information becomes dominant.

title: Urban Power Grid Load Forecasting and Analysis Based on BO-AM-CNN-KAN and SHAP
authors: Chunyu Qu, Jinfu Teng, Yan Li, Xiuping Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Traditional short-term load forecasting methods have problems such as difficulty in optimizing hyperparameters, insufficient prediction accuracy, and lack of interpretability. This article proposes an interpretable load forecasting framework based on Bayesian optimization (BO), Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Attention Mechanism (AM), Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN), and SHAP to address these pain points. Firstly, a Gaussian surrogate model is constructed using BO to perform multiple rounds of prior calculations and iteratively output the optimal hyperparameters. Secondly, a residual structure composed of CNN and AM was used to efficiently extract load features. Then, KAN outputs load prediction results based on the reconstructed feature components. Then, load characteristic analysis is conducted through SHAP, and the analysis results can be used as a reference for power industry workers. Finally, taking the power grid load data of a certain city as an example, the effectiveness of this study is verified.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)

title: RF-Sputtered CoFeB Films with Switch-Like Magnetic Response for Wireless Power Transmission in Implantable Devices
authors: Laura Mazón-Maldonado, Roghaieh Parvizi, Hadi Heidari
abstract: CoFeB is a bioinert soft magnetostrictive material, with a switch-like behaviour, useful as transducers for implanted medical devices and wireless power transmission. CoFeB thin-films were investigated in order to determine the influence of three key parameters: film thickness, the application of a magnetic field during deposition, and substrate roughness. CoFeB layers were deposited with different thicknesses and characterised by COMSOL modelling, vibrating sample magnetometry (VSM) and magnetic force microscopy (MFM). A general improvement in magnetic properties was observed by increasing the film thickness, concurrently to a decrease of the boron content, which increased the uniaxial magnetic anisotropy. An enhancement was observed too by the application of a magnetic field for the sputtering of films of 226nm. Notably, 100nm films deposition on rough PET substrates was found to be the most impactful factor in altering the material's ferromagnetism.

title: UAV-Aided Mmwave Massive MIMO Broadcasting With Rotatable Antenna Array
authors: Xuzhong Zhang, Lin Xiang, Jiaheng Wang, Xiqi Gao
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-assisted millimeter-wave (mmWave) massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) broadcasting provides a promising technique for efficiently delivering common information in next-generation wireless networks. However, most existing studies on UAV mmWave MIMO broadcasting assume isotropic antennas with fixed orientations, neglecting the directional radiation patterns of practical mmWave antennas. This results in undesired beam misalignment and limits transmission efficiency. To overcome these performance bottlenecks, this paper proposes a novel massive MIMO broadcasting scheme based on a rotatable antenna array (RAA) mounted on a UAV base station (BS). Through rotation, the RAA can not only steer main lobes toward users to improve signal reception, but also enhance channel correlations to enable more effective broadcast beamforming. To fully harness the potential of the RAA, we first develop a low-complexity joint design of the broadcast beamformer and RAA orientation for a finite number of antennas in the RAA. We further show that users with identical azimuth cosines have colinear channels, whereas other users' channels become asymptotically orthogonal when the RAA has a large number of antennas. This property enables us to derive the asymptotically optimal broadcast beamformer in closed form. More importantly, we reveal that unlike fixed antenna orientations (FAOs), the RAA can enable favorable channel alignment, allowing some users' channel vectors to become colinear, which promotes efficient beam sharing and improves broadcasting performance. With these insights, we derive the optimal RAA orientation for isotropic antennas and propose a low-complexity orientation design for practical directional antennas. Simulation results show that broadcasting systems utilizing RAAs significantly outperform those with non-rotatable antennas, both for isotropic and directional antennas.

title: Multimodal Mixture-of-Experts for ISAC in Low-Altitude Wireless Networks
authors: Kai Zhang, Wentao Yu, Hengtao He, Shenghui Song, Jun Zhang, Khaled B. Letaief
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is a key enabler for low-altitude wireless networks (LAWNs), providing simultaneous environmental perception and data transmission in complex aerial scenarios. By combining heterogeneous sensing modalities such as visual, radar, lidar, and positional information, multimodal ISAC can improve both situational awareness and robustness of LAWNs. However, most existing multimodal fusion approaches use static fusion strategies that treat all modalities equally and cannot adapt to channel heterogeneity or time-varying modality reliability in dynamic low-altitude environments. To address this fundamental limitation, we propose a mixture-of-experts (MoE) framework for multimodal ISAC in LAWNs. Each modality is processed by a dedicated expert network, and a lightweight gating module adaptively assigns fusion weights according to the instantaneous informativeness and reliability of each modality. To improve scalability under the stringent energy constraints of aerial platforms, we further develop a sparse MoE variant that selectively activates only a subset of experts, thereby reducing computation overhead while preserving the benefits of adaptive fusion. Comprehensive simulations on three typical ISAC tasks in LAWNs demonstrate that the proposed frameworks consistently outperform conventional multimodal fusion baselines in terms of learning performance and training sample efficiency.

title: FS-CrossMamba: Dual-Branch Frequency-Guided Spatial and Spectral-Attention Driven Spectral Cross-Mamba Network for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Tianxiang Zhang, Gongcai Zhang, Wenxin Jia, Yuxin Zeng, Mengting Hou, Jiangyun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral Image Classification (HSIC) is intrinsically constrained by insignificant inter-class discrepancies and high spectral redundancy. Although Mamba-based methods have appeared recently, current techniques still struggle to properly address the minor differences between classes and the substantial spectral redundancy present in HSIC. To overcome these issues, we introduce the Dual-Branch Frequency-Guided Spatial and Spectral-Attention Driven Spectral Cross-Mamba Network (FS-CrossMamba). Unlike conventional methods that estimate state transition parameters solely from the input sequence, our method explicitly injects auxiliary priors into the SSM state transition parameters through two specialized branches: the Frequency-domain-guided Spatial Cross-Mamba (FSCM) branch, exploiting amplitude and phase priors to guide spatial selective scanning and preserve structural coherence, and the Spectral-attention-driven Spectral Cross-Mamba (SASCM) branch, utilizing global spectral attention to reweight spectral responses and strengthen long-range dependency modeling. Furthermore, we design a Triple-branch Convolutional Adaptive Spectral–Spatial Coupling Module (TCASSCM) to model rich multi-scale spectral–spatial relationships as the initial embedding. Comprehensive experiments on five benchmark datasets demonstrate that FS-CrossMamba reliably achieves state-of-the-art results in comparison with topperforming CNN-, Transformer-, and Mamba-based approaches.

authors: N/A

title: ISAR Technique for Inland Waterway Surveillance Using Spaceborne Single-Channel SAR Data
authors: Ilaria Nasso, Fabrizio Santi, Debora Pastina
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: This paper proposes an inverse synthetic aperture radar (ISAR) technique specifically tailored for inland waterway surveillance using single-channel spaceborne SAR data. The approach delivers well-focused vessel images on a homogeneous plane, enabling reliable size estimation and supporting automatic target recognition. In addition, it jointly estimates vessel kinematic parameters, including speed and heading, and corrects azimuthal displacement to recover the actual vessel position. A key novelty lies in exploiting the elongated structural characteristics of ships for their positioning without relying on along-track interferometry, multi-platform acquisitions, or wake detection. Comprehensive theoretical and numerical performance analyses are presented under different use cases. The proposed method is further validated using real SAR data acquired over different waterways and vessel types, demonstrating robust performance and practical applicability across a wide range of operational scenarios relevant to inland traffic.

title: An Improved Lightweight YOLOv8 Model for Frozen Dumplings to Surface Defect Detection
authors: Baoyong Guo, Du Xiong, Yuping Wang
abstract: Surface defect detection in industrial scenarios still faces many challenges, such as small defect size, weak texture, high similarity, and the need for real-time performance. To address these issues, this paper proposes a lightweight defect detection method based on the improved YOLOv8 framework, called D-YOLOv8. Firstly, a lightweight heterogeneous structure is introduced into the backbone network, replacing some standard convolutions with GSConv, reducing the number of parameters while maintaining feature representation capabilities. Secondly, in order to enhance the perception of minute defects, a four-scale feature fusion structure, BiFPN, was constructed. A high-resolution feature layer, P2, is introduced to achieve efficient feature fusion. Finally, an attention mechanism module CA and a dual IoU threshold decoupling mechanism are integrated into the detection head to optimize localization and classification tasks, improving robustness in high-similarity scenarios. Experimental results on a self-built frozen dumpling defect dataset and a publicly available PCB dataset show that the proposed method achieves 93.8% mAP, outperforming several mainstream detection models, while maintaining high inference speed and low model complexity. The proposed method provides an efficient and practical solution for real-time industrial defect detection, especially suitable for small targets and samples with high similarity.

title: Reflection–Transmission Guided Distorted Born Iterative Method for 1-D Plasma Inverse Profiling
authors: Shaimaa E. Elghetany, M. A. Maisto, G. Sorbello, R. Solimene, L. Di Donato
abstract: This paper proposes an iterative strategy for one-dimensional slab inverse profiling using reflection and transmission data. The method exploits transmission measurements to estimate the average contrast level and reflection measurements to identify the slab support and dominant discontinuities, providing a reliable initialization for a distorted Born iterative scheme. At each iteration, the internal field and the associated Green’s function are updated through a forward solver, and the contrast profile is refined by solving a linearized correction problem. The iterations are terminated when the relative residual error on the scattered field falls below a prescribed threshold. Numerical simulations demonstrate accurate reconstructions for representative piecewise-constant and piecewise-linear profiles. The approach is assessed on cold-plasma slabs in both lossless and lossy dispersive regimes, including a nested two-step inversion to retrieve the physical parameters of the lossless plasma model.

title: Participatory Design for Intergenerational Dialogue: How the Voice® Model Can Foster a Longevity Society in the Italian Context
authors: Camilla Borghi, Beatrice Ferrarini, Valeria Leonardi, Catherine Butcher, Adam Cosheril, Lynne Corner, Nic Palmarini

title: Research on Backup Shutdown Design for Modular Multi-Purpose Small Modular Reactor Against Cyberattacks
authors: Mengyue Sheng, Chen Zhao, Shuo Wang, Suyang Yan, Yue Guo, Peng Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Although digital technologies have improved the operational efficiency of modular multi-purpose small modular reactors (SMRs), they also introduce cyberattack risks. To ensure safe operation, this paper studies the backup shutdown design in accordance with relevant nuclear safety requirements, targeting scenarios under cyberattacks during power operation, and adheres to the minimization design principle. The design clarifies cyberattack assumptions and functional requirements for backup shutdown, and develops a main control room + local operation strategy with detailed backup shutdown and information indication procedures. Meanwhile, a non-digital hardwired cybersecurity alarm for cyberattacks is designed. For backup shutdown–related equipment and monitoring parameters involving digital processing circuits, improvements are adopted, including adding analog signal paths, installing manual hard operators and indicators, and replacing isolation modules. Related control is implemented through relay or pure analog backup drive modules. The scheme ensures reliable reactor shutdown and controllability under cyberattacks, providing an effective backup shutdown solution for modular multi-purpose SMRs against cyber threats.

title: Research on Joint Clearing of Energy and Regulation Markets with Renewable Energy Participation
authors: Xi Tang, Hongbin Sun, Zhenyu Duan, Qiuchen Shen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: To examine the market clearing characteristics associated with renewable energy participation, this paper develops a joint clearing model for the energy market and frequency regulation market with renewable integration. The model minimizes the total system operating cost while considering unit output limits, ramping constraints, frequency regulation capacity constraints, and power balance requirements. Under a unified joint clearing framework, two representative scenarios, namely a thermal power-only system and a renewable-thermal hybrid system, are constructed for comparative case analysis. The results indicate that, under the tested settings, renewable energy participation is associated with differences in system operating cost, unit dispatch, and frequency regulation capacity allocation, which further lead to different market clearing outcomes. This study provides a case-based reference for understanding the coordinated participation of renewable energy in electricity markets.

title: An Approach Towards AC Power Loss Reduction in Additively Manufactured Winding for Electrical Machine
authors: Zahoor Ahmad, Ants Kallaste, Muhammad Usman Naseer, Toomas Vaimann, Shahid Hussain
abstract: Metal additive manufacturing enables the possibility of designing an optimized profile-shaped winding, which significantly enhances the performance of an Electrical Machine (EM). However, due to the large conductor cross-section, the state-of-the-art additively manufactured coils are more susceptible to high-frequency Alternating Current (AC) power losses. This paper focuses on AC power losses reduction while maintaining the constraints, such as conductor area and cooling channel dimensions. A hollow-shaped conductor is initially designed, and high-frequency losses are analyzed. Subsequently, a U-shaped conductor is designed using the same cooling channel dimensions for comparison. The performance of the U-shaped conductor in terms of AC power loss reduction is examined. Thereafter, to achieve further reduction in AC power losses, a new arrangement is proposed by modifying the conductor orientation. The proposed conductor arrangement enables a 12% reduction in conductor height by adopting a U–inverted-U configuration within the slot. This decrease in conductor height improves the current density distribution by reducing non-uniformity and lowering the maximum current density at high frequencies. A detailed comparative analysis shows that the proposed design, at an operating frequency of 1 kHz, exhibits reduced total power losses by 44% and 20.3% compared to the hollow-shaped and U-shaped conductor layouts, respectively.

title: Learning From Crowds With Multiple Feature Dynamic Fusion-Based Annotation Generation
authors: Jing Zhang, Zhi Zheng, Xiaoqian Jiang, Victor S. Sheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: As the scale of data grows for machine learning, annotating data accurately is extremely time-consuming and with high economic costs. To alleviate this dilemma, crowdsourcing has been widely used for data collection and annotation. Learning from crowds gradually becomes a popular machine learning paradigm, where learning models are trained with datasets labeled by different crowd workers. Therefore, it inevitably introduces label noise, which seriously degrades the performance of models. Previous research reveals that modeling the process of generating crowdsourced labels is a powerful tool to solve label noise. However, the sparsity of crowdsourced labels in practice hinders the effectiveness of modeling the capabilities of workers. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel Multi-Feature Dynamic Fusion Annotation Generation Network (MFDFAGen-Net). The proposed model can dynamically rectify the learning process by using the correlation between noise transition and instance dependency, which enables the learned worker-capability and instance-rectification confusion matrices to optimize with each other. In addition, two confusion matrices are fused with both worker and instance features to enhance the expression of the capability of workers. Theoretical analysis indicates that MFDFAGen-Net can better learn the reliability and bias of instances and workers, providing more fine-grained knowledge. Extensive experimental results on a variety of synthetic and real-world datasets consistently also show that the proposed MFDFAGen-Net significantly outperforms many state-of-the-art methods.

title: A Sliced Learning Framework for Online Disturbance Identification in Quadrotor SO(3) Attitude Control
authors: Tianhua Gao, Masashi Izumita, Kohji Tomita, Akiya Kamimura
abstract: This article introduces a dimension-decomposed geometric learning framework called Sliced Learning for disturbance identification in quadrotor geometric attitude control. Instead of conventional learning-from-states, this framework adopts a learning-from-error strategy by using the Lie-algebraic error representation as the input feature, enabling axiswise space-decomposition (“slicing”) while preserving the SO(3) structure. This is highly consistent with the geometric mechanism of cognitive control observed in neuroscience, where neural systems organize adaptive representations within structured subspaces to enable cognitive flexibility and efficiency. Based on this framework, we develop a lightweight and structurally interpretable Sliced Adaptive-Neuro Mapping module. The high-dimensional mapping for online identification is axially “sliced” into multiple low-dimensional submappings (“slices”), implemented by shallow neural networks and adaptive laws. These neural networks and adaptive laws are updated online via Lyapunov-based adaptation within their respective shared subspaces. To enhance interpretability, we prove exponential convergence despite time-varying disturbances and inertia uncertainties. To the best of the authors' knowledge, sliced learning is among the first frameworks to demonstrate lightweight online neural adaptation at 400 Hz on resource-constrained microcontroller units, such as STM32, with real-world experimental validation.

title: A Bayesian Game-Theoretic Framework for False Data Injection in Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Valeria Bonagura, Leonardo Badia, Chiara Foglietta, Federica Pascucci, Stefano Panzieri
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: We use Bayesian game theory to model the strategic interplay of a transmitter in a cyber-physical system (CPS) that is under false data injection (FDI) by a malicious agent, whose characteristics are partially known. The transmitter aims to minimize age of incorrect information (AoII), whereas the malicious agent seeks to maximize it. The uncertain representation of the transmission costs of the malicious agent complicates the equilibrium. Existing studies on false data injection in CPS largely assume complete knowledge of the adversary or concentrate on detection mechanisms. However, the problem of how incomplete information affects information timeliness—measured by the AoII—remains unresolved. We show how the attack can be effectively contained by reacting to the expected value of injection costs. The novelty of our approach lies in combining the AoII metric with Bayesian game-theoretic modeling under incomplete information to derive optimal defense strategies against FDI attacks. More in general, we provide insights into strategic countermeasures to mitigate the impact of malicious interventions and enhancing the overall resilience of CPSs.

title: Correction to “Enhanced UPQC Control Scheme for Power Quality Improvement in Wave Energy Driven PMSG System”
authors: Hafiz Ahmed, Doğan Çelik
