title: Frequency-Aware Decision Transformer for Offline Policy Learning in Adaptive Twin Control
authors: Cao Han, Ran Chen, Shuaizhen Yao, Chunyan Xu, Xiaowei Mei
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Adaptive twin control provides a promising paradigm for learning and evaluating control policies using historical trajectories and simulated system responses, especially when direct interaction with physical systems is costly or unsafe. In this paper, we focus on offline policy learning as an algorithmic component in adaptive twin control pipelines, rather than constructing a full-stack vehicular digital twin system. To improve trajectory representation learning under noisy, heterogeneous, and non-stationary offline data, we propose a Frequency Decision-Making Transformer (FDMT). Built upon the MetaFormer framework, FDMT introduces two complementary modules: Time-Aware Diverse Trend (TDT), which captures local temporal trends and mitigates noisy or suboptimal behaviors, and Frequency Periodic Tracking (FPT), which exploits frequency-domain attention to encode long-term periodic and structural dependencies in trajectory sequences. By integrating time-domain trend modeling and frequency-domain dependency learning, FDMT enhances the robustness of return-conditioned policy learning. Experiments on standard D4RL MuJoCo continuous control benchmarks demonstrate that FDMT consistently improves normalized returns over representative offline reinforcement learning baselines. These results suggest that frequency-aware offline policy learning can serve as a useful decision-making module for adaptive twin control scenarios, while high fidelity vehicular digital twin deployment is left for future work.

title: CIPHERBD: A Backdoor Defense Towards Privacy Preserving Neural Network Training
authors: Tanren Liu, Zhuzhu Wang, Xin Kang, Yang Liu, Yilong Yang, Zhihong Liu, Zhuo Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The privacy-preserving neural network (PPNN) utilizes cryptographic techniques such as Secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC) to enable collaborative model training while maintaining data confidentiality. However, PPNN does not address data content security, leaving it vulnerable to backdoor attacks where maliciously crafted data samples introduce triggers that manipulate model behavior. Existing backdoor defense mechanisms typically rely on computationally intensive gradient based optimization or require high computational precision, making them impractical in the MPC context. To address these challenges, we propose a novel backdoor defense mechanism for PPNN, termed CIPHERBD. Rather than relying on computationally intensive gradient-based optimization and model retraining, CIPHERBD reconstructs the backdoor trigger using private maximum operations and low-cost additions applied to a small subset of backdoor samples. To mitigate the reliance on high-precision computation in identifying backdoor samples through sample-level feature difference analysis, we propose a pixel-independent trigger approximation method. This technique aims to reconstruct an approximate trigger rather than the original one embedded by the adversary. For enhanced efficiency in confirming the presence of backdoors, we incorporate a feature-strength test that reduces the number of private inference queries required for detection. This optimization decreases computational complexity from quadratic to linear related to the number of classes, thereby improving scalability in multi class settings. Additionally, we optimize the core operation of our trigger approximation methodology by introducing a novel paradigm for secure maximum/minimum computations. This approach reduces communication outside security comparisons by approximately 46% compared to the traditional paradigm. We evaluate CIPHERBD using three distinct neural network architectures and three benchmark datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness against three prominent backdoor attack strategies.

title: An Unscented Information-Form Finite-Memory Approach for Simultaneous Localization and Calibration with Application to Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Dong Kyu Lee, Jung Min Pak, Du Yong Kim, Choon Ki Ahn
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) localization is typically performed through sensor fusion to leverage the complementary strengths of multiple sensors. However, establishing reliable localization redundancy with a single sensor remains fundamentally important for system reliability. In particular, external forces during flight, such as collisions, wind effects, or payload changes, induce significant discrepancies between control commands and the actual motion, making robust localization an inherently challenging task. This study explores a simultaneous localization and calibration (SLAC) algorithm for UAVs that improves localization accuracy by directly estimating the unknown external disturbances and compensating for their effects. A finite-memory SLAC (FM-SLAC) algorithm uses only recent information from a finite interval and has shown strong robustness in two-dimensional localization for mobile robots. However, in three-dimensional (3D) UAV localization, it exhibited numerical instability during the computation of the large matrix inversions included in the filter gain. To overcome this drawback, this study proposes a novel unscented information-form finite-memory approach for SLAC (UIFM-SLAC). By employing an unscented transformation (UT), an unscented finite-memory SLAC (UFM-SLAC) is developed to avoid the calculation of the Jacobian matrix and the inverse of large matrices required in the conventional FM-SLAC. Furthermore, an information form is integrated into the framework to establish the UIFM-SLAC, which reduces dependence on initial estimates, thereby eliminating numerical instabilities in high-dimensional models and mitigating accuracy loss caused by the linearization of nonlinear dynamics. Experimental comparisons with standard and state-of-the-art algorithms for robust UAV localization were conducted, and the results demonstrated the superior accuracy and robustness of the UIFM-SLAC.

title: CDRL-Det: Causal-Debiased Representation Learning for Robust Rotated SAR Ship Detection
authors: Shanshan Sun, Xiaogang Dong, Xiwen Qin
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Synthetic aperture radar ship detection faces unique challenges where coherent speckle noise and dynamic sea clutter often mimic ship scattering characteristics, leading to severe confounding bias. To address this, we propose a causal de-biased representation learning framework. Grounded in a structural causal model, we introduce a causal feature de-biasing module. By deriving a first-order Taylor approximation of backdoor adjustment, this module disentangles target semantics from environmental confounders. Furthermore, a contextual angle rectification module is designed to correct orientation biases via spatial disentanglement. Experiments on the SSDD+ and RSDD datasets demonstrate state-of-the-art performance. Notably, the proposed method boosts the average precision to 88.7% on the complex RSDD dataset and achieves a 7.9% improvement in high-sea-state scenarios, validating its superior robustness in learning de-biased ship representations.

title: Arc Tracking Resistance Tests on Polymeric Insulating Materials Using High Frequency High Voltages – An Approach
authors: Michael Kempf, Myriam Koch
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Insulation systems for a wide range of applica-tions – from energy technology and the automotive industry to aerospace – are increasingly exposed to high field strengths and high frequencies simultaneously. This can be attributed to a continuous development and increasing inte-gration of power electronic components such as wide band gap semiconductors. This paper presents arc tracking re-sistance tests on polymeric insulating materials using sinus-oidal high frequency high voltages with frequencies up to 2 MHz. The aim of the investigations is, on the one hand, to examine the extent to which the standardized test methods (IEC 61621 and ASTM D459-99) can be transferred to testing with high frequencies. On the other hand, the article focuses on the frequency dependence of the arc tracking resistance for three different materials. With regard to the test method-ology, it has been shown that an arc tracking resistance test using high frequencies cannot be carried out in accordance with the standards. This primarily concerns the specified current limiting, but also the applied voltage level, which must be adjusted for the high frequency tests. Concerning the frequency dependence of the arc tracking resistance, it can be summarized that testing becomes more critical for the materials as the frequency increases. This was demon-strated by measured parameters such as a decreasing time to failure, rising temperatures, an increasing arc penetration depth and more pronounced burn-off marks on the test sam-ples surface after testing. These observations can primarily be attributed to increased energy input with rising frequency using the adapted arc tracking resistance test method for high frequencies.

title: Fairness-Oriented Transmission Design in PASS-Assisted Backscatter Communication Networks
authors: Chenyang Du, Jing Guo, Meng Hua, Peng Liu, Zesong Fei
abstract: In this work, we investigate a pinching antenna system (PASS)-assisted backscatter communication network, where multiple backscatter devices (BDs) utilize signals from a dielectric waveguide for both energy harvesting and backscattering communication. To guarantee communication fairness among all BDs, we formulate a max-min rate optimization problem by jointly optimizing the positions of the pinching antennas (PAs) and the time allocation of the BDs. Then, by employing an alternating optimization (AO) algorithm, the original problem is decomposed into three tractable subproblems and solved iteratively. To mitigate the combined impact of large-scale path loss and small-scale phase variation in the optimization of PA positions, a two-stage optimization strategy is proposed to decouple these effects and solve them sequentially. Finally, simulation results illustrate that the proposed scheme outperforms the benchmark schemes such as the conventional multiple-input multiple-output scheme and the fixed-position scheme, and achieves nearly the same performance as an ideal scheme where small-scale phase effect is perfectly eliminated.

title: Climate Risk-averse Power System Planning Under Renewable Drought Events
authors: Haiyang Jiang, Audun Botterud, Grant Ruan, Rahman Khorramfar, Chris P. Nielsen, Ershun Du, Ning Zhang
abstract: With increasing shares of renewable energy, episodes of continuously low renewable output, referred to as renewable drought (RD) events, pose growing challenges to the long-term adequacy of renewable-dominant power systems. This paper develops a meteorology-driven framework to characterize the spatiotemporal features of RD events and represent the inadequacy risk arising from their multi-day persistence and cross-regional dependence. Based on this, a risk-aware planning model is formulated to integrate typical-day operation with RD events of varying durations in long-term planning. Two case studies on the modified Garver's 6-node system and China's East Power Grid show that increasing renewable penetration amplifies inadequacy risk under RD events, while coordinated flexibility resources—including inter-regional transmission, long-duration storage, and diversified wind–solar portfolios—can effectively mitigate such risk.

title: Online Stator Resistance Estimation of Low-Power PMSMs Using Data Driven Methods
authors: Lutfu Emre Efe, Umut Orguner, Salih Baris Ozturk, Akshay Vijayrao Deshmukh, Bilal Akin
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Accurate estimation of stator resistance is critical for the high-performance control and thermal monitoring of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSMs), particularly in sensorless drives where parameter mismatch leads to significant position errors.This work targets low-power PMSM drives, where the relatively large stator resistance provides favorable signal-to-noise conditions for the proposed data-driven estimation framework. Conventional online estimators often suffer from convergence lag, stability trade-offs, or high computational burdens. To address these limitations, this study presents a novel two-stage data-driven estimation framework. First, experimental data is collected offline using inverter and microcontroller at various operating points to construct a parametric model which indirectly covers the thermal dynamics of the machine. To identify this model efficiently, a Nested Non-Linear Least Squares (Nested NLS) algorithm is introduced. By structurally decoupling the linear and nonlinear parameters, this algorithm reduces the training computation time by a factor of 5.83 compared to standard optimization, enabling rapid self-commissioning. In the second stage, the identified model is utilized for online tracking via a computationally efficient recursive estimator that predicts resistance variations in real-time without signal injection. Experimental validation on a Texas Instruments TMS320F28069M MCU controlled low-power PMSM drive under aggressive load and speed cycles demonstrates that the proposed method eliminates estimation lag and achieves superior tracking accuracy, with a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) as low as 0.0021 Ω and a maximum percentage error maintained below 0.5%.

title: A Dual-Branch Network-Based Method for Unified Diagnosis of IGBT Open-Circuit and Current Sensor Faults in Three-Phase Inverters
authors: Jundong Zhao, Shu Cheng, Xun Wu, Weicheng Li, Dandan Wang
abstract: Inverters are extensively used in industrial automation, renewable energy, and transportation systems. Under complex operating conditions, power switches and current sensors are susceptible to failures, and their fault signatures can become confusable under closed-loop control. To address this issue, this article proposes a unified fault diagnosis approach for distinguishing insulated-gate bipolar transistor (IGBT) open-circuit (OC) faults and current-sensor faults under a single-fault-source assumption. A Fourier-based analytical model is first developed to characterize and compare the dominant harmonic features of switch and sensor faults. Then, a dual-branch neural network is constructed to perform fault-type discrimination. The model integrates a Transformer-based spectral branch and a BiGRU-based temporal branch to capture harmonic-domain relationships and waveform dynamics, respectively. Furthermore, a cross-attention fusion mechanism is introduced to enhance interaction between heterogeneous representations, improving separability in confusable scenarios. The proposed method operates using only two phase-current measurements without auxiliary voltage sensing. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively diagnoses 30 types of power-switch and current-sensor faults, achieving an average diagnostic accuracy of 98.95%.

title: Automated Design of Countermeasures for ICS Based on Security Standards
authors: Kousei Sakata, Takashi Ogura, Noritaka Matsumoto, Eriko Ando, Cristina Vera, Hideaki Ishii, Satoshi Takemoto
abstract: Industrial control systems are increasingly exposed to cyber threats, leading to the establishment of security regulations and standardization frameworks worldwide. However, selecting optimal security measures under resource constraints remains a major challenge. To address this issue, we propose the Accountable Design Framework (ADFW). ADFW consists of an Accountable Database that systematically links security requirements, threat intelligence, countermeasures, and device constraints. It also incorporates two automated optimization methods: backtracking search and a Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability (CIA)-weighted heuristic search built upon it. Both approaches are formulated as a 0-1 integer linear programming problem to enable systematic and comparable evaluation. The proposed framework efficiently evaluates requirement fulfillment and feasibility while maintaining compliance with International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) 62443. Simulation experiments demonstrate that the CIA-weighted heuristic search outperforms a naive greedy baseline under availability-focused conditions. These results confirm that ADFW enables efficient, explainable, and IEC 62443-compliant security optimization even under resource-constrained environments.

title: Dual-Stream of CNN and Transformer feature-based Cross-Attention Model for Multiclass Ocular Surface Squamous Neoplasia (OSSN) Classification
authors: Iyyappan Sagadevan, Ruban Nersisson
abstract: Effective treatment planning and management of Ocular Surface Squamous Neoplasia (OSSN) depend on early, precise diagnosis from slit-lamp images. However, a major obstacle for automated systems is the diverse appearances that OSSN categories might take, along with small clinical variances. Although deep learning methods, including hybrid architectures, exhibit promise, a major obstacle to developing an accurate multi-class classification system remains the challenge of customising a single model to capture both precise local knowledge and a broad global context. The Dual-stream cross-Attentional CNN-Transformer Network (DACTNet), a novel hybrid deep learning model designed to enhance multi-class classification of OSSN, is presented in this article. DACTNet’s cross-attention module provides a more comprehensive and distinctive representation of ocular surface diseases by combining the complementary features learned by the CNN and ViT streams. A primary dataset of multi-class OSSN images, comprising normal, benign, pre-invasive, and invasive classes, is used to evaluate DACTNet. The individual performance of the lightweight CNN and ViT models is contrasted with that of the network as a whole. According to experimental findings, DACTNet outperforms comparable models across key classification metrics, including overall accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, underscoring its superior ability to distinguish among OSSN classes. It outperformed the best current state-of-the-art models in sensitivity, precision and F1 score, achieving an accuracy of 94%.

title: A Study on the Reliability Design of Medical Device Software for Hybrid Architectures
authors: Honggang Li, Suping Zhang
abstract: With modern medical devices evolving into increasingly complex Medical Cyber-Physical Systems (MCPS), hybrid architectures that combine a non-real-time host with a hard real-time lower controller are now widely used to balance rich human–machine interaction with deterministic real-time control. This architectural split improves functionality, but it also introduces intra-device heterogeneous interaction risks. In practice, the asymmetry between host-side non-deterministic scheduling and lower-controller hard real-time constraints can lead to timing violations, data integrity corruption, and state inconsistency. Existing reliability design methods for single-controller systems do not address these issues well, while prior studies have focused mainly on inter-device interoperability or high-level safety principles and have provided limited engineering support for internal interactions in hybrid architectures. To address this gap, this paper proposes the Hybrid Architecture Reliability Design Framework (HARDF). HARDF defines three heterogeneous interaction fault models and introduces a set of defensive design patterns tailored to asymmetric interaction behavior, including asymmetric heartbeat, closed-loop command validation, and independent safety monitoring. It also incorporates a Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL)-based continuous verification mechanism to support automated fault reproduction and iterative refinement. A full-lifecycle empirical study conducted during the development of a Class III high-risk pulsed therapy device shows that HARDF helped identify and resolve approximately 75.6% of fatal/severe defects before system integration testing and reduced severe defect density during system integration testing from 0.57 defects/KLOC to 0.17 defects/KLOC, a reduction of approximately 70.2%. These results suggest that HARDF provides a practical and systematic reliability design approach for managing heterogeneous interaction risks in hybrid-architecture medical device software.

title: Multi-view Data Exploration Recipes for Making Sense of Multi-source Industrial Data
authors: Fabian Fingerhut, Elena Tsiporkova
abstract: Industrial assets, such as wind turbines, pumps or heavy-duty vehicles, produce large data traces which can be used for diverse purposes. However, making sense of real-world industrial data is challenging, as it is typically produced from different, highly heterogeneous sources, such as sensor measurements, configuration specifications or log records stemming from different components and assets. Applying off-the-shelf artificial intelligence (AI) algorithms does not necessarily yield relevant insights, as these methods struggle to handle the complexity, variability, and domain-specific constraints of the data. In this paper, we bridge this gap, by formalising three data exploration strategies into standardised workflows (recipes) for effectively analysing, integrating, and deriving actionable insights from multi-source industrial data. The benefits of using these recipes for exploring industrial data are demonstrated on multi-source datasets collected during the in-the-field operations of four different types of industrial assets: feedwater pumps, compressors, windmills, and heavy-duty trucks. Our findings underline the importance of conceiving and validating formal data analysis methodologies enabling the optimal exploitation of industrial data without subjecting each new application context to an initial trial-and-error data exploration phase.

title: Impact of Optimal Electric Vehicle Charging Station Placement on Distribution Network Performance: A Comparative Study
authors: Imad Eddine Gherbi, Ibrahim Alhamrouni, Younes Zahraoui, Nor Hidayah Abdul Kahar, Nurul Asyikin Mohamed Radzi
abstract: The integration of Electric Vehicle Charging Stations (EVCSs) into distribution networks presents significant challenges, including increased power losses and voltage instability. This paper presents an optimization framework for the optimal placement of EVCSs in radial distribution systems with existing distributed generation. The objective is to minimize total active power loss over a 24-hour period while adhering to operational constraints, including bus voltage limits and line thermal capacities. A deterministic Non-Linear Programming (NLP) model is formulated and solved using the Interior Point Optimizer (IPOPT). The methodology is validated on the IEEE 33-bus and 69-bus networks, which incorporate fixed photovoltaic and biomass generation units. The proposed deterministic approach successfully identified optimal EVCS locations that significantly reduce system-wide power losses and improve voltage stability. To validate its reliability, the proposed method is benchmarked against three popular metaheuristic algorithms: Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Genetic Algorithm (GA), and Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO). The results demonstrate that while the deterministic NLP framework entails a higher computational cost, it consistently outperforms all three metaheuristic methods in solution quality. This advantage is particularly pronounced in the larger 69-bus network, where the NLP model achieved a 47.15% reduction in power losses, whereas the metaheuristic algorithms failed to exceed 7.1% due to premature convergence. These findings indicate that, for standard radial distribution systems, the deterministic optimization framework offers a superior trade-off between solution optimality and reliability compared to stochastic metaheuristics, albeit at a higher computational cost. While the NLP model guarantees convergence to a high-quality solution, the study highlights the importance of selecting the appropriate optimization strategy based on the specific network size and available computational resources.

title: Indoor and outdoor integrated localization scheme for mobile robot using multi-sensor fusion and graph optimization
authors: Liyang Zhang, Kai Cheng, Zhiyou Pan, Lei Pan, Yang Liu, Lixia Guo, Yan He
abstract: Mobile robot navigation and localization utilizing multi-sensor fusion have attracted significant attention due to the advantages of high positioning accuracy, extensive perception range and strong system robustness. To meet the application requirements of seamless indoor and outdoor transitions and improve the accuracy of asynchronous sensor data fusion, a neural network weighted factor graph (NN-WFG) positioning method using IMU, RTK and LiDAR is proposed by combining factor graph theory and neural network model. Corresponding sensor factor node models are established according to the advantageous characteristics of different sensors, and a new multi-sensor fusion factor graph positioning model is constructed by integrating variable nodes and factor nodes. Additionally, a neural network factor weighted function is designed to optimize the trust allocation of different sensors during scenario transitions, thereby enhancing positioning accuracy and system robustness. Furthermore, an incremental smoothing optimization algorithm applying Givens transformation and QR matrix decomposition is developed to reduce computational complexity and improve real-time positioning performance. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that compared with the inertial navigation system (INS), extended Kalman filter (EKF), conventional particle swarm optimization (PSO), LiDAR-Inertial Odometry via Smoothing and Mapping (LIO-SAM) and Deep Learning-Assisted Error-State Extended Kalman Filter (DL-ES-EKF) methods, the proposed scheme can not only reduce the mean location error by approximately 40% under indoor and outdoor scenarios, but also significantly improve the computational efficiency and system robustness.

title: Indoor Smartphone Positioning Method via a Single Wall Reflection Using a Parametric Speaker
authors: Xin Luo, Masanari Nakamura, Hiromichi Hashizume, Masanori Sugimoto
abstract: With the rapid development of the Internet of Things (IoT), indoor localization has become crucial for applications such as automated manufacturing, robot navigation, and human–machine interaction. Although acoustic localization enables high-precision positioning, it has traditionally required multiple speakers. To address this, single-speaker methods have been proposed in recent years; however, they face challenges in signal detection, resulting in unstable positioning. Leveraging the speaker’s high directivity, two-dimensional positions of a smartphone are estimated from the time and angle differences between direct and wall-reflected signals. In this paper, the reflected signal is obtained from a single wall, and the Δz denotes the height difference between the microphone and the speaker. Experiments using 16–18 kHz linear chirp signals and automatic rotation from 0° to 180° showed average errors of 0.052 m (90th percentile = 0.095 m) at Δz = 0 m and 0.124 m (90th percentile = 0.278 m) at Δz = 0.2 m, indicating stable performance. When Δz = 0.4 m, signal energy decreased and localization became unstable, marking the effective range limit. These results confirm the feasibility of low-cost, high-directivity acoustic localization and support future 3D extensions.

title: Electromechanical Performance of a Soldered Stack of ReBCO Tapes at 77 K
authors: Di Wang, Qianjun Zhang, Yongqi Yang, Kan Tan, Pengjie Wang, Lang Qin, Shouzhi Wang, Binbin Wang, Rui Chen, Yi Tan
abstract: To overcome the mechanical issues of Central Solenoid (CS) coils in compact Spherical Tokamak (ST) devices with tight small winding radii, e.g., 171.7 mm, this work deals with the mechanical analysis of a stack of three tapes, which is needed for high current density and flexible coil design. Tensile, bending, and transverse compression tests were performed to analyze the behavior of the conductor performance under operating loads. It is concluded that the normalized critical current (Ic) of the stack of three tapes remains higher than 0.95 when bent with a radius larger than 20 mm, and no degradation (<5%) is found at tensile stress ≤600 MPa or compressive stress ≤400 MPa. These results validate the mechanical toughness of stack of three tapes, a necessary input for their use in high-field (up to 10 T) fusion magnets.

title: Cooperative Optimization Method for Thermal Reserve Capacity Considering Non-Curtailment of Wind and Solar Power
authors: Haiyang Wen, Lei Chen, Qizhen Yang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: This paper proposes a cooperative optimization method for reserve capacity combining thermal power and energy storage, tailored for scenarios where wind and solar power units operate under non-curtailment conditions in large-scale renewable energy bases. First, the frequency regulation capability of wind and solar units operating in maximum power point tracking mode is analyzed to determine the reserve capacity requirements under frequency security constraints. Subsequently, a cooperative optimization model for thermal-storage reserve capacity is constructed, and a multi-timescale three-layer cooperative control strategy is designed: wind and solar units operate in maximum power point tracking mode without participating in reserve allocation; the middle layer adopts a coordination mechanism where thermal power dominates secondary frequency regulation while energy storage assists in deviation compensation; in the lower-layer primary frequency regulation, energy storage provides fast support through virtual inertia and droop control, wind and solar units participate in transient response via virtual inertia, and thermal power relies on natural inertia to maintain system stability. Finally, the effectiveness of the optimization model is validated. Compared with conventional control methods, the proposed strategy enhances the system frequency security margin while ensuring non-curtailment operation of wind and solar power.

title: State of Health Estimation Based on Multi-Segment Charging Data Fusion
authors: Yuntao Zou, Zeling Xu, Zhichun Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Estimating the state of health (SOH) of batteries relies on charge-discharge data. Obtaining the current maximum available capacity requires a complete charge-discharge cycle. Existing public battery aging datasets conduct cycling tests under constant-current constant-voltage (CCCV) protocols. Energy storage batteries, however, charge and discharge at constant power following grid dispatch commands, and their charging duration is determined by grid demand. Consequently, the battery management system (BMS) cannot acquire complete charge-discharge cycle data. SOH estimation methods based on complete cycle data suffer from limited accuracy under these conditions. This paper proposes a SOH estimation method tailored for incomplete charge-discharge conditions in energy storage systems. The method jointly estimates SOH by leveraging multiple charging segments collected during operation. Experiments on the CALCE dataset demonstrate that the multisegment fusion approach achieves higher estimation accuracy than single-segment methods. In the low-voltage range, the MAPE decreases from 2.685% to 1.782%. The proposed method extracts segments from laboratory full-cycle data to simulate energy storage operating conditions, providing a pathway for transferring laboratory data to energy storage scenarios.

title: Quantitative Assessment of the Flexibility of Load Regulation in Typical Energy-Intensive Industrial Loads Considering Complex Process Constraints
authors: Xiang Zhang, Zhenyang Zhao, Jiazheng Sun, Ziang Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: At present, the regulation of flexible resources for industrial loads tends to overlook the complex internal production process constraints of the loads. There is a lack of a multi-time-scale coupled assessment system for quantifying the flexibility of load regulation. In response to the above problems, this paper proposes a quantitative assessment method for the flexibility of multi-dimensional regulation of typical energy-intensive industrial loads that takes into account complex process constraints. Taking the electrolytic aluminum load as an example, an electrolytic aluminum load model considering the thermal conductivity characteristics of the electrolytic cell is proposed; A multi-timescale regulation flexibility assessment index system was constructed, and a combined weighting method based on minimizing the deviation of subjective and objective weights was adopted to ultimately achieve quantitative scoring of the multi-time-scale flexibility of high-energy-consuming loads. The results show that the evaluation system proposed in this paper can effectively avoid the risk of over-limit, and the comprehensive quantification method based on G1-entropy weight -TOPSIS proposed can accurately depict the multi-dimensional true response potential of industrial load.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)

title: Multi-Source Data-Driven Multi-Objective Collaborative Decision-Making Method for Comprehensive Perception of Distribution Networks
authors: Guihua Qiu, Shuhong Wu, Cheng Ding
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: In response to the strong nonlinearity and control uncertainty caused by the high proportion of source and load access in the new distribution network, a multi-source data-driven panoramic perception and multi-objective adaptive collaborative decision-making framework is proposed. Firstly, a mapping model of the operating state is constructed by decoupling the spatio-temporal features, achieving a formal representation of the high-dimensional decision space considering physical security domains and equipment life constraints. Secondly, a dual-delay deep deterministic policy gradient algorithm is introduced to establish an end-to-end decision mapping mechanism from operating features to control instructions, and a reward function embedded with physical criteria is designed to guide the decision search. Finally, a sensitivity adaptive adjustment mechanism based on state feedback is constructed to realize the real-time transfer of decision preferences between the risk domain and the steady-state domain and multi-objective game decision-making. Simulation results show that this method only requires 15.2 ms for single-step decision-making, reduces network loss by 7.5%} while ensuring a 99.9% voltage qualification rate, significantly enhancing the decision robustness under complex conditions, and achieving Pareto optimal collaborative decision-making for the safety, economy and reliability of the distribution network.

title: Design and Implementation of an Intelligent Management and Control System for Substation Monitoring Information Point Tables
authors: Jianbin Qiu, Yueqing Chen, Jianhong Chen, Haomin Ran, Wei Lin, Yongyi Ding
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: In response to the problems of low efficiency, insufficient standardization and difficult data consistency in the traditional manual preparation of substation monitoring information point tables, this paper has developed an intelligent management and control system. Taking the SCD file as the sole data source, the system connects point-table generation, multilevel collaborative acceptance, station-side configuration verification, and other links to form a lifecycle management framework for substation monitoring information point tables. By integrating rule-based generation, semantic-assisted checking and adaptive rule optimization, this system improves the correctness and standardization of generated results with consistency control over design, configuration and operation. The simulation results show that the system can significantly reduce the time required for point table preparation and verification, enhance the correctness and standardization of automatically generated results, enhance both station-side configuration consistency and master-station integration consistency, and show good engineering application prospects.

title: Research and Application of Digital Twin System for Offshore Terminals of Large-Capacity Floating Wind Power
authors: Guoming Gan, Zifeng Sun, Wei Ye, Tian Jiang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Aiming at the technical challenges of complex marine environment, strong motion coupling, difficult safety control and lagging early warning in offshore terminal operations of large-capacity floating wind power, this paper develops a dedicated digital twin system. After analyzing core system requirements and clarifying research objectives, a six-layer hierarchical architecture is designed, with key technologies such as multi-source data collection, twin modeling and 3D simulation broken through. Focusing on collision early warning algorithm and mooring force monitoring model, the system realizes accurate calculation and graded early warning of key parameters. Application results show that the system achieves global real-time monitoring, multi-scenario simulation and intelligent early warning, effectively solving the problems of data islands and difficult operation control, providing technical support for safe and efficient offshore installation and important engineering application value for deep-sea wind power digitalization.

title: A Multi-lithic Finemet Toroidal Inductor with Soft Iron Powder Insertion for High-Frequency Converters
authors: Gourab Banerjee, Devraj Roy, Sunil Meti, Dibyendu Mandal, Navakanta Bhat, Srinivasrao Shivashankar, Mainak Sengupta
abstract: This paper proposes a novel multi-lithic toroidal inductor structure employing two materials of different magnetic permeabilities for high-frequency applications. Electromagnetic and thermal performance enhancement of toroidal inductors is achieved through complementary shape modifications while preserving the overall toroid form. It employs moulded nanocrystalline Finemet powder and high-purity iron powder to form the composite magnetic core. The influence of the curvature centre and radius at the material interface is analysed, followed by optimisation to identify the key design parameters. Experimental validation of the proposed inductor is performed in a lab-developed power converter and benchmarked against that of a standard toroid, demonstrating a 2% efficiency improvement in a 15 W converter and 5°C reduction in temperature rise at 1 MHz. The fabrication approach demonstrates good repeatability (±5% inductance variation) and scalability, the latter validated through simulations for a 150 W case.

title: FGO-ABRC: A Factor Graph Optimization Framework for AUV Bathymetric Residuals Calibration Integrating Multi-Source Observations
authors: Mingyi Gan, Xianhai Bu, Fanlin Yang, Yaxue Wang, Ami Hassan Md Din, Kelvin Tang Kang Wee, Bo Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: With the widespread deployment of autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) in deep-sea exploration, AUV-based multibeam echosounder system (MBES) has become an essential equipment for acquiring high-resolution seabed topography. However, the complex marine environment introduces multiple error sources, such as attitude fluctuations, bottom detection errors, and pressure bias, which lead to bathymetric errors and degrade mapping consistency. To address this problem, we propose a factor graph optimization based AUV bathymetric residuals calibration (FGO-ABRC) framework. The method first performs preprocessing to eliminate global offsets in the measurements. Then, constructs a factor graph framework that integrates multi-source observations to achieve globally consistent estimation of the AUV’s vertical state. Furthermore, multiscale overlap consistency factors are incorporated to enhance seafloor continuity and robustness by leveraging redundant observations in overlapping swaths. To validate the proposed method, experiments were conducted using in-situ AUV data. Quantitative results demonstrate significant improvements: the mean depth discrepancy across the entire survey region decreased from 0.606 m to 0.007 m, and the root mean square error (RMSE) dropped from 1.169 m to 0.230 m. The ablation analysis further validated the effectiveness of each algorithmic step. Meanwhile, the computational efficiency and the composition of the residual errors were also analyzed. The proposed method effectively enhances the vertical consistency and overall accuracy of AUV-based MBES bathymetry, and provides a scalable bathymetric error calibration framework for underwater topographic mapping through multi-sensor fusion.

title: Geometry-Dependent Radiation of Pinching Antennas: Theory, Simulation, and Measurement
authors: Haoyang Li, Weidong Liu, Zhensheng Chen, Chaoyun Song, Gaojie Chen
abstract: Most existing studies achieve beamforming by adjusting the positions of pinching antennas (PAs) and typically model PAs as isotropic radiators. However, under the dielectric scatterer model, the PA radiation pattern depends on its geometry. This letter investigates the radiation patterns of PAs with different geometries through full-wave simulations and measurements, and demonstrates how geometry influences the radiation directivity. A geometry-aware channel model is proposed to demonstrate the impact of geometry-dependent radiation on the received signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). In addition, an arc-shaped PA is introduced to enable transmit-direction control in PA systems. A PA system prototype consisting of a dielectric waveguide, waveguide transitions, and a PA element is proposed. Prototype measurements are used to validate the simulations and to characterize the directivity of square and triangular PAs, and the measurement procedure can be applied to obtain radiation patterns for PAs with general geometries. The simulation and measurement results jointly demonstrate that PA geometry is critical in PA systems because it influences the radiation characteristics significantly.

title: A Provably Secure Multifactor Authentication and Key Exchange Protocol with Anonymity for Next-Generation IoT
authors: Awaneesh Kumar Yadav, Ravi Kumar, An Braeken, Madhusanka Liyanage
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: With the rapid surge in IoT devices, communication between the IoT devices and the server becomes more frequent. Since IoT devices are considered at the edge of the networks, their communication is completely exposed to the server, making them prone to several attacks. In addition to this, IoT devices have limited energy and computational resources. Therefore, there is an impelling necessity for an authentication mechanism suitable for security and taking into account the resource constraints. This paper shows that a recently proposed protocol by Daojing et al. is prone to serious attacks such as stolen device attacks, suffers from integrity violations, and does not offer perfect forward secrecy. We propose an alternative and more secure authentication mechanism for this type of model and also show that this protocol offers better performance with respect to the state-of-the-art. The proposed protocol achieves reductions of 75%, 40%, 36%, and 71% in computational, communication, storage, and energy consumption costs, respectively. Additionally, the protocol only has two communication phases. Furthermore, prototype implementation and simulation with the NS3 tool are carried out to show the applicability of the proposed work in real-time scenarios.

authors: Mafalda Gamboa, Sofia Thunberg, Sara Börjesson, Miriam Sturdee, Sara Ljungblad
