title: Generalizable Stochastic Optimizations for UAV-Assisted ISAC Systems: A Hybrid Decision Causal Convformer Framework
authors: Yiyang Ni, Junmao Liu, Pengcheng Xia, Hui Zhang, Jun Li
abstract: In this paper, we investigate an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) assisted integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system, where reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) is mounted on and powered by the UAV. We aim to maximize the average achievable rate via jointly optimizing the UAV trajectory and beamforming at both the base station (BS) and the RIS with varying environments. While deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is capable of solving stochastic optimizations, it lacks generalization in dynamic context. The decision Transformer (DT) is then designed to solve the generalization issue of DRL. However, it shows poor performance in capturing the short range local dependency patterns that arise in trajectories governed by a Markov decision process (MDP). To address these limitations, we propose a hybrid decision causal Convformer (HDCC). We first formulate the optimization problem with the framework of MDP. Then we utilize two complementary parallel branches in our HDCC, i.e. self-attention for modeling long range global dependencies and convolution for extracting short range local temporal patterns. Next, we propose a causal block embedded in the convolution branch. This is to identify intrinsic causal relations among convolution features and convert them into an auxiliary action proposal, improving action prediction and policy robustness. Simulation results show that the proposed HDCC achieves an improvement of 9.4% in terms of average transmission rate, compared to the conventional DT.

title: An Isolated Integrated Battery Charger With Double Active Filter for Electric Vehicle Based on Open-Winding Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor
authors: Jiate Zhang, Jiadan Wei, Yingying Xie, Bo Zhou
abstract: In this manuscript, an isolated integrated battery charger (IBC) with the double active filter based on open-winding permanent magnet synchronous motor is proposed, the additional low-volume high-frequency transformer and low-frequency diodes are adopted to reconfigure the system into a single-stage isolated AC/DC converter with double H-bridge active power filter, and the corresponding basic control method is given to achieve power factor correction and grid current control. Moreover, the current cooperative control is then proposed herein to eliminate pulsating charging torque and secondary power pulsation. Finally, the simulation and experiment results are implemented to verify the effectiveness and feasibility of proposed isolated IBC.

title: Seamless Beam Handover for Direct-to-Satellite IoT in Ultra-Dense Multi-Beam LEO Networks
authors: Fei Liu, Guoquan Li, Long Xing, Jinzhao Lin, Yu Pang
abstract: As a promising infrastructure for direct-to-satellite Internet of Things (DtS-IoT) communication, ultra-dense multi-beam Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite networks can enable global connectivity for distributed IoT deployments. However, the orbital dynamics of LEO satellites induce frequent inter-beam handovers that simultaneously degrade Quality of Service (QoS) due to communication interruptions and challenge network load balancing. To address these, we propose a novel handover scheme comprising a geometry-based method for Beam Remaining Service Time (BRST) calculation in multi-beam LEO networks, and a Double Deep Q-Network (DDQN) based optimization method that adapts to LEO network dynamics. The scheme integrates BRST with estimated channel quality and idle channel availability as comprehensive handover metrics, while formulating the optimization through DDQN to maximize throughput during active sessions of DtS-IoT devices. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme outperforms existing methods by achieving higher throughput while maintaining effective load balancing characteristics, particularly benefiting resource-constrained DtS-IoT devices in remote environments.

title: Multi-Objective Parameter Optimization of Artificial-Cavern Compressed Air Energy Storage Systems via Two-Person Zero-Sum Game
authors: Xianfeng Zhang, Shengwei Mei, Xuhui Zheng, Sen Cui, Laijun Chen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Advanced adiabatic compressed air energy storage (AA-CAES) systems exhibit deep thermodynamic coupling between compression and expansion subsystems, rendering simultaneous optimization of round-trip efficiency (RTE) and capital cost a high-dimensional nonconvex problem. This paper develops a game-theoretic parameter optimization framework for a 60 MW/240 MWh artificial-cavern AA-CAES system operating over a 3-10 MPa sliding-pressure range. A thermodynamic model integrating adiabatic multi-stage compression/expansion, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon$</tex>-NTU heat exchange, and multi-grade thermal-oil/water storage is first formulated. The resulting bi-objective problem is converted into a mixed-integer linear program (MILP) via Special Ordered Sets type 2 (SOS2) piecewise linearization and binary expansion. Objective weighting coefficients are derived from the Nash equilibrium of a two-person zero-sum game, eliminating subjective weight assignment. Case studies show that the proposed framework raises RTE from 58.9 % to 61.1 % (+2.1 pp) and reduces total capital cost by 5.6 % (710 to 670 M C N Y) relative to conventional empirical design. Full-range dynamic simulations across the entire sliding-pressure envelope confirm operational robustness.

title: Coordinated Optimization of the Hydropower and the Wind Power Based on the Second-Order Eigen-Sensitivity to Suppress the LFO
authors: Diwen Tao, Jiangming Jiao, Peng Duan, Xiaoyu Zhang, Peng Yang, Xianke Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: The hydro-wind power complementation plays an important role in the power system. The guide vane opening of the hydropower and the slip of the double-fed induction generator (DFIG) jointly alter the outputs of the units and the power flow, thereby changing the system's stability. The existing optimization of the hydropower or the DFIG ignores the interaction between the two, leading to inaccurate optimized parameters. In this paper, the coordinated optimization of the guide vane opening of the hydropower and the slip of the DFIG, based on the newly derived analytical expression of the second-order sensitivity, is proposed to suppress the low-frequency oscillation (LFO). Firstly, by taking the power flow solution as the intermediate variable, the firstorder sensitivities of the critical eigenvalue with respect to the guide vane opening of the hydropower and the slip of the DFIG are newly derived. Secondly, to reduce the truncation error in the optimization, the second-order sensitivity of the critical eigenvalue is newly derived by considering the interaction between the hydropower and the DFIG. Finally, to improve the damping ratio of the critical eigenvalue, an optimization of the hydropower and the DFIG is proposed based on the sensitivity information and the interior point method. The simulations verify the effectiveness of the proposed optimization to suppress the LFO.

title: Research on Short-Term Power Load Forecasting Method Based on Multi-Dimensional Fusion Model
authors: Zhenye Zhang, Chaobin Lin, Kun Hu, Haodong Liu, Ruopeng Li
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: With the accelerated development of modern power systems, short-term load forecasting has become a critical foundation for ensuring grid safety and optimizing generation planning. Traditional forecasting methods face challenges including limited factor consideration dimensions, insufficient fitting capacity, and difficulties in balancing prediction accuracy and robustness. To address these issues, this study proposes a multi-dimensional integrated optimization method for short-term power load forecasting. The approach comprehensively incorporates meteorological data, date factors, and electricity pricing patterns. It employs empirical mode decomposition to decouple multi-time-scale characteristics from load sequences, combines k-means clustering for dimensionality reduction and component integration, constructs a CNN-Bi-LSTM hybrid neural network to extract deep features and perform time-series fitting, incorporates attention mechanisms to enhance key feature representation, and utilizes an improved grey wolf algorithm for global optimization to achieve precise short-term load forecasting. Validation using operational data from regional power grids demonstrates that this method outperforms conventional algorithms and standard deep learning models in prediction accuracy, scenario adaptability, and computational efficiency.

title: Design and Application of Wind-Photovoltaic-Storage Hybrid Operation System for Clean Energy Bases
authors: Fahong Zhang, Lu Chen, Bingyan Ma, Jianjun Xu, Yongchuan Zhang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: To address the operational challenges of large-scale new energy integration and meet the demands of full-chain optimization for wind-photovoltaic-storage complementary systems, a modular hybrid operation system was designed and developed. The system adopts a three-tier architecture (Presentation Layer-Business Logic Layer-Data Access Layer) based on Spring Boot and MySQL, integrating core functions including base information management, data input, wind-photovoltaic prediction, capacity configuration, and complementary dispatching. Guided by the principles of high cohesion, low coupling, and user-friendliness, a power station-oriented modular construction scheme was implemented to enhance adaptability across multiple scenarios. Engineering application verification at the Changbei New Energy Base shows that the system's capacity configuration results are highly consistent with manual planning schemes, reducing planning design time by over 60% while improving the accuracy of key indicators by 12%–15%. The system provides reliable technical support for the scientific planning and efficient operation of clean energy bases, with significant engineering practical value and promotion prospects.

title: A Post-Quantum Decentralized Threshold Authenticated Key Agreement Scheme for Securing Vehicular Ad-Hoc Networks
authors: Lu Wei, Dan Wu, Jie Cui, Ruonan Ying, Hong Zhong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Authenticated key agreement (AKA) between vehicles and roadside units (RSUs) is a fundamental component for securing Vehicular ad-hoc networks (VANETs). However, most existing AKA schemes rely on conventional cryptographic primitives, such as discrete logarithm and large integer factorization, rendering them vulnerable to emerging quantum attacks. Although several post-quantum solutions based on lattice-hard problems have been proposed, their heavy dependence on a centralized trusted authority exposes them to single-point-of-failure risks. To address these issues, we propose a decentralized authenticated key agreement scheme grounded in the security assumptions of the Ring Learning With Errors (RLWE) and Inhomogeneous Small Integer Solution (ISIS) problems. The proposed scheme achieves strong resistance against quantum adversaries while preserving the privacy of communicating entities. Furthermore, a threshold-based voting mechanism is employed to mitigate the inherent key exposure problem within network infrastructure. By distributing authentication authority across multiple edge server nodes, the scheme significantly enhances system robustness against single-node failures and collusion attacks. Comprehensive security analysis and performance evaluations demonstrate that the proposed scheme satisfies diverse security requirements with acceptable computational and communication overhead, thereby confirming its practicality for deployment in real-world VANETs environments.

title: Fuzzy Game-theoretic Tube Model Predictive Control for Integrated Vehicle Stability System
authors: Guoshun Cai, Chen Sun, Yiming Shu, Shuo Bai, Guodong Yin, Wei He
abstract: This paper develops a fuzzy game-theoretic tube model predictive control (MPC) framework for coordinated vehicle lateral motion control using active front steering (AFS) and direct yaw moment control (DYC). The vehicle dynamics are represented by a discrete-time Takagi-Sugeno fuzzy model to capture operating-condition dependence and parametric uncertainty, while a common-feedback tube MPC structure is employed to guarantee robust constraint satisfaction through an offline-designed invariant tube and terminal set. On this basis, the nominal control problem is formulated as a two-player finite-horizon Nash game, allowing AFS and DYC to optimize individual performance objectives under shared state dynamics and constraints. To enable real-time implementation, two fixedcomplexity online Nash solvers are considered: a best-response (BR) iteration scheme and a variational inequality (VI) formulation solved by an extragradient method. The closed-loop analysis establishes recursive feasibility under bounded disturbances and finite-iteration online equilibrium computation. In addition, a practical input-to-state stability result is derived, in which the effect of inexact online Nash solutions is explicitly captured through a practical-descent framework. Compared with the BR solver, the VI-based solver provides a more direct residual-based interpretation of equilibrium approximation accuracy and its relation to closed-loop stability margins. Hardware-in-the-loop experiments under multiple driving maneuvers verify that the proposed framework is computationally tractable and effective in real time, while achieving robust tracking performance, constraint satisfaction, and coordinated actuator usage.

title: Geometric Correlation-Guided Motion Improvement for Geometric Partitioning Mode in VVC
authors: Jiabao Zhu, Jinhao Kuang, Jiaxin Zeng, Linyun Liu, Hongbo Li, Yucheng Zhong, Licong Ma, Wanglin Lai, Li Yu
abstract: The Geometric Partitioning Mode (GPM) in Versatile Video Coding (VVC) significantly enhances inter prediction efficiency by employing non-rectangular partitions. However, the Motion Vectors (MVs) for these irregular sub-partitions still follow the derivation of rectangular blocks, which often leads to weakly correlated MV candidates and motion mismatch. To address this issue, we propose a unified Geometric Centroid-based Correlation Model (GCCM) to quantify the correlation between GPM sub-partitions and their neighboring regions. By jointly considering geometric centrality and boundary connectivity, GCCM provides an effective metric to guide motion derivation for irregular sub-partitions. Based on GCCM, we redesign the Temporal MV Prediction (TMVP) derivation at the sub-partition level using the most correlated collocated position, and further perform correlation-guided Template Matching (TM) for MV refinement. Experimental results on VTM-12.0 demonstrate that the proposed method achieves 1.94% and 1.14% bit rate savings on Low-delay B and Random Access configurations, respectively.

title: Sub-Nyquist Wideband Spectrum Sensing and DOA Estimation with Sparse Co-Arrays
authors: Xiaoning Liu, Dawei Gao, Xiaoming Zhi, Yaxing Yue, Guisheng Liao
abstract: This letter addresses the joint estimation of frequency and direction-of-arrival for multiple uncorrelated narrowband sources within a wide bandwidth under sub-Nyquist sampling. We propose an architecture employing a sparse co-array where each element is followed by two parallel channels: one delay-free and the other with a fixed, identical delay, requiring no optimization. For this space-time structure, we develop a joint estimation algorithm based on covariance matrix extension, which increases the degrees of freedom, resolves more sources than physical sensors, and automatically pairs the estimated carrier frequencies with their corresponding directions of arrival. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves higher estimation accuracy.

title: Effective and Efficient Temporal Graph Neural Networks via Polynomial Spectral Sparsification
authors: Zhixuan Chen, Xinyu Liu, Longlong Lin, Tao Jia
abstract: Temporal graph neural networks (T-GNNs) have emerged as an effective paradigm for learning dynamic node representations by modeling the temporal evolution of graph structures. Among them, personalized PageRank (PPR)-based T-GNNs have recently gained increasing attention due to their strong empirical performance and theoretical grounding. Nevertheless, existing PPR-based T-GNN methods suffer from two fundamental limitations. First, their aggregation mechanisms are typically restricted to lower order neighborhoods, which hinders the capture of higher order temporal dependences and leads to degraded performance. Second, feature propagation and feature transformation are often tightly coupled, resulting in limited scalability on large temporal graphs. To overcome these challenges, we propose temporal personalized PageRank with spectral sparsification (TPPSS), a novel framework that seamlessly incorporates temporal information into the PPR formulation while decoupling propagation from transformation through a polynomial spectral sparsification. A key technical contribution of TPPSS lies in a unified matrix-based formulation that replaces the conventional rowwise computation of PPR-like scores, enabling efficient and scalable. Moreover, by leveraging spectral sparsification, TPPSS constructs a sparse computational graph that effectively suppresses noise and redundancy. Extensive experiments on six datasets showcase the effectiveness, efficiency, and scalability of our solutions compared to eight competitors.

title: TTT-Dance: A Diffusion Framework Leveraging Test-Time Training and LayerScale for Adaptive Music-Driven Dance Generation_supp1-3695826.pdf
authors: Zixing Zhang
abstract: Music-driven dance generation requires precise motion modeling tightly aligned with musical rhythms. However, existing methods, while producing perceptually convincing motions, often fail to generalize to out-of-distribution music and unseen dance styles. We propose TTT-Dance, a diffusion-based framework that integrates Test-Time Training (TTT) into the denoising network, enabling rapid, sample-specific adaptation during inference to enhance robustness and generalization. Additionally, we incorporate LayerScale modules into the decoder to stabilize the optimization of long temporal sequences and cross-modal interactions, thereby accelerating convergence and improving motion coherence. Extensive experiments on public benchmarks show that TTT-Dance consistently surpasses stateof- the-art baselines across multiple metrics, including physical plausibility, rhythm alignment, and motion diversity, demonstrating its effectiveness in music-dance generation.

title: A Multiple Half-Pitch-Shift Compensation Method for Robust Fiber Weave Effect Mitigation
authors: Zijun Jian, Mu-Shui Zhang, Yuying Li, Zixin Wang, Qianfa Liu
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: As data rates rise, the fiber weave effect (FWE) has become a critical source of signal integrity degradation in high-speed printed circuit boards (PCBs). Presently, there is still no effective and practical method for FWE mitigation. In this letter, a novel half-pitch-shift (HPS) compensation method is proposed for high-performance FWE suppression with low cost. A number of traces are designed, fabricated, and measured for robust verification. The measured results show that the proposed method reduces the average time skew of stripline differential pairs with a length of 260 mm from 19.31 to 1.16 ps, 93.99% reduction. To the author’s best knowledge, this method has overwhelming superiority over other methods in terms of robustness, mitigation, and cost. It is the most practical and applicable solution for low-cost FWE mitigation currently.

title: Transverse Magnetic Coupling Coefficients in Three-Layer Waveguides: Possibilities of Disappearing Gratings
authors: Tso-Min Chou, Nikhil Polley, Weida Zhang, Emmanuel Aloyine, Mina Aghamohammadi, Ify Achu, Jerome K. Butler, Nai-Hsiang Sun, Gary A. Evans
abstract: An equivalent magnetic surface current formulation is used to derive closed-form analytical expressions for the coupling coefficient, stop band width, and group velocity associated with coupling between identical and nonidentical transverse magnetic (TM) modes in asymmetric three-layer optical waveguides incorporating a sinusoidal grating at a waveguide interface. The analytical results are systematically compared with numerically accurate Floquet–Bloch solutions and with a broad set of previously published analytical expressions. The analysis reveals that, for a narrow but practically relevant range of wavelengths, core thicknesses, and large refractive-index contrasts characteristic of silicon photonic waveguides, the TM coupling coefficient exhibits a nonmonotonic dependence on normalized thickness, increasing to a maximum, decreasing to zero, and then increasing again before ultimately decreasing. This counterintuitive behavior arises from cancellation between the transverse and longitudinal electric-field components that contribute to the equivalent magnetic surface current, leading to a “disappearing grating” condition in which the coupling coefficient vanishes despite nonzero modal intensity at the grating interface. This effect has no analog in transverse electric (TE) mode coupling, which exhibits a single maximum for all cases considered. We define this “disappearing grating” condition as the regime in which the coupling coefficient vanishes due to cancellation of field components despite nonzero modal intensity at the grating interface. The results clarify long-standing discrepancies among TM coupling formulas reported in the literature and identify a subset of expressions, derived using different analytical methods, that yield identical results and agree closely with accurate Floquet–Bloch solutions over a range of grating depths and index contrasts. The derived expressions provide physically transparent insight and practical analytical benchmarks for the design and validation of grating-coupled photonic devices, including distributed feedback lasers, distributed Bragg reflector lasers and silicon photonic waveguides.

title: A MARL-Based Beam Hopping Framework for Dynamic Overflying LEO Satellite Networks
authors: Rui Huang, Jiangbo Si, Zan Li, Boyu Deng, Jingchao Wang, Haoqin Zhao, Hang Hu
abstract: In overflying Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite beam hopping (BH), the rapid variation in topology, intermittent visibility, dynamic traffic evolution, and the large joint beam and power action space collectively lead to significant challenges in beam scheduling. Traditional deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based methods require exhaustive exploration of beam positions and power combinations, resulting in excessive training time and high computational complexity, which limits their practical deployment during LEO satellite overflight. Future LEO satellite networks demand high throughput to meet the needs of various applications, but the intelligent beam scheduling schemes for overflying satellites face high training complexity. These challenges become more critical for satellite-assisted IoT services, where massive low-rate terminals require wide-area and efficient access. To address this issue, this paper investigates the BH problem for overflying LEO satellite clusters and proposes a complexity-efficient DRL framework. To mitigate the excessive action space and the difficulty of acquiring channel state information during satellite overflight, a statistical pre-association mechanism is first introduced to eliminate infeasible satellite-cell pairs based on long-term traffic and visibility information. On this structured decision space, a constrained scheduler based on Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization performs coordinated beam selection. Furthermore, fractional programming (FP) based power control is embedded into the learning loop to generate high-reward guidance signals, improving policy learning efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms conventional DRL-based methods in terms of throughput and delay. Compared to traditional DRL-based methods, the average throughput is increased by 5-30%, and the training time is reduced by 50-60%.

title: PD-GAN: A Physics-Guided Dual-Domain Network for Fabric Texture Restoration Under Optoelectronic Saturation
authors: Guan-Sen Bai, Yi-Meng Zhang, Wen-Jian Kuang, Xiang Liu, Bing Tu, Harm Tolner
abstract: High-precision Automated Optical Inspection (AOI) relies heavily on the data fidelity of acquired surface images. However, in the quality control of light-colored or glossy industrial fabrics, the mismatch between high surface reflectance and the limited dynamic range of standard sensors inevitably leads to sensor saturation. This "physical annihilation" of textural information creates measurement blind spots, severely compromising the precision of geometric measurements, such as yarn diameter and thread density. To address this metrological challenge, this paper proposes a Physics-guided Dual-domain Generative Adversarial Network (PD-GAN), designed to recover the topological integrity of textures from overexposed measurements. First, to overcome the scarcity of paired industrial data, a physics-based reverse optical simulation pipeline is established to model the nonlinear mapping between ideal and saturated sensors. Second, a dual-domain restoration network is constructed: a Masked Spatial Reduction Attention (MSRA) mechanism captures global structural consistency, while a Frequency Selection Module (FSM) explicitly enforces the periodicity of yarn topology in the spectral domain. Experimental results on a standardized hardware acquisition system demonstrate that, tailored for the strict topological constraints of industrial textiles, PD-GAN accurately reconstructs the orthogonal grid topology. Consequently, the proposed method ensures high metrological fidelity, reducing the error in downstream yarn density measurement to 0.0%±2.3%. This effectively extends the dynamic range of optical instruments via a software-hardware co-design approach.

title: Fully Automatic Landing Site Selection by a UAV in an Unknown Environment
authors: Salim Iratni, Moussa Diaf
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are widely deployed due to their low cost, autonomy, flexibility, and ability to operate in hard-to-reach areas. However, autonomous landing in unknown environments in the presence of hazards such as water or rough terrain remains challenging. In this paper, we propose a multi-criteria decision-making framework for safe landing site selection based on colored 3D point clouds, from which parameters such as local slope, local roughness, distance from the UAV, and proximity to hazards are extracted. These parameters are combined using a fuzzy inference system to calculate an overall safety index, the highest value of which indicates the safest landing location. To estimate slope and roughness, we also propose a method based on anisotropic diffusion filtering for decorrelating these parameters while preserving topographic discontinuities. Validation of this method on 50 synthetic DEMs yielded high accuracy, with a slope RMSE of 1.45° and a PCC of 99.76%, and a roughness RMSE of 0.08m and a PCC of 58.66%, thereby outperforming conventional methods. We also enhanced the performance of semantic segmentation for water body detection using superpixelization. An accuracy of 97.99% and an F1 Score of 79.14% are achieved, thereby ensuring reliable identification of this hazard. The overall framework was tested on a semi synthetic scene featuring a water surface and two real 3D scenes obtained from a UAV and containing varied terrain, including sand dunes, gravel, vegetation, and rocky slopes. The safest landing site was consistently selected for all UAV positions and altitudes, with total processing times ranging from 8.1s to 281s, demonstrating near real-time feasibility.

title: Evaluating Large Language Models for Generating YouTube Programming Tutorial Metadata from Video Transcripts
authors: Mohammad D. Alahmadi
abstract: YouTube programming tutorials are widely used by developers, yet their metadata (titles and descriptions) is often generic, incomplete, or dominated by promotional content. In this paper, we investigate the ability of three large language models, Claude Opus 4, GPT-5.2, and DeepSeek, to generate descriptive metadata for YouTube programming tutorials using only video transcripts as input.We construct a balanced dataset of 60 videos from 49 unique YouTube channels, spanning five programming languages (Python, Java, JavaScript, C#, and C++) and three duration categories (short, medium, and long). The generated metadata is evaluated using three lexical and two semantic metrics (ROUGE-1, ROUGE-2, ROUGE-L, BERTScore, and SBERT cosine similarity) and a blind human evaluation study with ten evaluators. Our results show that Claude Opus 4 achieves the best overall performance, leading in 7 out of 10 lexical and semantic metrics, including all three ROUGE variants and SBERT cosine similarity for descriptions. All three models produce titles with high semantic similarity to the originals (BERTScore > 0.89), and descriptions with BERTScore above 0.80, while description scores are lower due to the stylistic mismatch between LLM-generated summaries and the promotional nature of original YouTube descriptions. The human evaluation confirms these findings: all three LLMs received higher ratings than the original metadata across all criteria, with Claude Opus 4 scoring above 4.0 on a 5-point scale for title relevance and description criteria. We further analyze the effects of programming language and video duration on metadata quality, finding that no single model dominates across all languages for titles, while medium-length videos yield the best results. Our results show that LLMs can generate metadata that is more relevant, clearer, and more informative than what is typically provided by video creators.

title: DDialogue: A Collaborative Framework for Cross-Sectoral Dialogue through Data
authors: Alessandro Fornaroli, Ravinithesh Annapureddy, Daniel Gatica-Perez
publish: Proceedings of the 19th Participatory Design Conference 2026, Vol. 1: Full Papers
abstract: None

title: A PINN-Based Modeling and State Estimation Method for Doubly-Fed Wind Turbines
authors: Shaojian Song, Yang Gao
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: To tackle the problems of low efficiency and poor parameter identifiability in conventional mechanism models of doubly-fed induction generator (DFIG) wind turbines, and the absence of physical constraints and weak generalization in purely data-driven approaches, a state estimation method based on physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) is proposed. The dataset is obtained from a PSCAD/EMTDC simulation of a 2 MW grid-connected DFIG system, and it covers startup, steady operation, wind-speed disturbance, and fault ride-through stages over 65 s. A multilayer perceptron (MLP) is used as the backbone network, and electrical power balance, reactive power balance, and electromechanical torque balance are embedded into the loss function as physical constraints. Based on this model, measurable variables are mapped to generator active power, reactive power, and electromagnetic torque, so that the state estimation results can be used to verify model accuracy under unseen operating conditions. Comparative experiments against linear regression (LR), conventional MLP, temporal convolutional network (TCN), Transformer, extended Kalman filter (EKF), unscented Kalman filter (UKF), and particle filter (PF) show that our method achieves the best overall accuracy. The average root mean square error (RMSE) is reduced to 0.0031, the average mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) is 0.026%. These results indicate that the proposed method is suitable for accurate and real-time monitoring of DFIG wind turbines.

title: Adaptive Voltage Control Strategy for Distributed Energy Storage Based on SOC and Voltage Deviation
authors: Xuemei Luo, Yu Liu, Guofeng Zhuang, Jie Ma, Jie Wang, Xinzheng Ma
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of distributed photovoltaic (DPV) systems, voltage fluctuations in distribution networks are becoming increasingly prominent. Distributed energy storage systems (DESS), as flexible regulation resources, play a vital role in voltage stability control. This paper proposes an adaptive voltage control strategy for distributed energy storage based on SOC and voltage deviation. First, a control function is constructed based on the deviation between the node voltage and the rated voltage. The voltage deviation is shaped by introducing deadband, hysteresis, and nonlinear amplification. This approach can suppress significant voltage overshoots and reduce the frequency of energy storage operation. Based on this, a distribution network model incorporating DPV and DESS is established and simulated in OpenDSS. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm can effectively alleviate voltage deviation and overshoot issues in the distribution network, improving the voltage compliance rate.

title: Flexible Power Sharing in Multi-Three-Phase PMSM via Virtual Current Regulation With Vector Space Decomposition
authors: Zekai Lyu, Aten Man Ho Chau, Haolan Zhan, Litao Dai, Shuangxia Niu
abstract: Power sharing among winding sets in multi-three-phase machines plays a crucial role in high-power applications such as electric propulsion and offshore wind energy systems. This paper presents a flexible power-sharing control strategy from the perspective of vector space decomposition, in which a virtual stator current regulation method is developed to enable independent control of each winding set. The key idea is to introduce adjustable gains associated with the desired power-sharing coefficients to reconstruct the phase currents. The proposed method does not require additional complex computations or extra coordinate transformations. Moreover, the dynamic performance of the torque subspace is also optimized. Experimental results obtained from a prototype dual three-phase permanent-magnet machine validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Improving the Reliability of Port Equipment Maintenance Decision Support Via Context-Aware Knowledge Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Zhaojie Fu, Haizhou Chen, Haojie Li, Hualin Yang, Tao Bi, Weili Zhao, Dong Zhao
abstract: Reliable acquisition and organization of maintenance knowledge are essential for ensuring effective decision support in complex industrial operations and maintenance (O&M) environments. However, existing retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) approaches typically rely on fixed retrieval strategies and single-metric relevance ranking, which limits their ability to adapt to heterogeneous task contexts and balance accuracy with knowledge completeness, thereby reducing the reliability of AI-assisted maintenance decision-making. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a context-aware knowledge graph retrieval-augmented generation framework (ContextKG-RAG) for improving the reliability of port equipment maintenance decision support. The proposed framework introduces a context-aware retrieval mechanism that infers query task contexts via topic modelling and adaptively adjusts knowledge graph traversal depth and expansion scope using a dynamic programming strategy. In addition, a multi-objective knowledge organization method is developed to construct a core reasoning skeleton while selecting supplementary knowledge nodes to jointly optimize accuracy, diversity, and contextual relevance of retrieved information. Extensive experiments on both the public HotpotQA dataset and a real-world port equipment O&M dataset demonstrate that the proposed approach consistently outperforms state-of-the-art RAG baselines in terms of retrieval quality and response quality. Furthermore, robustness evaluations under degraded knowledge graph conditions verify that the framework maintains stable performance in incomplete knowledge environments. These results demonstrate that the proposed framework provides a reliable and context-adaptive knowledge acquisition mechanism for supporting trustworthy AI-assisted maintenance decision-making in complex industrial environments.

title: A Thyristor-Switched Pulsed Power System With 78-ns Jitter for a Coaxial Plasma Gun_supp1-3692821.pdf
authors: Neil P. Laya
abstract: A fiber-optically controlled and thyristor-switched coaxial plasma gun (CPG) pulsed power system has been designed, built, and fired at up to 2 kV and 6.4 kA, with a 6.65-MW momentary peak power output from the capacitor. Total system firing jitter from initial digital signal to 90% peak current rise was measured at 78.15 ns, representing 0.18% of the total pulse time of 44.10 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s. This includes both thyristor turn-on and CPG breakdown. Standard deviation of peak current over repeated firings was measured to be 21.09 A (0.33%). The system has experienced 100+ firings at 2 kV without a single misfire. A voltage profiling analysis has been performed where voltage differentials across system components, including the thyristor, were collected temporally for the entire pulse. High-speed and long-exposure photography were used to verify CPG operation and mass-injection/gas-puff system timing. This study sets the groundwork for plasma-load pulsed power systems using thyristors, as well as informing expanded voltage and current capability, enabling fine timing control and multisystem synchronization, allowing greater repeatability and reliability, and informing multithyristor array systems.

title: Cross-Modality Self-Distillation for Visible-Infrared Person Re-identification
authors: Zihao Hu, Yongguo Ling, Zhiming Luo, Wenhao Shao, Shaozi Li, Zhun Zhong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Visible-Infrared Person Re-identification (VI-ReID) faces large challenges due to the cross-modality gap and intra-class variations. Existing methods mainly focus on mitigating the cross-modality discrepancy by optimizing the embedding space of high-level features. However, two critical issues are often overlooked: low-level features suffer from larger modality gaps, and redundant channels exhibit limited identity discrimination. These limitations hinder the overall discriminative performance of the model. To address the above issues, we propose a novel Cross-Modality Self-Distillation (CMSD) network, which consists of two key components: Cross-layer Semantic Distillation (CSD) and Intra-layer Alignment Distillation (IAD). These two modules are mutually beneficial to jointly explore cross-layer modality discrepancy and intra-layer channel discrepancy. Specifically, CSD distills modality-shared semantics from high-level features to guide low-level features in learning more reliable modality-invariant representations. Furthermore, IAD decomposes feature maps into salient and redundant channels while transferring identity-related information from salient to redundant channels, thereby enhancing overall discrimination of modality-invariant features. Our extensive experiments on the SYSU-MM01, RegDB, and LLCM datasets demonstrate that the components of our proposed CMSD mutually enhance each other, leading to superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: Development and Characterization of KID-Based SPIAKID Instrument
authors: Zhaohang Peng, Faouzi Boussaha, Piercarlo Bonifacio, Jie Hu, Paul Nicaise, Christine Chaumont, Florent Reix, Josiane Firminy, Elisabetta Caffau, Cyril Pannetier, Jean-Philippe Amans, Moustapha Dekkali, Thibaut Vacelet, Alexine Marret, David Horville, Michel Piat
abstract: The SpectroPhotometric Imaging in Astronomy with Kinetic Inductance Detectors (SPIAKID) instrument is designed and currently being developed to explore ultra-faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies in the optical and near-infrared bands. We envisage to install SPIAKID at the New Technology Telescope (NTT) in Chile as a guest instrument and targets a field of view of 2' × 2', which can be achieved using four 20,000-pixel arrays at the focal plane. Each array is divided into 10 subarrays, with 2,000 pixels per feedline. The superconducting kinetic inductance detector (KID) comprises a TiN/Ti/TiN trilayer meander and an Nb interdigitated capacitor. In a newly developed anti-vibration dilution cryostat, 10 readout lines were constructed utilizing 10 low-noise amplifiers (LNAs). The performance of the device is characterized using a vector network analyzer (VNA) and a homodyne scheme at various temperatures between 20 and 495 mK, producing preliminary results of pulse response and energy resolution (energy resolving power) under illumination delivered through an optical fiber at 405 nm. The measured resonant frequencies of the KIDs range from 3.8 to 8.5 GHz. The energy resolution of the resonator initially improves with increasing temperature but subsequently degrades. So far, the best measured energy resolution is 2.5 at 275 mK and 405 nm.

title: Robust RIS Selection for mmWave Communication Systems with Predictive Beamforming Tracking
authors: Yuyin Ma, Yunkai Yang, Yijun Lu, Zhen Tian, Kaoru Ota, Mianxiong Dong
abstract: In dynamic millimeter-wave systems aided by multiple reconfigurable intelligent surfaces, sustaining beam alignment over all candidate links can impose prohibitive signaling and computational overhead. This letter develops a low-complexity framework that decouples prediction-driven RIS selection from fine-grained beam tracking. Specifically, we propose a Hybrid Tracking–Selection Indicator (Hybrid-TSI), which jointly captures the predicted signal-to-noise ratio and the associated estimation uncertainty. By adaptively penalizing links that are highly susceptible to angular tracking errors, Hybrid-TSI enables reliable RIS selection in time-varying channels. After selection, an extended Kalman filter is executed exclusively for the active RIS to refine the UE angle state, while the remaining RIS links are updated through prediction. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves a better trade-off between performance and complexity compared to the traditional full-tracking scheme.

title: Rotatable Antenna-Enhanced Cell-Free Communication
authors: Kecheng Pan, Beixiong Zheng, Yanhua Tan, Fangjiong Chen, Emil Björnson, Robert Schober, Rui Zhang
abstract: Rotatable antenna (RA) is a promising technology that can exploit new design dimensions by optimizing the three-dimensional (3D) boresight directions of directional antennas. In this letter, we investigate an RA-enhanced cell-free system for downlink transmission, where multiple RA-equipped access points (APs) cooperatively serve multiple single-antenna users over the same time-frequency resource. Specifically, we aim to maximize the sum rate of all users by jointly optimizing the AP-user associations and the RA boresight directions. Accordingly, we propose a two-stage strategy to solve the AP-user association problem, and then employ fractional programming (FP) and successive convex approximation (SCA) techniques to optimize the RA boresight directions. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed RA-enhanced cell-free system significantly outperforms various benchmark schemes.

title: Blank Page
authors: N/A

title: Burhan: A Constraint-Based Engineering Framework for Computational Rhetorical Semantics in Classical Arabic
authors: Reema G. Al-Anazi, Wadee A. Nashir, Badriyya B. Al-Onazi, Anoud A. Alhamad, Hassan Alshamrani, Wala F. Bin Subait
abstract: Figurative language poses persistent challenges for Natural Language Processing (NLP), as distributional representations struggle to model non-compositional semantic mappings. This limitation is particularly pronounced in Classical Arabic, where available computational resources emphasize morphosyntactic annotation while underrepresenting formalized rhetorical structure. This paper presents Burhan, a computational semantic data engineering framework and curated dataset designed to formalize figurative meaning for machine processing. The resource comprises 1,367 manually annotated records (402 similes, 965 metaphors) extracted from the Quranic corpus, yielding 1,402 rhetorical instances due to nested or multi-figurative constructions within single verses. Its core contribution lies in a constraint-aware JSON schema that explicitly encodes structural components, Source–Target domain mappings, pragmatic functions, and cognitive processing effort, enabling automated validation and downstream integration. We describe a four-stage human-in-the-loop annotation pipeline—from structural identification to schema normalization—achieving substantial inter-annotator agreement (Fleiss’ κ = 0.87). Empirical downstream deployments further demonstrate the learnability and structural robustness of the schema, achieving up to Macro-F1=0.70 in multi-task rhetorical classification and Micro-F1=0.86 in multi-label parsing without schema refactoring. By providing a formally structured, closed-domain gold standard, Burhan supports reproducible experimentation in metaphor detection, neurosymbolic modeling, and semantic knowledge graph construction, establishing a reusable semantic infrastructure for explainable and ontology-driven approaches in Arabic NLP.

title: An Advanced Persistent Threat Detection Framework Based on Graph Attention Networks with Spectral Feature Refinement
authors: Chun-I Fan, Ji-Shan Wu, Cheng-Han Shie, Hsin-Nan Kuo, Bo-Yi Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Recent studies have proposed APT detection methods based on provenance graphs. However, existing systems face challenges due to large-scale graphs, leading to high memory consumption and limited efficiency. Many datasets provide only preprocessed data without disclosing log collection and attribute mapping, hindering reproducibility. Some approaches rely on commercial EDR or high-quality threat intelligence, limiting real world adoption. To address these limitations, this study proposes a Graph Attention Network (GAT)-based APT detection framework with spectral feature refinement. The framework uses Sysmon logs and integrates MITRE ATT&CK technique matching with open source Sysmon filtering rules to construct Rule-Filtered Provenance Graphs (RFPGs), reducing graph size while preserving security-relevant semantics. Textual and numerical attributes are encoded into unified node and edge representations to model heterogeneous system entities and interactions. A spectrally refined GAT model is then employed for malicious node detection, with an Interval Bias mechanism to adjust detection sensitivity under a controlled precision trade-off. Experimental results show that the proposed system achieves 98.25% accuracy, 90.58% precision, and a 0.36% false positive rate. Compared with a baseline model, it reduces inference time by up to 10.8% and GPU memory consumption by 11.4%. Model inference takes 0.82 seconds, while the complete pipeline requires approximately 5 seconds.

title: Equitable and Inclusive PD for Public Services: Using Shared Reflexivity to Unpack Enablers and Limitations
authors: Uttishta Sreerama Varanasi, Rūta Šerpytytė, Joanna Saad-Sulonen
publish: Proceedings of the 19th Participatory Design Conference 2026, Vol. 1: Full Papers
abstract: None

title: Robust Scheduling Method for a Multi-Energy Microgrid with Hydrogen–Ammonia Coupling
authors: Pengwei Zhao, Ruijin Zhu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Against the background of the dual-carbon targets and new power system development, hydrogen-ammonia integrated multi-energy microgrids have attracted increasing attention because of their advantages in flexible storage, long-duration regulation, and low-carbon energy substitution. However, renewable generation fluctuations, load variations, and electricity price uncertainty may significantly undermine system economy and operational security. To address these issues, this paper proposes a robust scheduling method for a hydrogen-ammonia coupled multi-energy microgrid cluster. A coordinated electricity-heat-hydrogen-ammonia operation model is established, and a robust optimization framework is introduced to capture the uncertainties of electricity prices and renewable output. With the objective of minimizing total operating cost, the optimal scheduling strategy is derived. The results demonstrate that the proposed method can improve renewable energy utilization, reduce dependence on external electricity purchases, and enhance system adaptability under uncertainty while ensuring reliable energy supply.
